overview: Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is a flagship initiative by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India, launched on 19th April 2021. It aims to provide financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization. The scheme operates through a network of eligible incubators across India and supports startups in graduating to a level where they can raise investments from angel investors, venture capitalists, or seek loans from commercial banks. The scheme is sector-agnostic and provides both grant and debt/convertible debenture funding to eligible startups.
eligibility: A startup, recognized by DPIIT, incorporated not more than 2 years ago at the time of application. The startup must have a business idea to develop a product or a service with market fit, viable commercialization, and scope of scaling. The startup should be using technology in its core product or service, or business model, or distribution model, or methodology to solve the problem being targeted. Preference would be given to startups creating innovative solutions in sectors such as social impact, waste management, water management, financial inclusion, education, agriculture, food processing, biotechnology, healthcare, energy, mobility, defence, space, railways, oil and gas, textiles, etc. Startup should not have received more than Rs 10 lakh of monetary support under any other Central or State Government scheme. This does not include prize money from competitions and grand challenges, subsidized working space, founder monthly allowance, access to labs, or access to prototyping facility. Shareholding by Indian promoters in the startup should be at least 51% at the time of application to incubator for the scheme, as per Companies Act, 2013 and SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018. A startup applicant can avail seed support in the form of grant and debt/convertible debentures each once as per the guidelines of the scheme.
benefits: Financial assistance in the form of grant up to Rs. 20 Lakhs for validation of Proof of Concept, prototype development, or product trials, and up to Rs. 50 Lakhs for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments. The grant is disbursed in milestone-based installments. Debt/convertible debentures are provided at a rate of interest not more than prevailing repo rate, with tenure fixed at time of sanctioning (not more than 60 months), and a moratorium of up to 12 months may be provided. Startups are not charged any fees by the incubator. Incubators receive a management fee of 5% of the Seed Fund grant. Selected startups receive physical infrastructure, support for testing and validating ideas, mentoring for prototype or product development or commercialization, and development of capacities in finance, human resources, legal compliances, and other functions. They also gain networking with investors and opportunities for showcasing in national and international events. The seed fund is disbursed to the startup's company bank account. Progress reporting is required at least once every 15 days via videoconference or physical meeting, and updates must be shared on the scheme dashboard at least monthly. Interim progress reports and utilization certificates are required to initiate subsequent installments of grant. Final report and audited utilization certificate are required at project completion.
financial_support: Seed Fund to an eligible startup by the incubator shall be disbursed as follows: Up to Rs. 20 Lakhs as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials. The grant shall be disbursed in milestone-based installments. These milestones can be related to development of prototype, product testing, building a product ready for market launch, etc. Up to Rs. 50 Lakhs of investment for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments. A startup applicant can avail seed support in the form of grant and debt/convertible debentures each once as per the guidelines of the scheme. For grants, the first installment to any selected startup shall be released not more than 60 days from receipt of application from the startup. For debt or convertible debentures, a similar timeline will be aimed but it is understood that due diligence and documentation in these cases can be sometimes time-consuming. Startups will receive the funds in their company bank accounts.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to Rs. 20 Lakhs as grant for validation of Proof of Concept, or prototype development, or product trials. Up to Rs. 50 Lakhs of investment for market entry, commercialization, or scaling up through convertible debentures or debt or debt-linked instruments
application_process: 1. Ensure you are a DPIIT-recognized startup (if not, apply for recognition via https://www.startupindia.gov.in/content/sih/en/startupgov/startup_recognition_page.html). 2. Navigate to the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme portal (https://seedfund.startupindia.gov.in/) and click on Login. 3. Enter your Startup India login credentials (username and password). 4. Click on 'Apply Now'. 5. Fill or upload necessary details as per the fields in the application form (including entity details, team profile, problem statement, product/service overview, business model, customer profile, market size, quantum of funds needed, projected utilization plan for funds, etc.). 6. Recheck the data in the Entity Details page and click on Save and Next. 7. Select incubator preferences (up to 3 incubators in order of preference) and click on Save and Next. 8. Select the funding instrument (Grant/Debt/Debenture) under funding details to view the incubator preference list. 9. Check the Self Declaration box and click on Submit. 10. Your application is submitted successfully. 11. Track the progress of your application on the Startup India portal dashboard using your login credentials. 12. If rejected, you may apply afresh after 6 weeks.
deadlines: Last date for submission of startup applications under the Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) is 31 May 2026. Incubators to complete 100% startup selection by 30 June 2026.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India
contact_details: Email: dipp-startups@nic.in | For queries related to incubator portfolio: sisfs@investindia.org.in | Grievance form: https://seedfund.startupindia.gov.in/contact

overview: TIDE 2.0 (Technology Incubation and Development of Entrepreneurs 2.0) is a Government of India scheme under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) aimed at promoting tech entrepreneurship through financial and technical support to incubators engaged in supporting ICT startups using emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, Block-chain, Robotics etc. in seven pre-identified areas of societal relevance. The scheme will be implemented through 51 incubators and handholding of approximately 2000 tech start-ups over a period of five years. It supports tech startups addressing societal challenges in seven selected thematic areas identified based on national priorities in the realms of Healthcare, Education, Agriculture, Financial inclusion including digital payments, Infrastructure and transportation, Environment and clean tech, and Clean energy solutions.
eligibility: Proposals from 51 TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centres are to be obtained from institutions of higher learning and R&D organisations having incubation facilities and established linkages with large companies, industry bodies, industry clusters so as to provide necessary mentorship and possible infusion of innovative businesses into their larger business operations.
benefits: Financial and technical support to incubators; support to startups at various stages viz. Entrepreneurs-in-residence (EiR): for idea to Proof of Concept upto Rs. 4 Lakh, Grants: for prototype development upto Rs. 7 Lakh and Investment: for product development and market outreach upto Rs. 40 Lakh; handholding of approximately 2000 tech startups over a period of 5 years; development and management of a comprehensive online platform and ecosystem for promoting virtual incubation across the country; creation of MeitY Startup Hub with a holistic ambition to coordinate the ICT startup-innovation ecosystem in the country; protection of IPR generated through Centre of Excellence in IPR (CoE-IP).
financial_support: The scheme provides grant-in-aid from MeitY with a budgetary outlay of Rs. 264.62 Crores to be released over a period of five years. Funding is 100% grants-in-aid from MeitY with no loan component. Support to TIDE Incubation Centres includes provisioning of EiRs, Grants and Investments. Stages for release of payment are based on submission of Utilization Certificate, Technical & Financial Progress Report, Audited statement of accounts, and Recommendations of the PRSG.
grant_amount_per_entity: Entrepreneurs-in-residence (EiR): for idea to Proof of Concept upto Rs. 4 Lakh, Grants: for prototype development upto Rs. 7 Lakh and Investment: for product development and market outreach upto Rs. 40 Lakh.
application_process: 1. Proposals from 51 TIDE 2.0 Incubation Centres are to be obtained from institutions of higher learning and R&D organisations having incubation facilities and established linkages with large companies, industry bodies, industry clusters. 2. MoU to be executed by Implementing Agencies/Partnering Institutes. 3. Submission of Surety Bond by Implementing Agencies. 4. Submission of Utilization Certificate of Previous release. 5. Technical & Financial Progress Report. 6. Audited statement of accounts. 7. Recommendations of the PRSG.
deadlines: The scheme was implemented from 01 February, 2019 to 31st January, 2024 (60 months duration).
implementing_agency: Innovation and IPR Division, MeitY in association with 51 incubation centres from institutes of higher learning and R&D organisations

overview: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 is a government initiative approved on April 13, 2026, with a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore to mobilize venture capital for the startup ecosystem. It builds upon the original Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) launched in 2016, aiming to provide access to venture capital across startup stages and sectors, with a special focus on innovation-driven manufacturing, deep tech, and early growth stage startups. The scheme operates through SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that invest in equity and equity-linked instruments of DPIIT-recognized startups.
eligibility: The eligibility criteria for AIFs and startups are covered in the operational guidelines issued by DPIIT, which include segment-wise provisions, selection and monitoring processes, and reporting and assessment requirements. AIFs must be SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds, and startups must be recognised as 'startups' by the Central Government under DPIIT criteria.
benefits: The scheme provides access to venture capital for startups through SEBI-registered AIFs investing in equity and equity-linked instruments. It supports capital availability for deep tech, manufacturing, early growth stage, and sector/stage agnostic startups. Returns from the scheme (net of up to 5% utilisation for ecosystem capacity building) are deposited back to the Consolidated Fund of India. The scheme also enables mentorship and nurturing of investee startups by supported AIFs before exit/sale of investments.
financial_support: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 operates as a Fund of Funds, contributing to the corpus of SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn invest in equity and equity-linked instruments of DPIIT-recognized startups. The scheme does not provide direct funding to startups but mobilizes venture capital through AIFs. Funds are disbursed to AIFs in tranches based on commitments over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles, with AIFs raising additional funds from other investors to meet target corpus amounts.
application_process: The application process involves AIFs submitting proposals to Implementation Agencies (IAs), which conduct due diligence. A Venture Capital Investment Committee (VCIC) constituted by DPIIT evaluates the proposals for recommendation. VCIC includes representation from industry, subject matter experts, and IAs, and considers AIFs managed by experienced professionals with proven track records for funding under the Scheme. Post-investment, supported AIFs mentor and nurture their investee startups before exit/sale of investments.
deadlines: Commitments to AIFs spread over the 16th and 17th Finance Commission Cycles
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), with Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI) as the Implementation Agency (IA) for FFS 1.0 and also for Startup India FoF 2.0, along with other domestic IA(s) selected to implement the proposed Scheme

overview: The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) is aimed at providing credit guarantee up to a specified limit against loans extended by Member Institutions (MIs) to finance eligible borrowers viz. Startups as defined in the Gazette Notification issued by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) and amended from time to time. The Government of India established the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups with a fixed corpus for providing credit guarantees to loans extended to DPIIT recognised startups by Scheduled Commercial Banks, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) and Venture Debt Funds (VDFs) under SEBI registered Alternative Investment Funds. The revised framework has enhanced guarantee coverage, increasing the maximum limit from ₹10 crore to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower. CGSS does not provide guarantee cover to DPIIT recognised startups directly, but through a Trustee (NCGTC), which in turn provides guarantee cover to MIs who provide loans to startups.
eligibility: Borrower: The eligibility criteria for an entity to borrow under the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups shall be as follows, wherein an entity should be: Startup as recognized by DPIIT as per Gazette Notifications issued from time to time, and Startup not in default to any lending/investing institution and not classified as Non-Performing Asset as per RBI guidelines, and Startup whose eligibility is certified by the Member Institution for the purpose of guarantee cover. Lending/Investing Institutions: The eligibility criteria for the lending/investing institutions under the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups shall be as follows: Scheduled Commercial Banks and Financial Institutions, RBI registered Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) having a rating of BBB and above as rated by external credit rating agencies accredited by RBI and having minimum networth of Rs. 100 crore. However, it may be noted that in case an NBFC subsequently becomes ineligible, due to a downgrade in the credit rating below BBB, the NBFC shall not be eligible for further guarantee cover till upgradation again to eligible category. SEBI registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs).
benefits: The instruments of assistance would be in the form of Venture debt, working capital, subordinated debt/mezzanine debt, debentures, optionally convertible debt and other fund-based as well as non-fund-based facilities, which have crystallised as debt obligations. Credit guarantee coverage under this model would be either transaction-based or umbrella-based. For transaction-based guarantee cover: The Trust shall provide guarantee cover, subject to a maximum of Rs. 20 crore per borrower, as per the details given below: 85% of the amount in default for loan amount up to Rs. 10 crore, 75% of the amount in default for loan amount exceeding Rs. 10 crore. For umbrella-based guarantee cover: The Trust shall provide guarantee cover of actual losses or up to a maximum of 5% of Pooled Investment on which cover is being taken from the fund in Startups, whichever is lower, subject to a maximum of Rs. 20 crore per borrower. Losses are defined as aggregate of principal investments of written off assets along with three months accrued interest from the date of default. In case of partially written off assets, only the principal portion written off along with three months accrued interest thereon from the date of default will be accounted for the loss assets. The umbrella-based guarantee cover will run through the life of the venture debt fund.
financial_support: The maximum amount of debt (fund-based or non-fund-based facilities) eligible for guarantee cover under the Scheme has been revised to ₹20 crore per borrower. The Government of India established the Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups with a fixed corpus for providing credit guarantees to loans extended to DPIIT recognised startups by Scheduled Commercial Banks, Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) and Venture Debt Funds (VDFs) under SEBI registered Alternative Investment Funds. The revised framework has enhanced guarantee coverage, increasing the maximum limit from ₹10 crore to ₹20 crore per eligible borrower.
grant_amount_per_entity: ₹20 crore per borrower
application_process: 1. Online Application: Visit the Jan Samarth Portal using the link below and complete the online application: Apply on Jan Samarth Portal ( List of Member Institutions available on the Jan Samarth Portal ). 2. For step-by-step guidance, please refer to the Jan Samarth Portal instructional video: Watch Instructional Video. 3. Apply through Member Institutions: Approach the nearest branch of any Member Institution (MI) directly and enquire about the required process. The list of eligible Member Lending Institutions (MLIs) is available at the link below: View Eligible MLIs. 4. Offline Application: Approach the nearest branch of any Member Institution (MI) directly and enquire about the required process. 5. Registration Process: All eligible institutions may register itself under the said scheme by submitting a signed undertaking (format given on website) and Board Resolution. Upon successful registration of the Member Institution (MI), login credentials of the MI shall be created whereafter it can apply for guarantee cover on NCGTC’s portal. To know more and register as an MI, visit the NCGTC 's portal.
implementing_agency: National Credit Guarantee Trust Company (NCGTC)

overview: Startup India Initiative is a flagship Government of India program launched in 2016 by DPIIT to foster innovation, support entrepreneurs, and drive sustainable economic growth. It provides DPIIT recognition to eligible startups, enabling access to tax benefits, easier compliance, IPR fast-tracking, funding schemes, and other incentives. The initiative aims to build a robust startup ecosystem across India through simplification, funding support, and industry-academia partnerships.
target_beneficiaries: startups; entrepreneurs; MSME; women entrepreneurs; deeptech startups; agri-startups
eligibility: An entity is eligible for DPIIT recognition if it is incorporated or registered in India as a private limited company (as defined in the Companies Act, 2013) or registered as a partnership firm (registered under section 59 of the Partnership Act, 1932) or a limited liability partnership (under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008) or a Multi-State Cooperative Society registered with the Central Registrar of Cooperative Societies (under the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act, 2002) or a Cooperative Society registered under any State or Union Territory Cooperative Societies Act with the respective Registrar of Cooperative Societies in India; is within a period of ten years from the date of its incorporation or registration (up to twenty years for DeepTech startups); has a turnover for any of the financial years since incorporation or registration not exceeding two hundred crore rupees (three hundred crore rupees for DeepTech startups); and is working towards innovation, development or improvement of products or processes or services, or is a scalable business model with a high potential of employment generation or wealth creation. Provided that, an entity formed by splitting up or reconstruction of an existing business shall not be considered a Startup.
benefits: DPIIT recognised startups can access self-certification compliance under 9 labour and environment laws (no inspections for 3 years for labour laws, random checks for white category environment laws), fast-tracking of patent applications with 80% rebate on filing fees, government bearing facilitator costs for IP applications, income tax exemption for 3 consecutive financial years out of first ten years under Section 80-IAC, exemption from prior experience/turnover in public procurement, exemption from Earnest Money Deposit (EMD) in government tenders, faster winding up within 90 days under Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, access to Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS), Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS), tax exemption on investments above fair market value, and access to various government schemes and initiatives including Seed Fund Scheme, MAARG mentorship platform, Startup India Investor Connect, BHASKAR knowledge registry, and State/UT incentives.
financial_support: Funding support is provided through the Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) with a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore, managed by SIDBI, which invests in SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn invest in startups. The Credit Guarantee Scheme for Startups (CGSS) provides credit guarantee up to ₹20 crore per borrower for loans extended by Member Institutions to DPIIT recognised startups. The Startup India Seed Fund Scheme (SISFS) provides financial assistance to early-stage startups for proof of concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialisation.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 20 Lakhs from SISFS for early-stage validation
application_process: 1. Register on the Startup India portal (www.startupindia.gov.in) to get a Bhaskar ID. 2. Log in to the Startup India portal and navigate to the 'Get Recognised' section. 3. Fill out the DPIIT recognition application form with details about the startup's uniqueness and innovation. 4. Upload required documents including Certificate of Incorporation/Registration, PAN, and business description/pitch deck. 5. Submit the application for review. 6. Alternatively, apply via National Single Window System (NSWS) at www.nsws.gov.in by logging in, adding 'Registration as a Startup' to approvals, filling the form, and submitting. 7. Track application status on NSWS or Startup India dashboard. 8. Upon approval, download the DPIIT Recognition Certificate from NSWS, Startup India portal, or DigiLocker.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry
contact_details: Email: startupindia@dipp.gov.in | Helpline: 1800-11-5565 | EPABX: 011-23061222, Fax: 011-23062626 (Udyog Bhawan, New Delhi 110011)

overview: Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) is a Government of India initiative launched under the Startup India Action Plan in 2016, managed by SIDBI, with a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore. It does not invest directly in startups but contributes to SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) that in turn invest in equity and equity-linked instruments of DPIIT-recognised startups. The scheme aims to catalyse venture capital availability, support innovation-driven manufacturing, nurture early-stage startups, and promote ecosystem development by earmarking up to 5% of returns for capacity building activities.
eligibility: SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) are eligible to receive contributions from the Fund of Funds for Startups. AIFs must raise the balance 50% or more of the stated fund size from other investors to be eligible for contribution. The Scheme supports AIFs across four segments: Segment 1 (Deep Tech), Segment 2 (Micro VCs), Segment 3 (Tech-driven innovative manufacturing), and Segment 4 (Sector/stage agnostic). Startups must be recognised by DPIIT as per Gazette Notifications to be eligible for investment by AIFs supported under the Scheme.
benefits: Indirect financial support to startups through SEBI-registered AIFs that invest in equity and equity-linked instruments. The Scheme enables access to venture capital, supports innovation-driven manufacturing, nurtures early-stage startups, and promotes ecosystem development. Returns (capital redeemed and returns earned) net of up to 5% utilisation for capacity building are deposited back to the Consolidated Fund of India (CFI).
financial_support: The Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) has a total corpus of INR 10,000 crore. It does not invest directly in startups but contributes to the corpus of SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) for investing in equity and equity-linked instruments of DPIIT-recognised startups. The contribution is made in tranches after AIFs raise the balance 50% or more of the stated fund size from other investors. The Scheme has an expanded scope with segmented approach to target key segments (Deep Tech, Micro VCs, Tech-driven innovative manufacturing, Sector/stage agnostic) and includes operational flexibilities such as supporting AIFs with larger corpuses for capital-intensive segments, longer duration AIFs for startups with longer R&D cycles, higher contribution for specific segments where private capital is limited, and moderation of investment multiplier to encourage larger number of AIFs to benefit from the Scheme.
application_process: 1. AIFs submit proposals to the Implementation Agency (IA). 2. IAs conduct due diligence on the proposals. 3. A Venture Capital Investment Committee (VCIC) constituted by DPIIT, including representation from industry, subject matter experts, and IAs, evaluates the proposals. 4. VCIC considers AIFs managed by experienced professionals with proven track records for funding under the Scheme. 5. Post approval, AIFs raise funds from other investors/contributors to meet the target corpus amount including from Startup India FoF 2.0. 6. AIFs evaluate startups for investments in tranches over a period. 7. Supported AIFs mentor and nurture their investee startups before exit/sale of investments.
implementing_agency: Small Industries Development Bank of India (SIDBI)

overview: The Government of India has launched a comprehensive initiative to foster innovation and support start-ups through its Startup Accelerator of MeitY for Product Innovation, Development, and Growth (SAMRIDH) program, addressing the need for an accelerator program that drives social impact and scales solutions for India's challenges. The program aims to support 300 tech Start-Ups in 3-year duration with cohort size of 5-10 Start-Ups. SAMRIDH Program is conceptualized and being implemented to provide support to selected accelerators for extending acceleration services to start-ups along with one-to-one matching funding support of up to ₹40 lakh. In the first round of cohort, 22 Accelerators spread across 12 states are supporting 175 startups, selected through a multilevel screening process.
eligibility: The program supports selected accelerators for extending acceleration services to start-ups. Accelerators are selected through a multilevel screening process. In the first round of cohort, 22 Accelerators spread across 12 states are supporting 175 startups.
benefits: Support to selected accelerators for extending acceleration services to start-ups along with one-to-one matching funding support of up to ₹40 lakh. Benefits include customer connect, investors connect and connect to international markets for startups.
financial_support: SAMRIDH Program provides one-to-one matching funding support of up to ₹40 lakh to selected accelerators for extending acceleration services to start-ups.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to ₹40 lakh
application_process: The evidence does not provide a numbered step-by-step application procedure for the SAMRIDH scheme.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India

overview: Digital India Bhashini is an initiative under the Digital India Programme, implemented by the Digital India Corporation (DIC) under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY). It aims to facilitate seamless communication between various Indian languages and English, promoting linguistic inclusivity and digital empowerment. The initiative fosters collaboration between startups and government agencies to accelerate technological innovation while celebrating linguistic diversity.
eligibility: The initiative collaborates with startups and government agencies; specific eligibility criteria for participation are not detailed in the provided evidence.
benefits: Promotes linguistic inclusivity, enables seamless multilingual communication, supports digital empowerment, fosters innovation through startup-government collaboration, and preserves India’s linguistic diversity as a tool for inclusive digital growth.
financial_support: No specific financial support mechanism (grants, loans, subsidies) is mentioned for beneficiaries under Digital India Bhashini in the provided evidence.
application_process: No step-by-step application process for entities to join or benefit from Digital India Bhashini is described in the evidence.
deadlines: No application deadlines or submission windows are specified for Digital India Bhashini in the evidence.
implementing_agency: Digital India Corporation (DIC) under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)

overview: FutureSkills Prime is a collaborative initiative between the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) and the National Association of Software and Service Companies (NASSCOM) aimed at transforming India into a Digital Talent Nation by providing cutting-edge skills in emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, data analytics, cloud computing, and cybersecurity. The program aligns with National Occupational Standards (NOS) and the National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF) to ensure industry-relevant certification and employability. It offers a dynamic learning environment for students and professionals to acquire in-demand digital technology skills that are valued by employers.
eligibility: Open to students and professionals seeking to acquire skills in digital technology domains such as artificial intelligence, data analytics, cloud computing, cybersecurity, and related fields as per National Occupational Standards (NOS) and National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF).
benefits: Industry-recognized certification aligned with NOS and NSQF, enhanced employability in digital technology roles such as machine learning engineer, data scientist, business intelligence analyst, and information security analyst, access to a dynamic learning environment, and validation of skills for career advancement in the digital economy.
financial_support: The scheme does not provide direct financial support to individuals; it focuses on skill development and certification through industry-backed programs offered by NASSCOM and MeitY.
application_process: 1. Visit the FutureSkills Prime portal at https://futureskillsprime.in/ 2. Explore available courses in emerging technologies 3. Select a course aligned with career goals 4. Enroll in the chosen program 5. Complete the training and assessment 6. Earn certification upon successful completion.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) in collaboration with National Association of Software and Service Companies (NASSCOM)

overview: MeitY Startup Hub (MSH) is a nodal entity under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, established to promote technology innovation, startups, and creation of Intellectual Properties. It acts as a national coordination, facilitation, and monitoring centre integrating incubation centres, startups, and innovation-related activities of MeitY. MSH supports the startup ecosystem through various programs, partnerships, and initiatives including funding, mentorship, infrastructure access, and global market exposure.
target_beneficiaries: startups; incubators; accelerators; mentors; investors; MSME
eligibility: The scheme is open to startups, incubators, accelerators, mentors, and investors participating in the MeitY Startup Hub ecosystem. Specific eligibility criteria vary by program and are defined under individual initiatives such as SAMRIDH, TIDE 2.0, GENESIS, and corporate partnerships.
benefits: Benefits include access to funding through government schemes, mentorship by industry experts, access to labs and infrastructure, networking opportunities with investors and corporates, participation in hackathons and challenges, technology and infrastructure support, global market access via international delegations, and support for deep-tech innovation through partnerships with companies like MathWorks, Micron, Razorpay, and others.
financial_support: Financial support is provided through various government schemes such as SAMRIDH, TIDE 2.0, and GENESIS, where incubators receive grants to invest in startups. For example, the Micron-backed CREST accelerator provides funding of up to Rs. 10 Lacs per startup. MSH has disbursed ₹49.21 crore to 51 TIDE 2.0 Centers, 23 SAMRIDH Accelerators, and 63 GENESIS Implementing Agencies. Funding mechanisms include equity-linked opportunities, tailored credit lines, and seed grants via incubators.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to Rs. 10 Lacs (e.g., in CREST accelerator by Micron)
application_process: 1. Identify the relevant program (e.g., SAMRIDH, TIDE 2.0, GENESIS, or corporate partnership). 2. Visit the MeitY Startup Hub website (https://msh.meity.gov.in/). 3. Navigate to the specific program page. 4. Click on 'Apply Now' or similar call-to-action. 5. Fill out the application form with required details. 6. Upload necessary documents. 7. Submit the application. 8. Await evaluation by the selection committee. 9. If selected, proceed with onboarding and benefit activation.
deadlines: Varies by program; some have fixed deadlines (e.g., MedTech OCP 4.0 deadline: Mar 29, 2023), while others operate on a rolling basis or are announced periodically.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India

overview: NIDHI Prayas is a sub-scheme under the NIDHI (National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations) programme by the Department of Science and Technology (DST). It provides financial support in the form of grants to innovators for converting their ideas into prototypes. The scheme aims to promote innovation and entrepreneurship by supporting early-stage prototyping and proof-of-concept development.
eligibility: Individual innovators, startups, or entrepreneurs with a proof-of-concept or idea that requires prototyping support. The applicant must be an Indian citizen and the innovation should have potential for commercialization or social impact.
benefits: Financial support for prototyping, access to fabrication labs, mentorship, technical guidance, and networking opportunities with experts and investors.
financial_support: Grant-in-aid support up to INR 10 lakhs per innovator for prototyping and proof-of-concept development. The grant is disbursed in milestones based on progress review.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 10 lakhs
application_process: 1. Apply online through the NIDHI Prayas portal. 2. Submit idea/innovation details along with prototype development plan. 3. Undergo screening and evaluation by experts. 4. Shortlisted applicants present their idea before a selection committee. 5. Selected innovators receive grant approval and sign agreement. 6. Develop prototype with periodic reviews. 7. Submit final prototype and report for closure.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Department of Science and Technology (DST)

overview: NIDHI EIR (Entrepreneur-in-Residence) is a component of the National Initiative for Developing and Harnessing Innovations (NIDHI) programme under the Department of Science & Technology (DST). It aims to support aspiring entrepreneurs by providing a fellowship to pursue innovative business ideas without risk, encouraging them to take up entrepreneurship as a career option. The scheme offers financial support and mentorship to help convert ideas into viable startups.
eligibility: Individuals with innovative business ideas who are committed to pursuing entrepreneurship as a career. Applicants must be Indian nationals, preferably with a background in science, technology, or engineering, and should not be currently employed or engaged in any other full-time activity that would hinder full-time dedication to the entrepreneurial venture. The idea should be at the ideation or early prototype stage and have potential for scalability and innovation.
benefits: Selected Entrepreneurs-in-Residence receive a monthly fellowship grant, access to mentorship and guidance from experts, networking opportunities, and support in refining their business ideas. The program also provides access to incubation facilities, technical support, and assistance in preparing for further funding or incubation programs.
financial_support: The scheme provides a monthly fellowship of INR 30,000 for a period of up to 12 months to support the Entrepreneur-in-Residence in developing their innovative idea into a viable startup. The financial support is disbursed through the implementing NIDHI TBI (Technology Business Incubator) or other approved host institutions.
grant_amount_per_entity: INR 30,000 per month for up to 12 months
application_process: 1. Identify a NIDHI TBI or approved host institution offering the NIDHI EIR fellowship. 2. Prepare a detailed proposal outlining the innovative idea, its novelty, potential impact, and execution plan. 3. Submit the application along with required documents to the host institution. 4. Undergo evaluation by a selection committee based on innovation, feasibility, and entrepreneurial commitment. 5. Selected candidates are awarded the fellowship and begin the 12-month residency period.
deadlines: Varies by host institution; typically announced periodically
implementing_agency: Department of Science & Technology (DST), Government of India

overview: The GENESIS Program (Gen-Next Support for Innovative Startups) is a Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) initiative aimed at boosting the startup ecosystem in Tier-II and Tier-III cities across India. With a budgetary outlay of Rs. 490 Crore over 5 years, the scheme supports approximately 1600 tech startups by providing funding and support across pilot, early-stage, deep-tech, and investment stages. It focuses on consolidating MeitY's startup-related schemes and assets for greater efficiency and enhanced support.
eligibility: The scheme is designed for technology startups in Tier-II and Tier-III cities; specific eligibility criteria for individual components (EiR, Pilot Funding, Investment Support, Deep-Tech Funding) are not detailed in the provided evidence.
benefits: Support includes Entrepreneur in Residence (EiR) for idea validation, Pilot Funding for product piloting, Investment Support for market fundraising, and Deep-Tech Funding for high-impact solutions; non-financial benefits include mentorship, capacity building, and ecosystem strengthening in Tier-II/III cities.
financial_support: The scheme provides financial support through four components: Entrepreneur in Residence (EiR) Support, Pilot Funding Support, Investment Support, and Deep-Tech Funding Support, targeting startups at various stages from idea validation to scaling deep-tech solutions.
application_process: The application process is not explicitly detailed in the provided evidence; interested startups are advised to refer to the Implementing Agencies list and official links such as the Administrative Approval and Scheme Brochure (attached PDFs) for application procedures.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India

overview: The NIDHI Seed Support Program (NIDHI-SSP) is designed to provide timely financial assistance to incubators to support potential startups with promising ideas, innovations, and technologies. The key objective is to ensure availability of seed support through incubators for providing timely support to potential startups, enabling them to take their ventures to the next level and facilitate their success in the marketplace. The scheme also enables incubators to widen their pipeline of startups and share the success of their startups, ensuring long-term operational sustainability. This seed support acts as a bridge between the development and commercialization of innovative technologies, products, and services.
eligibility: For Incubators/TBIs: Applications for financial support will be accepted once per year through an online portal. A call for applications will be announced at the appropriate time. For Startups/Entrepreneurs: Innovators and startups can apply to one of the listed Startup Incubators/Technology Business Incubators (TBIs) for the NIDHI Seed Support Program (NIDHI-SSP). The Startup Incubators/TBIs announce periodic calls for applications, and innovators and startups are encouraged to visit the website of Startup Incubators regularly to stay informed about these calls.
benefits: For Incubators/TBIs: The maximum financial support that will be made available to an incubator is INR 1000 lakh, which will be released in phases according to the needs, capacity, and capabilities of the incubator, following a proper process. For Startups/Entrepreneurs: The maximum support that will be given to a deserving startup is up to INR 100 lakh. This support will be provided to help startups get off the ground and grow, increasing their chances of success in the marketplace.
financial_support: For Incubators/TBIs: The maximum financial support that will be made available to an incubator is INR 1000 lakh, which will be released in phases according to the needs, capacity, and capabilities of the incubator, following a proper process. For Startups/Entrepreneurs: The maximum support that will be given to a deserving startup is up to INR 100 lakh. This support will be provided to help startups get off the ground and grow, increasing their chances of success in the marketplace.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 100 lakh per startup
application_process: 1. Applications for financial support will be accepted once per year through an online portal. 2. A call for applications will be announced at the appropriate time. 3. Innovators and startups can apply to one of the listed Startup Incubators/Technology Business Incubators (TBIs) for the NIDHI Seed Support Program (NIDHI-SSP). 4. The Startup Incubators/TBIs announce periodic calls for applications. 5. Innovators and startups are encouraged to visit the website of Startup Incubators regularly to stay informed about these calls.
deadlines: Applications for financial support will be accepted once per year through an online portal. A call for applications will be announced at the appropriate time.
implementing_agency: Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India
contact_details: For more information, please write us an email on rajivarc[at]nic[dot]in

overview: Technology Business Incubators (TBIs) are initiatives under the Startup India program aimed at promoting new technology/knowledge/innovation-based startups that create jobs, wealth, and business in alignment with national priorities. They provide a platform for speedy commercialization of technologies developed by host institutions or academic/technical/R&D institutions, and offer cost-effective, value-added services such as mentoring, legal, financial, technical, and intellectual property-related support to startups.
target_beneficiaries: startups; entrepreneurs; students; academic institutions; MSME
eligibility: The proposal is to be submitted by an Academic/Technical R and D Institutions (called the host institute) and other institutions with a proven track record in the promotion of technology-based entrepreneurship. The host institute is supposed to have adequate expertise and infrastructure to support incubation activity. In case, the host institute is other than an academic institution, it should be a legal entity registered in India with the clear purpose and objective of promoting research, innovation, and entrepreneurial ecosystem. It would be desirable to have partnership with at least one academic institute of repute/technical institute of repute/existing TBIs or STEPs/institutions/government bodies engaged in the promotion of innovation and entrepreneurship. The financial support for establishing a TBI would be extended to a not-for-profit legal entity registered as a society/trust/section 8 company. DST encourages a TBI to collaborate with industry, an academic institution, and other institutions of repute focussing on innovation, research, commercialisation, and startup promotion. DST does not support for-profit incubators. The host institution should be in existence for at least 3 years.
benefits: Technology Business Incubators provide infrastructure, mentorship, financial support, legal assistance, technical support, and intellectual property-related services to nurture and support the innovation of startups. They facilitate speedy commercialization of technologies, offer value-added services, and help startups access funding, markets, and networking opportunities. TBIs also support ecosystem development through workshops, boot camps, conferences, and capacity-building initiatives.
financial_support: Financial support for establishing a Technology Business Incubator (TBI) is extended to a not-for-profit legal entity registered as a society/trust/section 8 company. The support includes grants for infrastructure, equipment, and operational expenditure. Specific funding details vary by scheme (e.g., DST-TBI, DBT-BioNest, AIM-AIC, MeitY-TIDE 2.0), with some offering recurring grants (e.g., Himachal Pradesh provides up to INR 30 lakhs per incubator per year for three years) or one-time capital assistance (e.g., Madhya Pradesh provides up to INR 50 lakhs).
application_process: 1. Submit proposal by an Academic/Technical R&D Institution or other eligible entity with proven track record in technology-based entrepreneurship. 2. Ensure host institute has adequate expertise and infrastructure to support incubation activity. 3. If host institute is non-academic, register as a legal entity in India with the purpose of promoting research, innovation, and entrepreneurial ecosystem. 4. Seek partnership with at least one academic/technical institute of repute or existing TBI/STEP/government body. 5. Apply for financial support as a not-for-profit legal entity (society/trust/section 8 company). 6. Collaborate with industry and academic institutions for innovation, research, commercialisation, and startup promotion. 7. Ensure host institution has existed for at least 3 years. 8. Await evaluation based on preparedness, approach, methodology, milestones, co-founding, sustainability, industry linkages, and business development support.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

overview: SERB Grants refer to funding provided by the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), a statutory body under the Department of Science and Technology (DST), to support basic research in science and engineering. These grants are aimed at promoting high-quality research through financial assistance to individual researchers, academic institutions, and R&D organizations. The scheme supports various categories of grants including core research grants, fellowships, and travel grants to foster innovation and scientific excellence across India.
target_beneficiaries: researchers; academic institutions; scientific associations; R&D organizations
eligibility: Eligibility varies by grant type but generally includes Indian nationals with a Ph.D. in science or engineering, regular faculty or scientists in recognized academic institutions or R&D laboratories, and individuals affiliated with SERB-recognized organizations. Specific criteria depend on the grant category such as Core Research Grant (CRG), Early Career Research Award (ECRA), or Ramanujan Fellowship.
benefits: Financial support for research projects, fellowships, travel, and equipment; access to research networks; recognition through prestigious fellowships; opportunities for national and international collaboration; and support for publishing research outcomes.
financial_support: SERB provides financial assistance in the form of grants for manpower, consumables, travel, contingency, and overheads. The quantum and duration depend on the specific grant scheme, with funding typically covering project costs for 3 years. Fellowships include monthly stipends and research grants.
application_process: 1. Identify the appropriate SERB grant scheme based on eligibility and research objectives. 2. Register on the SERB online portal (serbonline.in). 3. Fill out the application form with project details, budget, and timeline. 4. Upload required documents such as CV, publications, and institutional endorsement. 5. Submit the application before the deadline. 6. Applications undergo peer review and evaluation by expert committees. 7. Selected applicants are notified and funds are disbursed upon approval.
deadlines: Varies by scheme; specific calls are announced periodically on the SERB portal.
implementing_agency: Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), Department of Science and Technology (DST), Ministry of Science and Technology

overview: The SAIF Facilities scheme under the Department of Science & Technology (DST) provides access to sophisticated analytical instruments and facilities for research and development activities. It supports academic institutions, national laboratories, and industry by offering shared access to high-end equipment. The scheme aims to promote scientific research and technological development through centralized facility sharing.
target_beneficiaries: academic institutions; national laboratories; industry; research scholars
eligibility: Academic institutions, national laboratories, and industry entities engaged in scientific research and development activities are eligible to access SAIF Facilities. Access is typically granted based on project proposals and institutional requirements.
benefits: Access to sophisticated analytical instruments, shared use of high-end equipment, reduced capital expenditure for individual institutions, enhanced research capabilities, and promotion of collaborative R&D activities.
financial_support: The scheme does not provide direct financial support; instead, it facilitates access to existing infrastructure and equipment. Users may be charged nominal user fees for facility utilization, but no grants or loans are disbursed under this scheme.
application_process: 1. Identify the required facility or instrument from the SAIF network. 2. Submit an application through the respective SAIF center. 3. Provide project details and justification for facility use. 4. Obtain approval from the facility in-charge. 5. Schedule access and utilize the facility as per allocated time slots. 6. Submit utilization report and settle any applicable user charges.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Department of Science & Technology (DST)

overview: The Research Development and Innovation (RDI) Scheme is a flagship initiative under the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India, designed to accelerate investment in India's R&D and Innovation ecosystem. Approved by the Union Cabinet on 1 July 2025 and launched by the Hon'ble Prime Minister on 3 November 2025, the scheme aims to catalyse private sector investment in R&D with a total outlay of ₹1 lakh crore over six years, including ₹20,000 crore in FY 2025-26. It supports private sector enterprises, startups, and industries working in sunrise and strategic sectors to transform ideas into globally competitive technologies and products, with a focus on high-TRL projects (TRL 4 and above), acquisition of critical technologies, and creation of Deep-Tech Funds.
eligibility: Eligible Technology Entities (ETEs) include any legal entity engaged in developing Research, Development & Innovation (RDI)-intensive technology (Technology Readiness Level 4 and above), such as companies registered under the Companies Act, 2013, partnerships under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932, limited liability partnerships under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008, and startups as defined in the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade Notification G.S.R. 127(E) dated 19 February 2019, as may be modified from time to time. Entities must be registered in India under Indian laws and under control of resident Indian citizens, retain ownership of IPR registered in India under Indian laws/rules, and comply with GoI directions on security, export control, and other strategic concerns.
benefits: The scheme provides long-term, innovation-driven financing through low-interest long-term loans (typically unsecured), equity financing (especially for startups and early-stage deep-tech ventures), and contributions to Deep-Tech Fund of Funds (FoF) or other ROI-oriented FoFs aligned with national innovation priorities. It does not provide grants or short-term loans. Financing is available up to 50% of the total assessed project cost, with the remaining cost to be met by the project proponent through self-financing or commercial sources. In exceptional cases or strategic sectors, this limit may be relaxed with EGoS approval. The scheme also supports patient capital at low rates to survive failure risk and sustain R&D’s extended gestation periods.
financial_support: The RDI Fund provides financing up to 50% of the total assessed project cost, with the remaining 50% to be sourced by the project proponent from self-financing or commercial sources. In exceptional cases or strategic sectors, this limit may be relaxed with EGoS approval. Financing modes include low-interest long-term loans (typically unsecured), equity financing (especially for startups), and contributions to Deep-Tech Fund of Funds (FoF). The scheme does not provide grants or short-term loans. Funding is routed through Second Level Fund Managers (SLFMs) such as Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), Development Finance Institutions (DFIs), Non-Banking Finance Companies (NBFCs), and Focused Research Organisations (FROs), which invest in Eligible Technology Entities (ETEs) advancing RDI-intensive technologies.
application_process: 1. Register on the RDIF Management Portal at www.rdifund.anrf.gov.in. 2. Submit application via the portal’s workflows by the prescribed deadline. 3. Provide information and supporting documents related to Quality criteria in Digital Sealed Cover 1. 4. Provide undertakings related to Cost criteria in Digital Sealed Cover 2. 5. Applications are evaluated using the Quality and Cost Based Selection (QCBS) method with 80% weightage to Quality criteria and 20% to Cost criteria. 6. Quality criteria assess General Competence, R&D and RDI-Intensive Technology Focus, and ‘Crowd In’ of private capital. 7. Cost criteria for AIFs focus on management fees and carried interest; for Loan modality, on net interest margins and management fees. 8. Selected SLFMs must invest RDIF funds only in companies including startups advancing RDI-intensive technologies at TRL 4 and above, working in RDIF Priority Sectors, and in accordance with Funding Guidelines.
deadlines: Applications must be submitted on the RDIF Management Portal at www.rdifund.anrf.gov.in by 1700 hours IST on 31 January 2026.
implementing_agency: Department of Science & Technology (DST), Ministry of Science & Technology
contact_details: Email: rdi-fund@anrf.gov.in | RDI Cell - DST Room No. 7A, Old S&T Building, Technology Bhawan | RDIF Unit - ANRF Room No. 8, 4th Floor, Block II, Technology Bhawan, New Mehrauli Road, New Delhi - 110 016

overview: National Deep Tech Startup Policy (NDTSP) aims to support and nurture the unique requirements of Deep Tech startups in India. It establishes a foundational framework for India’s emerging deep tech ecosystem, anchored on four pillars: Ensuring the Security of India's Economic Future, Facilitating a Seamless Transition to a Knowledge Driven Economy, Bolstering National Capability and Sovereignty through the Atmanirbhar Bharat Imperative, and Fostering Ethical Innovation. The policy was developed following a recommendation by the Prime Minister's Science, Technology & Innovation Advisory Council (PM-STIAC) in July 2022 and involved a high-level NDTSP Consortium and Working Group for policy design and coordination.
eligibility: Deep tech startups are defined as entities working on producing a solution based on new knowledge/advancements within a scientific or engineering discipline or multiple disciplines, which is yet to be developed or is in the process of being developed; having a high percentage of expenditure on research and development (R&D) activities as a percentage of revenue/funding; owning or being in the process of creating significant novel intellectual property (IP) and taking steps to commercialize the same; and facing extended development timelines, long gestation periods, high capital and infrastructure requirements, and carrying large technical or scientific uncertainty.
benefits: The policy provides targeted support including financial incentives and regulatory measures for high-risk, high-impact startups through mechanisms such as the Deep Tech Fund of Funds (FoF), regulatory sandboxes, Deep Tech Translation Program, IP regime alignment, and Bharat Deep Tech Talent Pool. It enables access to venture capital, pilot debt-based funding, patient and global capital, and flexibility in government funding caps. It also supports IP registration, legal support, dispute resolution, faster examinations, reduced fees, patent valuation support, institutional capacity building, centralized IP database, IP insurance, and global protection frameworks.
financial_support: The Deep Tech Fund of Funds (FoF) under NDTSP is supported by the Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 with a corpus of ₹10,000 crore to mobilize venture capital, with expanded focus on deep tech startups. Operational guidelines incorporate flexibilities tailored to the unique needs of deep tech, including pilot debt-based funding and attraction of patient and global capital.
implementing_agency: Office of the Principal Scientific Adviser (PSA) to the Government of India

scheme_name: IndiaAI Mission – National Program on Artificial Intelligence
overview: The IndiaAI Mission – National Program on Artificial Intelligence is an umbrella initiative by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) to leverage transformative AI technologies for social impact, inclusion, innovation, and adoption. It encompasses key pillars such as the National Center on AI, Data Management Office, Skilling in AI, and Responsible AI. The program aims to create a comprehensive framework for AI development and deployment across sectors in India.
target_beneficiaries: startups; researchers; students; government agencies; industry
eligibility: The evidence does not specify explicit eligibility criteria for entities or individuals to benefit from the IndiaAI Mission. The program is structured as a national initiative with defined pillars, but no application-based eligibility is detailed in the provided text.
benefits: The program provides access to AI research and development through the National Center on AI, data infrastructure via the Data Management Office, skill development initiatives in AI, and frameworks for responsible AI governance. These benefits support innovation, inclusion, and adoption of AI technologies across sectors.
financial_support: The evidence does not detail direct financial support mechanisms (e.g., grants, subsidies) to individual beneficiaries under the IndiaAI Mission. Financial aspects are discussed in the context of overall Digital India Programme budget allocations, which include provisions for the IndiaAI Mission following separate Cabinet approval.
application_process: The evidence does not describe a numbered step-by-step application process for availing benefits under the IndiaAI Mission. The program is presented as a strategic initiative with defined pillars rather than a scheme with individual applications.
deadlines: The evidence does not mention any application deadlines, windows, or rolling basis for the IndiaAI Mission. It is structured as an ongoing national program.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY)

overview: The STPI + C-DOT Samarth Incubation Program is an initiative by Software Technology Parks of India (STPI) in collaboration with C-DOT to support startups through incubation services. It provides infrastructure, mentorship, funding access, and networking opportunities to foster innovation and entrepreneurship in emerging technologies. The program is part of STPI's broader mission to strengthen India's software product ecosystem and promote startup growth across Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
eligibility: Startups and entrepreneurs working in emerging technologies such as AI, IoT, Blockchain, FinTech, MedTech, AgriTech, Cybersecurity, AR/VR, and other domain-specific areas are eligible to apply. The program is open to innovators from Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities to promote inclusive growth. Applicants must be engaged in technology-driven product development and demonstrate potential for scalability and market impact.
benefits: Selected startups receive access to STPI's incubation facilities, mentorship from industry experts, networking opportunities with investors and financial institutions, infrastructure support (hardware/software labs), guidance on IPR and product development, and assistance in market access and go-to-market strategies. The program also provides exposure to government schemes and policies supporting innovation and entrepreneurship.
financial_support: The program provides seed funding support to startups, with financial assistance facilitated through linkages with venture capitalists, angel investors, and government funding schemes. Specific quantum and disbursement mechanisms are determined based on startup evaluation and available funding partners. STPI helps startups access financial support but does not directly grant funds in all cases.
application_process: 1. Identify the relevant Centre of Entrepreneurship (CoE) based on your technology domain and location. 2. Visit the STPI website or contact the respective CoE for program details and application guidelines. 3. Submit an online application through the STPI portal or designated application channel. 4. Provide necessary documents including business plan, team details, and proof of innovation. 5. Undergo screening and evaluation by the selection committee. 6. If shortlisted, participate in interviews or pitch sessions. 7. Receive selection notification and complete onboarding formalities. 8. Begin incubation with access to infrastructure, mentorship, and support services.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year based on cohort availability and program cycles.
implementing_agency: Software Technology Parks of India (STPI), Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

overview: Biotechnology Ignition Grant (BIG) scheme is the flagship programme of BIRAC which supports young startups. It provides early-stage funding support of INR 50 Lakhs as grant-in-aid for a period of 18 months to translate innovative ideas into Proof of Concept. The purpose of the BIG Scheme is to foster generation of ideas with commercialisation potential, upscale and validate proof of concept, encourage researchers to take technology closer to market through a startup, and stimulate enterprise formation.
eligibility: The Company/LLP should be registered under the Indian Companies Act, 1956/2013. The incorporation date of the Company/LLP should not be earlier than 5 years from the date of launch of a particular BIG call. A company is considered as 'owned' by resident Indian citizen(s) if minimum 51% of the capital is beneficially owned by resident Indian citizen(s) and/or Indian companies, which are ultimately owned and controlled by resident Indian citizens. Company/LLP’s application should be represented by a Project Leader. The Company/LLP must have its own in-house R&D facility that is functional and adequate to execute the project. If the Company/LLP does not have a functional and adequate laboratory of its own then it should be incubated at an incubator. If an applicant company, in which any promoter holding more than or equal to 20% of the shares, is a co-promoter of another ineligible company or a partner in another ineligible LLP, then the applicant will not be eligible. The only exception to this shall be a situation wherein the latter ineligible company/LLP is operating in a non-biotech domain. Companies that are in default of loan repayment to BIRAC, as well as their promoters, shall be deemed ineligible to apply under the scheme.
benefits: BIG provides early-stage funding support of INR 50 Lakhs as grant-in-aid for a period of 18 months. Successful BIG Innovators receive up to INR 50 lakh for research projects with commercialization potential with duration of up to 18 months. As part of the scheme, successful BIG Innovators receive mentoring, monitoring, networking and other business development related activities through 8 BIG Partners across the country.
financial_support: The funding support under BIG is in the form of a grant-in-aid. Extent of funding is up to INR 50 lakhs. The fund allocation under BIG is milestone based and released in 3-4 installments: 1. INR 20.00 lakhs upon signing of the Grant Agreement. 2. One to two instalments, each of up to INR 20.00 lakhs to be released based on satisfactory progress of project milestones and funding requirement. 3. Up to INR 5.00 lakhs upon successful completion of the project and submission of the Final Technical Report. Note: The final tranche shall be released as reimbursement upon submission and acceptance of the final Utilization Certificate (UC), Statement of Expenditure (SoE), and the Project Completion Report. Total fund not exceeding INR 50 lakhs. The funding is provided for a period of up to 18 months. The project is implemented in a milestone-based manner.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 50 lakhs
application_process: 1. BIRAC issues a national call inviting applications under BIG at least twice a year (typically on 1st of January & 1st of July). 2. The Call for Proposals is advertised through one or more means that may include BIRAC website, newspapers, journals and magazines. 3. The Call for Proposals is typically open for a period of 30 days. 4. The BIG Scheme is implemented across the country through BIRAC’s BIG Partners. The Call will mention the names of BIG Partners for the particular call. 5. Application for BIG needs to be submitted online ONLY through BIRAC website www.birac.nic.in. 6. Applicants are advised to fill-up and submit their applications early without waiting for the last date in order to avoid last-minute rush. Please note that the online system will stop accepting applications automatically at the specific date and time mentioned in the Call details. 7. Applicants are advised to provide sufficient details in their applications to allow for an informed and fair evaluation/review. 8. Requests for changes in a proposal once submitted will not be entertained. 9. Providing incorrect information intentionally is viewed adversely and may lead to disqualification.
deadlines: Call announcement calendar: The call for proposal is announced twice a year, i.e. on 1st January and 1st July. Call for proposals typically remains open for about 45 days.
implementing_agency: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), a Public Sector Enterprise, set up by Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India

overview: The Multiplier Grants Scheme (MGS) is implemented by the Department of Electronics and Information Technology (DeitY) to encourage collaborative R&D between industry and academic/R&D institutions for the development of commercially viable products and packages. Under the scheme, if industry supports R&D for product development at the institution level, the government provides financial support up to twice the amount contributed by industry. Proposals must be submitted jointly by industry and academic/R&D institutions.
target_beneficiaries: startups; incubator; academia; accelerator; R&D institutions
eligibility: Startup, Incubator/Academia/Accelerator having projects in electronics & information technology
benefits: Government provides financial support up to twice the amount contributed by industry; grants are given to academic/R&D institution(s) only; supports collaborative R&D for commercializable products; funding duration preferably less than 2 years for individual industry and 3 years for industry consortium.
financial_support: The Government grants, for individual industry, would be limited to a maximum of Rs. 2.0 Crores per project and the duration of each project should, preferably, be less than 2 years; for industry consortium these figures would be Rs. 4.0 Crores and 3 years respectively. The contribution of industry and grant-in-aid from DeitY will be given to academic/R&D institution(s) only.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 2.0 Crores per project for individual industry; Rs. 4.0 Crores for industry consortium
application_process: 1. Identify a collaborative R&D project between industry and academic/R&D institution in electronics & information technology. 2. Ensure the startup/incubator/academia/accelerator has a project in electronics & IT. 3. Prepare a joint proposal from industry and institution. 4. Submit the proposal jointly by industry and academic/R&D institution. 5. Await evaluation and approval based on commercialization potential and eligibility.
implementing_agency: Department of Electronics and Information Technology (DeitY), Govt of India

overview: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) is Government of India’s flagship initiative to create and promote a culture of innovation and entrepreneurship across the country. AIM’s objective is to develop new programmes and policies for fostering innovation in different sectors of the economy, provide platforms and collaboration opportunities for different stakeholders, and create an umbrella structure to oversee the innovation & entrepreneurship ecosystem of the country. It was set up in 2016 by NITI Aayog and has launched multiple programs including Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL), Atal Incubation Centres (AIC), Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC), Atal New India Challenges (ANIC), and others to support innovation at school, university, and enterprise levels.
target_beneficiaries: students; startups; entrepreneurs; MSME; women entrepreneurs; tribal entrepreneurs; rural innovators; academic institutions; research institutions
eligibility: Eligibility varies by sub-program: For Atal Tinkering Labs (ATL), schools across India can apply; for Atal Incubation Centres (AIC), universities, institutions, and corporates can apply; for Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC), applicants must be from underserved regions with partnership and matching funding; for Atal New India Challenge (ANIC), Indian companies (MSMEs or DPIIT-recognized startups) with a working prototype and majority Indian stakeholding are eligible; for Youth Co:Lab, youth-led startups with founders aged 15-29 (extended to 35 for women, PwDs, LGBTIQA+, tribal, and minority entrepreneurs) can apply; for AIM-PRIME, technology developers, incubation managers, scientists, and academicians with science-based deep tech ideas are eligible.
benefits: Benefits include access to state-of-the-art infrastructure (e.g., ATLs with IoT, 3D printing, robotics), mentorship from 6200+ Mentors of Change, funding support (up to INR 1 crore under ANIC, up to INR 2.5 crores for ACIC with matching), co-working spaces, lab facilities, networking opportunities, technical and business support, prototype development assistance, commercialization support, access to global ecosystems (e.g., RISE Accelerator), seed grants (INR 2.2–3.5 lakhs in Youth Co:Lab), skill development in design thinking and entrepreneurship, exposure to investors and markets, and recognition through national platforms and awards.
financial_support: AIM provides financial support through various programs: Atal New India Challenge (ANIC) offers grant-in-aid of up to INR 1 crore for startups over 12–18 months; Atal Community Innovation Centres (ACIC) receive up to INR 2.5 crores from AIM subject to equal or greater matching funding from partners; Youth Co:Lab National Innovation Challenge provides seed grants of INR 2.2–3.5 lakhs to top 6 startups; AIM-PRIME offers guidance and training but no direct financial grant; Atal Tinkering Labs receive grant-in-aid for establishment (amount not specified in evidence); Fund of Funds for Startups (FFS) of INR 10,000 Cr is managed by SIDBI (mentioned in context but not directly under AIM).
application_process: 1. Visit the official AIM website (aim.gov.in) to check for open applications under specific programs (ATL, AIC, ACIC, ANIC, etc.). 2. Note that application portals for ATL, AIC, and ACIC are currently not open (as per notice on homepage). 3. For open programs, register on the designated portal (e.g., atl.aim.gov.in for ATL). 4. Fill out the application form with required details (school/institution info, project proposal, etc.). 5. Upload necessary documents (certificates, prototypes, team details, etc.). 6. Submit the application and await evaluation. 7. Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews, presentations, or field verification. 8. Final selection is communicated via email or portal. 9. Selected entities receive grant-in-aid and must submit utilization certificates and progress reports. 10. Post-selection, beneficiaries gain access to mentorship, infrastructure, and ecosystem support.
deadlines: Rolling basis — application portals for ATL, AIC, and ACIC are not currently open; specific programs like Youth Co:Lab have fixed deadlines (e.g., apply before midnight on 20th March 2026 for Youth Co:Lab 2026).
implementing_agency: Atal Innovation Mission (AIM), NITI Aayog, Government of India

overview: SBIRI (Small Business Innovation Research Initiative) is a flagship program of the Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India, aimed at promoting innovation and entrepreneurship in the biotechnology sector by providing financial support to startups and MSMEs for proof-of-concept, prototype development, and technology validation. The scheme encourages industry-academia collaboration and supports early-stage biotech ventures with high growth potential.
eligibility: Indian startups and MSMEs registered under the Companies Act, 2013 or LLP Act, 2008, working in the biotechnology sector, with innovative ideas requiring proof-of-concept or prototype development. The applicant must be a legal entity registered in India, and the technology should have potential for commercialization. Preference is given to ventures with strong technical feasibility and market potential.
benefits: Financial support for proof-of-concept and prototype development, mentorship and technical guidance from DBT and BIRAC, access to DBT’s network of laboratories and research institutions, assistance in IP filing and technology transfer, and support for scaling up successful prototypes to market-ready products.
financial_support: SBIRI provides grant-based financial support of up to INR 50 lakhs per project for proof-of-concept and prototype development. The funding is disbursed in milestones based on project progress and is managed through the Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC). The grant does not require equity dilution and is intended for early-stage validation activities.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 50 lakhs
application_process: 1. Register on the BIRAC website (www.birac.nic.in) 2. Log in to the SBIRI application portal 3. Fill in the online application form with project details 4. Upload required documents including project proposal, CVs of team members, and incorporation certificate 5. Submit the application for review 6. Await evaluation by the expert committee 7. If shortlisted, attend interview/presentation 8. Receive award letter upon selection 9. Sign agreement with BIRAC 10. Receive first tranche of funds upon compliance
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year with periodic review cycles.
implementing_agency: Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India

overview: PACE (Promoting Academic Research Conversion to Enterprise) is a BIRAC scheme designed to bridge the gap between academic research and commercialization by supporting the development and validation of technologies up to proof-of-concept and beyond. It comprises two components: Academic Innovation Research (AIR) for academia-led proof-of-concept development and Contract Research Scheme (CRS) for industrial validation of academic prototypes. The scheme aims to stimulate technological innovation, encourage academic-industry collaboration, and support translational research with societal or national relevance.
target_beneficiaries: academic institutions; universities; research organizations; NGOs; industry partners; LLPs
eligibility: Academic Institute, University, NGO or Research Foundation, registered/accredited by a government body can apply either alone, or in partnership with academia or industry (while involvement of industry is optional for AIR Scheme, it is mandatory to have an industrial partner for CRS). Under the scheme, academia (Public or Private Institute, University, NGO, Research Foundation or trust/society), National research laboratories having a well-established support system for research shall be the primary applicant. The PI has to be a permanent facility of the applicant entity. Applicant can apply either individually or jointly with academic and/or industrial partner. Eligibility Criteria for academia: For Public or Private Institute, University, NGO, or Research Foundation, proper registration/accreditation from a government body is mandatory like UGC affiliation certification, AICTE, CSIR/DSIR/SIRO certificate etc. Eligibility criteria (Technical) for applicants under AIR: PI must have completed at least one or more extramural funded project in India with cumulative project duration of 3 years & in the same research area of the project proposed.
