Projects must have been funded by Govt. funding agencies or Industry. Project/s executed as PI (and not as Co PI) will only be considered to establish eligibility. Related Sanction order or funding note to be uploaded as a proof. PI must have authored at least one publication (not abstract) indexed in Scopus/web of science/research paper published in peer reviewed journal as first or lead author, or patents (filed) in the same research area of the project proposed for AIR. (Conference papers and review papers will not be considered to establish eligibility). Evidence of proof of principle (POP) and preliminary data, already gathered by the applicant, supporting the proposal is compulsory and must be submitted in the AIR application. Absence of which can result in disqualification of the proposal. A justification on how the project on completion would be CRS ready must be included. Therefore, the proposal should include the strategy for taking forward the outcomes and results towards product development with an industrial partner (CRS guidelines may be referred for further particulars). Proposals involving agriculture should have viable product/technology as an outcome that can be considered for advanced trials by the industry/authorized national agencies. If the AIR proposal has industry participation, then the partnering/collaborating company/LLP should be more than 5 years after incorporation. Applicants are encouraged to have industry partners in order to demonstrate translational strategy. The final technical objective/milestone of the AIR proposal should reflect technology/result that is near to industry readiness. If the application is submitted under AIR with industry/LLP participation, then the collaborating company/LLP should be more than 5 years old. The PI has to be a permanent faculty of the applicant entity. Eligibility criteria (Technical) for applicants under CRS: Evidence of proof of Concept (minimum TRL –3) and validation ready data supporting the proposal is compulsory and must be submitted in the CRS application. Absence of which can result in disqualification of the proposal. Proposals that have received AIR funding should have the same industrial partner who collaborated for AIR project. Any deviation must be duly justified with clarity on IP governance. The CRS proposal should be accompanied by the Commitment Letter by the industrial partner to exercise the first right for monetizing the product/technology. Eligibility Criteria for Industrial partner: Company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013 or Minimum 51% of the shares of the Company should be held by Indian Citizens holding Indian passport (Indian Citizens do not include Person of Indian Origin (PIO) and Overseas Citizenship of India (OCI) holders). Participating companies should have adequate in-house facility to address the project implementation aspects (which shall be evaluated during the site visit) OR incubated with any of the recognized Incubation Facility.
benefits: Funding Support (Grant): Total cost of the project must not exceed Rs. 50 lakhs (Non-recurring cost must not exceed 10% of the total cost) for AIR. For CRS, funding support is provided to both academic as well as industrial partners. While funding is provided to the academia for In-House research which forms a part of validation of the Proof of Concept, funds are provided to the industrial partner for validation. There is no ceiling to the funding. IPR: IP rights may be with academia alone, or jointly shared between academia and industry (if academia establishes PoC with industry) as per the understanding between the two partners. Although the IP rights reside with the academia, the industry partner has first right of refusal for commercial exploitation of the New IP. Technical criteria for applicants under AIR: Applicant must have completed at least one extramural funded project in India (with minimum project duration of 3 years & in the same research area of the project proposed), project must have been funded by Govt. funding agencies or Industry. Related Sanction order or funding note to be uploaded as a proof. Applicant must have authored one publication (indexed in Scopus/web of science) as first or lead author, or patents (filed) in the same research area of the project proposed for AIR. Applicant must upload the published paper or filing documents related to IP at the time of submission of application. Evidence of proof of principle (POP) and preliminary data, already gathered by the applicant, supporting the proposal is compulsory and must be submitted in the AIR application. Absence of which can result in disqualification of the proposal. Proposals involving agriculture should have viable product/technology as an outcome that can be considered for advanced trials by the industry/authorized national agencies. The final technical milestone of the AIR proposal should reflect technology/result that is near to industry readiness. Technical criteria for applicants under CRS: Evidence of proof of Concept (PoC) i.e., TRL-3 and validation ready data supporting the proposal is compulsory and must be submitted in the CRS application. Absence of which can result in disqualification of the proposal. Proposals that have received AIR funding should have the same industrial partner who collaborated for AIR project. Any deviation must be duly justified with clarity on IP governance. The CRS proposal should be accompanied by the Commitment Letter by the industrial partner to exercise the first right for monetizing the product/technology.
financial_support: Funding Support (Grant): Total cost of the project must not exceed Rs. 50 lakhs (Non-recurring cost must not exceed 10% of the total cost) for AIR. For CRS, funding support is provided to both academic as well as industrial partners. While funding is provided to the academia for In-House research which forms a part of validation of the Proof of Concept, funds are provided to the industrial partner for validation. There is no ceiling to the funding.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 50 lakhs
application_process: 1. Log on the BIRAC website http://www.birac.nic.in/. 2. If you are a registered user, log-in using the credentials, else you need to register your institution with by clicking on New User Registration. 3. In case of new user registration, a computer-generated password would be sent to the email-id provided at the time of registration. The password can be changed later. 4. Once you login, you would be navigated to the page displaying PACE link. 5. Click on the PACE link under Programmes and the active call would be highlighted. 6. Click on the active call against which you wish to submit the proposal. 7. Further details on How to submit a proposal would be available in the PACE User Guide available on the website. Academic User Registration is open only when the call is active. Online Proposal Submission can be done only during an active call. (Advertisement for Call for proposals is released in all national dailies and some biotechnology related magazines. Intimation of an active call is also displayed on the DBT and BIRAC websites). Please note: The number of ongoing projects at a given point of time must not exceed two as primary applicant and two as collaborator. The applicant must submit an undertaking at the time of submission of the proposal that there is no duplicate funding from any other funding agency for the same or similar proposal.
deadlines: 1st April – 15th May and 1st September – 15th October (two regular calls per year). Besides two regular calls, BIRAC may also announce need-based additional calls whose terms and conditions would be worked out separately and described in their respective RFPs.
implementing_agency: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), a Public Sector Enterprise, set up by Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India

overview: The Prime Minister's Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP) is a credit-linked subsidy scheme aimed at generating employment opportunities through the establishment of micro-enterprises in the non-farm sector. It provides financial assistance to individuals, self-help groups, and institutions for setting up new micro-enterprises. The scheme is implemented through Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) as the nodal agency at the national level, with State KVIC Directorates, State Khadi and Village Industries Boards (KVIBs), and District Industries Centres (DICs) as implementing agencies at the state and district levels. PMEGP merges the erstwhile Prime Minister's Rojgar Yojana (PMRY) and Rural Employment Generation Programme (REGP) to promote sustainable employment in rural and urban areas.
target_beneficiaries: individuals; self-help groups; institutions; MSME; entrepreneurs
eligibility: Any individual above 18 years of age is eligible. For setting up of project costing above Rs. 10 lakh in the manufacturing sector and above Rs. 5 lakh in the business/service sector, the beneficiary should possess at least VIII standard pass educational qualification. There is no ceiling on project cost for loans under the scheme. Existing units (under PMRY, REGP or any other scheme of Government of India or State Government) and the units that have already availed government subsidy under any other scheme of Government of India or State Government are not eligible.
benefits: The scheme provides a subsidy ranging from 15% to 35% of the project cost in the form of margin money, which is not repayable. The subsidy is higher for special categories (such as SC/ST/OBC/women/ex-servicemen/physically handicapped, NER, hill and border areas, etc.) and lower for general category beneficiaries. The remaining project cost is provided as a loan by banks. The subsidy is credited to the beneficiary's bank account after the project is verified and found viable. The scheme also provides training and technical support through KVIC and its affiliated institutions.
financial_support: Under PMEGP, the maximum cost of project is Rs. 50 lakh in the manufacturing sector and Rs. 20 lakh in the business/service sector. The subsidy (margin money) provided by the Government ranges from 15% to 35% of the project cost, depending on the beneficiary category and location. The balance amount of the project cost (i.e., project cost minus margin money) is provided as a loan by the Banks. The rate of interest on the loan is as per the prevailing norms of the respective banks. The margin money subsidy is released by the nodal banks in instalments and is credited to a special term deposit account of the beneficiary, which is not withdrawable until the lock-in period is over.
application_process: 1. The applicant must prepare a project report for the proposed micro-enterprise. 2. The applicant applies online through the PMEGP e-portal (https://www.kviconline.gov.in/pmegpeportal/pmegphome/index.jsp) or submits the application form offline to the concerned District Industries Centre (DIC) or KVIC/KVIB office. 3. The application is scrutinized by the concerned agency (DIC/KVIC/KVIB) for eligibility and viability. 4. Upon approval, the application is forwarded to the selected bank for sanction of loan. 5. The bank sanctions the loan and disburses the margin money subsidy (as per government norms) into a special term deposit account. 6. The beneficiary sets up the enterprise and starts operations. 7. The bank monitors the account and ensures compliance with scheme guidelines.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year.
implementing_agency: Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC)

overview: BioNEST Incubation is a scheme launched by BIRAC to foster the biotech innovation ecosystem by providing incubation space to startups and entrepreneurs. It supports the establishment of bio-incubators either as standalone entities or as part of academia, offering access to high-end instrumentation, mentorship, IP and technology management, legal support, resource mobilization, and networking platforms. The program aims to connect industry and academia, enable knowledge exchange, and establish efficient governance models for bioincubators. As of the latest data, BIRAC has supported 73 bio-incubators creating a cumulative area of 1,045,470 sq. ft. for budding entrepreneurs.
eligibility: Indian institutions including Universities/ Research Institutes/ Colleges/ Management Institutes/ Business Schools/ Business Incubators/Biotech-Parks/State government S & T bodies/ Biotech State Councils/Research Hospitals. Incorporation of an Indian legal entity* is recommended for Bioincubators, however it is not a must for the new applicants especially from Indian Institutions including Universities/ Research Institutes/Colleges/ Management Institutes/ Business Schools etc. *Private Company/ Not for profit Section 8 company/Trust/Society - The legal entities under the relevant Law of India having at least 51% Indian (Citizen) stakeholders (owners/partners/trustees/members/associates/directors etc. as applicable).
benefits: Incubation space for startups and entrepreneurs, connection between industry and academia for knowledge exchange, technical and business mentorship, enabling services for IP and Technology management, legal and contractual support, resource mobilization, networking platform, and establishment of an efficient governance model.
financial_support: The BIRAC funding for the BIRAC BioNEST Centres will be done in alignment with the Government’s umbrella scheme ‘Biotechnology Research Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development’ (Bio-RIDE). The funding support from BIRAC will be in the form of grant in aid for both Capital and Operational expenses and will be based on the need and scope of the project as adjudged by the BioNEST committee. Private entities/Institutions - Minimum contribution of 30% will be required for the total project cost by the applicant. Funding to Government entities shall be done as per the following categories: Government Academic Institutions (Centre/State -owned): Support shall be provided as Grant-in-aid; Central Government owned Section -8 company (ies): Support shall be provided as Grant-in-aid; State Government -Owned Section -8 company(ies) /Councils: Minimum contribution of 50% from the applicant is required (for applications under Category 4).
application_process: Step-1 Go to home page of BIRAC and click on the provided link. Step-2 Click on 'Click here to Register'. Step-3 Click on 'Click here to register/apply for BioNEST'. Step-4 The below shown page will open. Step-5 Read the instructions carefully and click on 'next' at the bottom of the page. Step-6 Register by filling in the details. Step-7 After registration, link for password reset will be sent over email. Reset the password to get the login credentials. Step-8 Click on to 'Login/Register'. Step-9 Login using the credentials. Step-10 After login, below shown page will open. Click on 'BioNEST'. Step-11 Click on 'Click here to submit new proposal'. Step-12 The below shown page will open. Kindly fill in details required. Step-13 Kindly fill in 'Basic information'. Step-14 Click on 'Proposal Details'. Step-15 After clicking on 'Proposal Details', below page will open. Download the proposal form by clicking on 'Click here to download format'. Fill the form and convert into PDF and upload. Step-16 Click on 'Project coordinator/lead' and fill in the details. Step-17 Click on Final submit.
implementing_agency: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), a Public Sector Enterprise, set up by Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India
contact_details: Dr. Chhaya Chauhan, Incharge-Incubation; Email: sped4.birac@nic.in

overview: The Credit Linked Capital Subsidy Scheme for Technology Upgradation (CLCSS) is a government initiative aimed at facilitating technology upgradation in Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) by providing an upfront capital subsidy of 15% on institutional finance of up to Rs 1 crore availed by them. The scheme supports the induction of well-established and improved technology in specified 51 sub-sectors/products, enabling MSEs to upgrade plant and machinery with state-of-the-art technology, with or without expansion. It also covers new MSEs that have set up facilities with appropriate eligible and proven technology duly approved under scheme guidelines.
eligibility: Micro and Small Enterprises (MSEs) that have availed institutional finance of up to Rs 1 crore for technology upgradation in the specified 51 sub-sectors/products, using well-established and improved technology duly approved under scheme guidelines, including new MSEs that have set up facilities with appropriate eligible and proven technology.
benefits: Upfront capital subsidy of 15% on eligible institutional finance (up to Rs 1 crore), technology upgradation with state-of-the-art machinery, improved productivity and competitiveness, support for both existing and new MSEs adopting approved technology.
financial_support: The scheme provides an upfront capital subsidy of 15% on institutional finance availed by MSEs, with a maximum eligible loan amount of Rs 1 crore, resulting in a maximum subsidy of Rs 15 lakh per unit. The subsidy is disbursed directly to the lending institution upon verification of technology upgradation and loan disbursement.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs 15 lakh
application_process: 1. Avail institutional finance (term loan) from a bank or financial institution for technology upgradation. 2. Ensure the technology is among the 51 approved sub-sectors/products under CLCSS. 3. Submit application through the lending institution with required documents. 4. Lending institution forwards the claim to the nodal agency. 5. Nodal agency verifies eligibility and approves subsidy. 6. Subsidy amount (15% of loan, up to Rs 15 lakh) is released to the lending institution, which adjusts it against the borrower's loan account.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME), Government of India

overview: ZED Certification (Zero Defect Zero Effect) is an extensive drive by the Ministry of MSME to create awareness amongst MSMEs about Zero Defect Zero Effect practices and motivate and incentivise them for ZED Certification while also encouraging them to become MSME Champions. The scheme aims to enhance MSME competitiveness, make them sustainable, and transform them into National and International Champions. It is implemented in two phases: Phase 1 for Manufacturing MSMEs with UDYAM Registration and Phase 2 for inclusion of Service Sector MSMEs. The scheme provides financial assistance/subsidy for obtaining ZED Certification at different levels (Bronze, Silver, Gold) and includes additional incentives like a joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- and support for handholding, technology upgradation, and surveillance assessments.
eligibility: All MSMEs registered with the UDYAM registration portal (of the MoMSME) will be eligible to participate in MSME Sustainable (ZED) Certification and avail related benefits/incentives. Any number of Units registered under one Udyam Registration can apply for subsidy under this Scheme. Each Unit (under one Udyam Registration) will need to apply for Certification separately to avail subsidy/benefits/incentives.
benefits: Joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- for ZED Certification (once, within 1 year of taking the ZED Pledge); Subsidy on cost of certification: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%; Additional subsidy of 10% for MSMEs owned by Women/SC/ST Entrepreneurs OR MSMEs in NER/Himalayan/LWE/Island territories/aspirational districts; Additional subsidy of 5% for MSMEs which are also a part of the SFURTI OR Micro & Small Enterprises - Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP) of the Ministry; Financial assistance for handholding support (up to Rs. 5 Lakhs per MSME: up to Rs. 2 Lakhs for handholding and up to Rs. 3 Lakhs for technology upgradation) with subsidy structure: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%, plus additional 10% for Women/SC/ST or special area MSMEs and 5% for SFURTI/MSE-CDP; Financial assistance for surveillance assessment: Silver Level: Rs. 10,000/-, Gold Level: Rs. 20,000/- with subsidy: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%; 100% subsidy for women-owned MSMEs on cost of certification (as per addendum dated 11.11.2023); Free MSME KAWACH (WASH Certification) for ZED registered MSMEs; Assistance for obtaining international certifications like CE for international market access.
financial_support: Financial assistance/subsidy is provided for obtaining ZED Certification at Bronze, Silver, and Gold levels. The cost of certification is: Bronze: Rs. 8,000, Silver: Rs. 32,000, Gold: Rs. 72,000 (as per original guidelines) or amended to Bronze: Rs. 10,000, Silver: Rs. 40,000, Gold: Rs. 90,000 (as per Amendment_ZED_guideline_2.0_17.9.2024.pdf). Subsidy on cost of certification: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%. Additional subsidy: 10% for MSMEs owned by Women/SC/ST Entrepreneurs OR MSMEs in NER/Himalayan/LWE/Island territories/aspirational districts. Additional subsidy: 5% for MSMEs which are also a part of the SFURTI OR Micro & Small Enterprises - Cluster Development Programme (MSE-CDP) of the Ministry. A limited-purpose joining reward of Rs. 10,000/- is offered to each MSME once they take the ZED Pledge, to be utilized within 1 year for ZED Certification (Bronze, Silver, Gold). For handholding support, a provision of up to Rs. 5 Lakhs per MSME is available: up to Rs. 2 Lakhs for handholding support and up to Rs. 3 Lakhs for technology upgradation, with subsidy structure: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%, plus additional 10% for Women/SC/ST or special area MSMEs and 5% for SFURTI/MSE-CDP. For surveillance assessment: Silver Level: Rs. 10,000/-, Gold Level: Rs. 20,000/-, with subsidy: Micro Enterprises: 80%, Small Enterprises: 60%, Medium Enterprises: 50%. Women-owned MSMEs are eligible for 100% subsidy on cost of certification (effective from 11.11.2023).
application_process: 1. Register on the ZED portal (https://zed.msme.gov.in/) by providing Udyam Registration Number and associated mobile number. 2. Validate Udyam registration through API. 3. Provide valid Email ID for receiving ZED ID, Password, and Pledge Certificate. 4. Take the ZED Pledge online. 5. After registration, login with ZED ID and password. 6. Apply for desired ZED Certification Level (Bronze, Silver, or Gold) through the dashboard. 7. Download the ZED MSME mobile application from Play Store (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.qci.zed.msme) or App Store. 8. Provide GST Number and TAN Number as applicable. 9. Opt to avail the Joining Reward of Rs. 10,000/- (if applicable), which will be deducted from the total cost of certification. 10. Proceed to payment of the MSME contribution (certification fee minus any joining reward applied). 11. Select payment option from the list and make payment via payment gateway. 12. After successful payment, submit Basic Information and Documents via mobile application: capture MSME Representative Selfie and Site Exterior Photographs, and upload required documents. 13. Assessor will verify the information and documents submitted. 14. In case of non-conformity, the applicant MSME will be given an opportunity to close the non-conformity(ies) within a defined time period (30 days for Bronze, 60 days for Silver/Gold surveillance, etc.). 15. After successful verification, the ZED Certificate and Assessment Report will be issued and sent to the applicant's email ID and made available on the applicant dashboard.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Quality Council of India (QCI)
contact_details: Email: zed.support@qcin.org | Phone: Mr. Shivam Chaubey: 9773770346 | Working days: Mon-Fri, 9:00 AM - 5:30 PM | Address: Ministry of MSME, Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Kartavya Bhawan - 03, Kartavya Path, New Delhi - 110001, India

overview: SFURTI (Scheme of Fund for Regeneration of Traditional Industries) is a programme of the Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises, Government of India, aimed at organizing traditional industries and artisans into collectives to enhance their competitiveness, ensure long-term sustainability, and improve artisanal income through infrastructure development, technology upgradation, training, product innovation, and market linkage support. The scheme focuses on Khadi, Village Industries, and Coir sectors, promoting collective business enterprises and convergence with public-private partnerships and e-commerce initiatives.
target_beneficiaries: traditional artisans; producers; SCs; STs; women; disadvantaged communities
eligibility: Any Implementing Agency (IA) interested in submitting a proposal under SFURTI Scheme shall first submit a Concept note along with necessary documents on the SFURTI portal. The IA must be registered as an Implementing Agency on the portal. Proposals are submitted online to Nodal Agencies (NAs), which include KVIC for Khadi and Village Industry clusters, Coir Board for Coir-based clusters, and other national-level institutions with sectoral expertise in Traditional Industries, competence in cluster/collective development, project management, and facilitation of market access, operating in the field for at least five years. The IA must authenticate the list of artisans, form a project-specific SPV as a 'for profit' entity, ensure land availability for CFC construction (owned or leased for 15+ years), and confirm IA/SPV's share of 10% (5% for NER and Hilly areas).
benefits: Support includes creation of Common Facility Centres (CFCs) and worksheds with IP-enabled CCTV cameras, procurement of machineries, working capital and raw material banks (RMBs), tools and technological upgradation (e.g., charkha upgradation, tool-kit distribution), warehousing facilities, training centers, skill development and capacity building, machine handling and maintenance training, institution development, exposure visits, market promotion initiatives, design and product development, participation in seminars/workshops on technology upgradation, brand building and promotion campaigns, new media marketing, e-Commerce initiatives, innovation, R&D initiatives, technological upgradation, and developing institutional linkages. Financial support includes working capital up to 20% of project cost for raw material procurement, direct wages, electricity and water costs, provided as a revolving fund for initial production cycle, recoupable from product sales.
financial_support: The scheme provides financial support for hard interventions including creation of Common Facility Centres (CFCs), worksheds, procurement of machineries, working capital and raw material banks (RMBs), tools and technological upgradation, warehousing, and training centers. Working Capital of up to 20% of project cost may be budgeted for raw material procurement, direct wages, electricity and water costs, provided as a revolving fund for the initial production cycle, to be recouped from product sales. Financial assistance is disbursed to Implementing Agencies (IAs) through Nodal Agencies (NAs) on recommendation of Technical Agencies (TAs) to ensure time-bound project completion.
application_process: 1. Register as an Implementing Agency on the SFURTI portal to create user ID and password. 2. Log in to the portal and download the Instruction/Guidelines for filling Concept Application and Scorecard. 3. Select 'Scorecard' from the dashboard. 4. Fill the basic details form and save. 5. Select the 'Edit' option to proceed after saving Cluster Details. 6. Upload the duly filled Concept Application Form (download format from dashboard if new applicant). 7. Fill details against each indicator, selecting options and uploading supporting evidence simultaneously before moving to the next indicator. 8. Upload supporting evidence for each indicator by choosing and uploading files (PDF, max 5 MB per indicator). 9. For indicators requiring multiple options, select multiple checkboxes as per instructions. 10. View the scorecard from the 'View Scorecard' tab. 11. Select the preferred Nodal Agency from the drop-down list and click the forward button to submit the scorecard with application and uploaded documents to the concerned Nodal Agency. 12. Upon forwarding, a submission intimation message is displayed; the scorecard can be printed if required.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

overview: The MSME Champions Scheme is a technology platform launched by the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises to empower MSMEs by providing support in finance, marketing, technology, and capacity building. It also disseminates information in eleven regional languages through 69 State control rooms nationwide. The scheme serves as a holistic platform for redressal, guidance, and handholding of MSMEs.
eligibility: MSMEs are eligible to benefit from the Champions portal as part of the Ministry's initiatives for redressal, guidance, and handholding.
benefits: Support in finance, marketing, technology, and capacity building; information dissemination in eleven regional languages through 69 State control rooms nationwide; redressal, guidance, and handholding for MSMEs.
financial_support: The Champions portal does not provide direct financial support but facilitates access to finance through guidance and handholding.
application_process: 1. Access the Champions portal through the Ministry's website. 2. Utilize the platform for redressal, guidance, and handholding. 3. Access information in eleven regional languages via State control rooms. 4. Seek support in finance, marketing, technology, and capacity building.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises

The MSME Incubation Scheme is designed to support early-stage startups and entrepreneurs by providing financial assistance, mentorship, and infrastructure to nurture innovative business ideas into viable enterprises. It aims to foster innovation, reduce the risk of failure for new ventures, and promote entrepreneurship among MSMEs by offering incubation support through approved incubators. The scheme focuses on technology-driven and high-potential ideas that can contribute to economic growth and job creation.
eligibility: The scheme is open to individuals, startups, and MSMEs with innovative business ideas that are technology-driven and have potential for scalability. Applicants must be registered as MSMEs or in the process of registration. The idea should be novel, feasible, and capable of generating employment and economic value. Preference may be given to ideas from SC/ST, women, differently-abled, and aspirational districts.
benefits: Financial support in the form of grant-in-aid for approved projects, access to incubation infrastructure, mentorship from industry experts, networking opportunities, assistance in IPR filing, market linkage support, and guidance on business planning and fundraising. The scheme also provides support for prototype development, technology validation, and commercialization readiness.
financial_support: Under the MSME Incubation Scheme, financial assistance is provided as a grant-in-aid to approved incubators, who in turn support the incubatees. The grant covers costs related to mentorship, infrastructure usage, prototype development, and other pre-incubation and incubation activities. The quantum of support is determined based on the project proposal and stage of the idea, with a focus on early validation and proof-of-concept.
application_process: 1. Identify an approved incubator under the MSME Incubation Scheme. 2. Submit a detailed project proposal including business idea, innovation quotient, market potential, and financial plan. 3. The incubator evaluates the proposal based on novelty, feasibility, and scalability. 4. Shortlisted applicants are invited for a pitch or interview. 5. Upon selection, a memorandum of understanding is signed between the incubator and the incubatee. 6. Financial support is disbursed in stages based on milestones achieved. 7. Progress is monitored through regular reporting and review by the incubator and MSME authorities.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

TREAD for Women is a scheme aimed at empowering women entrepreneurs by providing financial assistance, training, and support for setting up and expanding their enterprises. It focuses on promoting entrepreneurship among women, enhancing their skills, and facilitating access to credit and market linkages. The scheme is implemented through various channels including NGOs and government bodies to ensure reach and effectiveness.
eligibility: Women entrepreneurs above 18 years of age, engaged in or planning to set up micro and small enterprises in the non-farm sector, with preference given to those from SC/ST, OBC, minority, and differently abled communities.
benefits: Financial assistance in the form of grant up to 30% of project cost (maximum Rs. 10 lakhs), training and skill development support, market linkage assistance, and handholding for enterprise setup and expansion.
financial_support: The scheme provides a grant of up to 30% of the project cost, with a maximum ceiling of Rs. 10 lakhs per enterprise. The remaining 70% of the project cost is to be borne by the beneficiary or arranged through other means such as loans from financial institutions.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 10 Lakhs
application_process: 1. Identify a viable business idea and prepare a project report. 2. Approach the designated implementing agency (such as KVIC, Coir Board, or MSME-DFO). 3. Submit the application form along with project report and required documents. 4. Undergo evaluation and screening by the agency. 5. Upon approval, receive sanction letter and disbursement of grant in installments. 6. Utilize funds for setting up/expanding the enterprise as per the approved project plan. 7. Submit utilization reports and progress updates as required.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

overview: RKVY-RAFTAAR (Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana - Remunerative Approaches for Agriculture and Allied sector Rejuvenation) is a centrally sponsored scheme launched in 2017-18 to make farming a remunerative economic activity through strengthening the farmer's effort, risk mitigation and promoting agri-business entrepreneurship. It aims to promote agri-startups and agri-entrepreneurship by providing financial and technical support for innovation in agriculture and allied sectors. The scheme focuses on value addition, post-harvest management, and linking farmers to markets through agribusiness models.
eligibility: Eligible beneficiaries include agri-startups, agri-entrepreneurs, farmers, farmer producer organizations (FPOs), self-help groups (SHGs), cooperatives, NGOs, and MSMEs engaged in agriculture and allied sectors. Projects must be innovative, feasible, and have potential for scalability and impact on farmers' income. Preference is given to projects promoting value addition, post-harvest management, and market linkage.
benefits: Financial support in the form of grants for project implementation, technical guidance and mentorship, access to incubation and acceleration facilities, market linkage assistance, training and capacity building, and support for technology adoption and innovation in agriculture and allied sectors.
financial_support: Under RKVY-RAFTAAR, financial support is provided as grants to eligible entities for innovative agri-projects. The grant amount varies based on the project proposal and is sanctioned by the State Level Sanctioning Committee (SLSC). Funds are released in installments based on project milestones and utilization certificates. The scheme encourages private investment and co-funding from beneficiaries.
application_process: 1. Identify a viable agri-project idea aligned with RKVY-RAFTAAR objectives. 2. Prepare a detailed project report (DPR) including technical, financial, and economic feasibility. 3. Submit the application through the State Agriculture Department or designated nodal agency. 4. Application is screened by the State Level Sanctioning Committee (SLSC) for eligibility and merit. 5. Upon approval, grant is sanctioned and released in installments. 6. Project implementation begins with monitoring and evaluation by the implementing agency. 7. Submit utilization certificates and progress reports for subsequent fund releases.
deadlines: Applications are invited periodically by State Agriculture Departments; specific deadlines vary by state and are notified through official communications.
implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture, Cooperation & Farmers Welfare, Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare, Government of India

overview: The Seed Fund for Bio Startups (SEED Fund) is an equity-based funding scheme by BIRAC aimed at post-proof-of-concept (PoC) biotech startups to bridge the 'Valley of Death' and enable angel investment readiness. It provides capital assistance through selected BioNEST incubators, with funding deployed as equity or equity-linked instruments up to INR 30 lakhs per startup. The scheme supports early-stage validation and scaling by covering costs such as equipment, supplies, and hiring, and is implemented via a structured incubator partnership model.
target_beneficiaries: biotech startups; post-PoC startups; early-stage life sciences startups
eligibility: Incubator should have been supported through BIRAC’s BioNEST programme. Incubator should be operational for the last three years. Incubator should have in-house capacity in incubating & mentoring early stage Biotech/Life Sciences startups. Incubator should have established IP&TT facilitation services for startups. Incubator must have prior experience in management of early-stage funding schemes or other grants. Startups must be registered under Companies Act, 2013 and have at least 51% shareholders as Indian citizens (excluding OCI/PIO).
benefits: Equity or equity-linked funding up to INR 30 lakhs per startup; no management fees for incubators; structured investment and exit framework with 50% return retention by incubator and 50% restitution to BIRAC; access to incubator infrastructure, mentoring, and IP&TT support; monitoring and hand-holding support from incubators; half-yearly reporting to BIRAC for transparency and accountability.
financial_support: BIRAC provides grant-in-aid of INR 100–200 lakhs per cycle to selected BioNEST incubators for deployment in biotech startups. Each incubator can invest up to INR 30 lakhs per startup as equity or equity-linked instruments. The grant is parked in the incubator’s savings account under a separate account head and must be utilized within 36 months from the date of signing the SEED Fund Implementation Agreement (SIFA).
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 30 lakhs per startup
application_process: 1. BIRAC identifies and selects eligible BioNEST incubators through the BIRAC Incubator SEED Fund Committee (BISF Committee). 2. Selected incubators sign a SEED Fund Implementation Agreement (SIFA) with BIRAC. 3. BIRAC disburses grant-in-aid (INR 100–200 lakhs) to the incubator’s savings account under a separate head. 4. Incubator designs a selection mechanism to screen startups based on vesting requirements. 5. Incubator identifies Indian startups (registered under Companies Act, 2013, with ≥51% Indian citizen shareholders). 6. Incubator provides funding as equity/equity-linked instruments up to INR 30 lakhs per startup. 7. A legally binding agreement is signed between incubator and startup with terms for funding and investment. 8. Incubator monitors milestones and provides hand-holding support. 9. Incubator submits half-yearly implementation reports to BIRAC including Utilization Certificates (UC), Statement of Accounts (SOA), and project progress. 10. On exit, incubator retains 50% of gross return and restitutes 50% to BIRAC within 60 days.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round through selected incubators.
implementing_agency: Biotechnology Industry Research Assistance Council (BIRAC), a Public Sector Enterprise set up by Department of Biotechnology (DBT), Government of India
contact_details: Email: dkumar[at]birac[dot]nic[dot]in, tech03[at]birac[dot]nic[dot]in | Helpline: Not explicitly mentioned in evidence

overview: iDEX (Innovations for Defence Excellence) is a flagship initiative of the Ministry of Defence, Government of India, launched to foster innovation and technology development in defence and aerospace by engaging startups, MSMEs, innovators, and R&D institutes. It aims to create an ecosystem for indigenous defence solutions through grants, incubation, and industry collaboration. The scheme supports the development of cutting-edge technologies for the armed forces and promotes self-reliance in defence manufacturing under the 'Make in India' initiative.
eligibility: Startups, MSMEs, innovators, and R&D institutes registered in India are eligible to apply. Entities must be working on innovative defence and aerospace technologies that address the challenges articulated by the armed forces, Defence Public Sector Undertakings (DPSUs), and Ordnance Factories Board (OFB).
benefits: Financial support in the form of grants, access to defence testing infrastructure, mentorship from defence experts, opportunities for trials and evaluation by the armed forces, potential for induction into service, and facilitation of partnerships with defence PSUs and OFBs.
financial_support: iDEX provides grant funding of up to INR 1.5 Crore per project for proof of concept, prototype development, and trials. The funding is disbursed in milestones based on project progress and is managed through the Defence Innovation Organisation (DIO).
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 1.5 Crore per project
application_process: 1. Visit the iDEX portal and register as an innovator. 2. Review the problem statements released by the armed forces, DPSUs, or OFBs. 3. Submit a proposal addressing a specific problem statement. 4. Proposals are evaluated by the iDEX Evaluation Committee. 5. Shortlisted applicants are invited for presentations. 6. Selected projects are sanctioned funding and enter the development phase. 7. Progress is monitored through periodic reviews and milestone-based disbursement.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year against specific problem statements released periodically.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Defence, Government of India

overview: Mahila E-Haat is an online marketing platform launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development to support women entrepreneurs, self-help groups, and NGOs in showcasing and selling their products and services directly to buyers. The initiative aims to strengthen the socio-economic empowerment of women by leveraging technology for market access, financial inclusion, and entrepreneurship development. It provides a digital marketplace where women can list their handmade, homemade, and homegrown products without any cost.
eligibility: The platform is open to Indian women entrepreneurs, self-help groups, and NGOs engaged in manufacturing, production, or sale of products and services. There is no registration fee or cost to list products on the portal.
benefits: Women entrepreneurs can list their products for free on the Mahila E-Haat portal, gain access to a national market, interact directly with buyers, receive online payments, and benefit from promotional support. The platform facilitates transactions without charging any commission or fee to the sellers.
financial_support: Mahila E-Haat does not provide direct financial support, grants, loans, or subsidies. It is a free online marketplace that enables women to sell their products and earn income through direct sales to buyers.
application_process: 1. Visit the Mahila E-Haat portal at https://www.mahilahaat.gov.in/ 2. Click on 'Register as a Seller' 3. Fill in the required details including name, contact information, address, and bank account details 4. Upload product images and descriptions 5. Submit the registration form for verification 6. Once approved, list products and start selling to buyers across India.
deadlines: Rolling basis — registration and product listing are open throughout the year.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Women and Child Development

overview: Stand-Up India Scheme facilitates bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore to at least one Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST) borrower and at least one woman borrower per bank branch for setting up a greenfield enterprise. The enterprise may be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities, or trading sector. For non-individual enterprises, at least 51% shareholding and controlling stake must be held by an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. The scheme aims to promote entrepreneurship among underrepresented groups by providing financial support and handholding through designated agencies.
eligibility: Borrowers must be SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs above 18 years of age. Loans are for setting up greenfield enterprises. For non-individual enterprises, at least 51% of shareholding and controlling stake must be held by either an SC/ST or woman entrepreneur. The enterprise can be in manufacturing, services, agri-allied activities, or trading sector.
benefits: Access to bank loans between ₹10 lakh and ₹1 Crore, composite loan inclusive of term loan and working capital, promoter contribution of 15% margin money, security cover under Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL), handholding support through designated agencies, and facilitation of greenfield enterprise setup in manufacturing, services, agri-allied, or trading sectors.
financial_support: The scheme facilitates bank loans ranging from ₹10 lakh to ₹1 Crore per borrower. The loan is a composite loan inclusive of term loan and working capital. Promoters are required to contribute 15% as margin money. Security for the loan is provided under the Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Stand-Up India Loans (CGFSIL).
application_process: 1. Visit the Stand-Up India portal at https://standupmitra.in/. 2. Click on 'Apply Here' to begin the application process. 3. Approach a participating bank branch with your business proposal. 4. Ensure eligibility criteria are met (SC/ST/woman entrepreneur above 18 years, greenfield enterprise). 5. Submit required documents including identity proof, business plan, and project details. 6. Bank processes the loan application under the Stand-Up India Scheme. 7. Upon approval, loan is sanctioned and disbursed. 8. Utilize handholding support from designated Handholding Agencies (HHAs) if needed.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

overview: The IN-SPACe Seed Fund is a financial support initiative by the Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe) aimed at nurturing early-stage space startups in India. It provides seed funding to innovative ventures working on space technologies, applications, and services. The fund is designed to bridge the early-stage financing gap and promote indigenous innovation in the space sector. By supporting proof-of-concept and prototype development, the scheme aims to strengthen India's private space ecosystem.
eligibility: Indian-incorporated startups working on innovative space technologies, applications, or services with potential for commercialization and scalability. Preference is given to entities demonstrating technical feasibility, innovation, and alignment with national space goals.
benefits: Financial support for prototype development, proof-of-concept validation, and early-stage product development. Non-financial benefits include mentorship, technical guidance from ISRO and IN-SPACe experts, access to testing facilities, and regulatory facilitation.
financial_support: The scheme provides grant-based financial support to selected startups for early-stage activities such as prototype development, technology validation, and market discovery. The exact quantum of support per entity is determined based on project scope and milestones, subject to availability of funds and evaluation by the screening committee.
application_process: 1. Visit the official IN-SPACe website and navigate to the Seed Fund section. 2. Review eligibility criteria and guidelines. 3. Prepare a detailed project proposal including technical approach, innovation, commercialization plan, and budget. 4. Submit the application through the designated online portal or email as specified. 5. Applications are screened by an expert committee for technical merit, innovation, and feasibility. 6. Shortlisted candidates may be invited for presentations or interviews. 7. Final selection is based on overall evaluation and fund availability. 8. Selected startups receive grant approval and are onboarded for implementation.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year subject to fund availability.
implementing_agency: Indian National Space Promotion and Authorization Centre (IN-SPACe)

overview: The Start-up Village Entrepreneurship Program (SVEP) is a sub-scheme under the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM), approved in May 2015 and implemented since June 2011. It aims to help the rural poor come out of poverty by supporting them to set up and stabilize enterprises through financial assistance, training in business management and soft skills, and access to banking systems. The programme focuses on creating sustainable livelihoods and self-employment opportunities, particularly for youth and women, by building a local community cadre for enterprise promotion and integrating with other government schemes. SVEP operates through an IT-based workflow management system with web and mobile platforms to manage day-to-day support efficiently.
target_beneficiaries: rural poor; youth; women; self-help groups; community-based organisations
eligibility: The scheme targets the rural poor, particularly youth and women, who are part of self-help groups (SHGs) and village organizations (VOs) under DAY-NRLM. Beneficiaries are identified through community-based organisations and must be interested in setting up or stabilizing early-stage enterprises in rural areas. There is no explicit mention of income or educational criteria in the evidence, but the focus is on those lacking access to formal financial systems and dignified work opportunities.
benefits: Beneficiaries receive financial assistance for enterprise setup, training in business management and soft skills, mentoring through Community Resource Persons–Enterprise Promotion (CRP-EP), access to banking systems, and guidance for convergence with other government schemes. The programme also supports the creation of a local community cadre for enterprise promotion and provides hand-holding support until enterprises stabilize. Non-financial benefits include business development support, mentoring, and access to the Community Enterprise Fund (CEF) for credit linkage.
financial_support: Financial support is provided through the Community Enterprise Fund (CEF), which offers loans to rural enterprises for setup and stabilization. The fund is managed at the community level by community-based organisations, with disbursement decisions made locally. The mid-term report mentions tracking of CEF loan disbursement and repayment, indicating a revolving fund mechanism. However, specific quantum, interest rates, or repayment terms are not detailed in the evidence.
application_process: 1. Identify rural poor individuals through self-help groups (SHGs) and village organizations (VOs) under DAY-NRLM. 2. Community Resource Persons–Enterprise Promotion (CRP-EP) conduct entrepreneur registration and profile creation in six stages (general information, education/work, family, income, credit, expenditure profiles, and SVEP training details). 3. CRP-EP supports beneficiaries in preparing business plans and activity reports. 4. Business plans are reviewed and approved at the Block Resource Centre (BRC) level with input from mentors and functional experts. 5. Approved enterprises receive financial assistance via the Community Enterprise Fund (CEF) for setup and stabilization. 6. CRP-EP and BRC monitor enterprise performance through the Performance Tracking System (PTS) and provide hand-holding support for at least six months post-setup.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM), Ministry of Rural Development

overview: The DRDO Technology Development Fund (TDF) Scheme, under the 'Make in India' initiative, promotes the development of defence and dual-use systems, subsystems, components, or technologies by startups and MSMEs. It supports Indian Designed, Developed and Manufactured (IDDM) products to enhance self-reliance in defence technologies. The scheme aims to foster innovation and strengthen the defence R&D ecosystem by providing financial and technical support to eligible entities.
eligibility: The scheme is open to Indian industries, especially MSMEs and startups, for indigenisation of defence products, subsystems and components. The fund may also be utilised for developing new technologies as required by DRDO, Services and DPSUs. Industries can approach DRDO for support under the TDF scheme.
benefits: Financial support up to 90% of project cost, access to DRDO's technological and scientific support, testing facilities, technology transfer documents, handholding support, expert guidance from DRDO specialists, and opportunities for collaboration with DRDO laboratories on defence technology development.
financial_support: In TDF, a corpus fund of Rs. 100 Cr has been created to enable Indian industries, specially MSMEs, for indigenisation of the defence products, sub systems and components. The fund may also be utilised for developing new technologies as required by DRDO, Services and DPSUs. The cost of each project under TDF can be up to Rs 10 Cr. The industry can get funding up to 90% of the project cost.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to Rs 10 Cr per project (90% of project cost funded by TDF)
application_process: 1. Identify the technology development requirement aligned with TDF objectives. 2. Prepare a detailed project proposal including technical scope, budget, timeline, and expected outcomes. 3. Submit the proposal through the designated DRDO channel or portal. 4. Undergo technical and financial evaluation by DRDO experts. 5. Upon approval, sign the agreement and receive financial tranches as per project milestones. 6. Execute the project with periodic monitoring and reporting. 7. Submit final report and utilization certificate upon completion.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Defence Research & Development Organisation (DRDO), Ministry of Defence, Government of India

overview: Digital Agriculture Mission aims to establish a comprehensive farmer-centric digital and space-tech ecosystem to enhance transparency and efficiency in the execution of government programs while providing crucial support for evidence-based policy making for farmers’ welfare. It seeks to create a robust and holistic farmer-centric digital ecosystem in the country, improve transparency and efficiency in the implementation of government initiatives, support the government in making informed policy decisions, and promote public and private innovation and partnerships, encouraging Agri techs in India’s digital economy. The mission includes key components such as Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for Agriculture (Agristack, Krishi Decision Support System, Comprehensive Soil Fertility, and Profile Maps), Digital General Crop Estimation Survey (DGCES), support to Mahalanobis National Crop Forecasting Centre (MNCFC), and activities under the erstwhile NeGP-A scheme.
target_beneficiaries: farmers; agri-startups; research institutions; state governments; UT administrations
eligibility: States/UTs are eligible to submit proposals under the Digital Agriculture Mission, particularly for projects involving Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) for Agriculture, Digital General Crop Estimation Survey (DGCES), support to Mahalanobis National Crop Forecasting Centre (MNCFC), and IT initiatives for States, Academic & Research Institutions under the Mission. Proposals must include DPR, financial implications, clear timelines, and technology involved, and should preferably be submitted in the first quarter but not later than the second quarter of the Financial Year.
benefits: The mission provides support for evidence-based policy making, enhances transparency and efficiency in government program execution, promotes public-private partnerships in the agri-tech sector, enables real-time crop survey data collection through Digital Crop Survey (DCS), supports soil resource mapping at 1:10,000 scale, facilitates automated General Crop Estimation Survey (GCES) processes, and strengthens crop forecasting and yield estimation through integration with MNCFC. It also supports IT initiatives for states and academic institutions to develop scalable digital solutions using emerging technologies.
financial_support: Funds under the Digital Agriculture Mission are released to States/UTs in 4 equal tranches as per the latest guidelines of the Department of Expenditure (DoE), subject to submission of satisfactory status reports via PFMS. The mission provides financial assistance for Digital Crop Survey (DCS) pilots, manpower deployment for DCS/Help Desk/PMU, procurement of DCS-related infrastructure, and utilization of existing state funds for DCS activities. Academic and research institutes may receive 100% funds for building scalable digital solutions using advanced technologies aligned with Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) in Agriculture, subject to recommendation by a High-level Evaluation Committee.
application_process: 1. States/UTs should submit proposals under NeGP-A preferably in the first quarter but not later than the second quarter of the Financial Year. 2. The proposal must contain DPR, financial implication, clear timelines, and technology involved. 3. Proposals are examined based on use of latest technology, innovation, scalability, success, and viability of the proposed solution before proposing for pilot. 4. The States/UTs are advised to come up with proposals whose life-cycle from conception to implementation is preferably for one year with clear timelines of implementation. 5. Funds to the States/UTs are released in 4 equal tranches as per DoE guidelines, subject to submission of satisfactory status reports via PFMS. 6. Next installment is released after receipt of satisfactory status report from the States/UTs. 7. Pilot projects requiring basic infrastructure (hardware & manpower) have such costs borne by the concerned state. 8. The Centre has co-ownership of intellectual property created during the pilot, requiring States/UTs to share details and outcomes with the Centre. 9. States/UTs are required to share Cost-Benefit analysis with the Central Government as part of feedback project monitoring. 10. The sanctioned project must be completed within the stipulated time, extendable by up to one year at a time with prior approval, up to a maximum of two years; unspent amount must be surrendered if not completed in the extended year.
deadlines: Proposals should be submitted preferably in the first quarter but not later than the second quarter of the Financial Year.
implementing_agency: Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare (DA&FW), Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
contact_details: Email: us-it@nic.in | Phone: 011-23382926 (Under Secretary, IT Division)

overview: The ONGC Startup Fund is a corporate social responsibility (CSR) initiative by Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC) aimed at supporting startups through financial assistance and mentorship. It operates under ONGC's CSR policy to foster innovation and entrepreneurship, particularly in sectors aligned with energy and sustainability. The fund provides grants to eligible startups for project development and scaling, with a focus on inclusive growth and sustainable development goals.
eligibility: CSR Projects will be identified by the Corporate CSR Group, New Delhi and the CSR Departments at work centres across India, as per the criteria set in para 4.2 of this policy. It shall apply to all CSR Projects undertaken by ONGC as per liberal interpretation of activities listed in Schedule-VII of the Act, within the geographical limits of India alone, except for training of Indian sports personnel representing any State or Union territory at national level or India at international level and preferably towards the benefit of marginalized, disadvantaged, poor and deprived sections of the community and the environment and achievement of Sustainable Development Goals.
benefits: Financial support for CSR projects, capacity building of CSR personnel through engagement with International Organizations, transparent and efficient process via the ONGC CSR portal (www.ongccsr.co.in), monitoring and evaluation of projects, impact assessment for projects with outlay of Rs. 1 Cr. or more, and reporting through annual CSR report included in Board’s Report.
financial_support: The Board of ONGC will ensure that in each Financial Year (FY), at least two percent of the average net profit (calculated as per Section 198 of the Act) made during the three immediately preceding Financial Years is spent on CSR activities/projects. Funds are disbursed to implementing agencies fulfilling criteria laid down under the Rules, requiring valid CSR-1 registration number. Expenditure towards Impact Assessment will be booked towards CSR for that financial year, not exceeding two percent of total CSR expenditure or fifty lakh rupees, whichever is higher.
application_process: 1. Identify CSR project aligned with Schedule VII of Companies Act, 2013 2. Prepare Detailed Project Report (DPR) with need-based assessment, objectives, beneficiary details, timelines, budget break-up 3. Submit proposal through ONGC CSR portal (www.ongccsr.co.in) with required documents 4. ONGC CSR Committee reviews and recommends to Board for approval 5. Upon approval, sign agreement with Implementing Agency 6. Disburse funds as per implementation schedule 7. Monitor project progress through field visits, documentation, beneficiary feedback 8. Conduct impact assessment for projects ≥ Rs. 1 Cr. 9. Submit utilization report and fund utilization certificate 10. Report outcomes in annual CSR report
deadlines: Rolling basis — proposals can be submitted throughout the year as per the annual action plan formulated and recommended by the CSR Committee and submitted to the Board for approval.
implementing_agency: Corporate CSR Group, New Delhi and the CSR Departments at work centres across India

overview: The Startup Village Entrepreneurship Programme (SVEP) is a sub-component of the Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana - National Rural Livelihoods Mission (DAY-NRLM) aimed at promoting entrepreneurship in rural areas by supporting the creation and growth of village-level enterprises. It focuses on providing financial assistance, training, and handholding support to rural entrepreneurs, particularly from poor households, to establish sustainable businesses in non-farm sectors. The programme operates through Community Resource Persons - Enterprise Promotion (CRP-EPs) and Block Resource Centres (BRCs) to deliver end-to-end support for enterprise development.
target_beneficiaries: rural entrepreneurs; poor households; first-time entrepreneurs; SHG members; youth
eligibility: The programme targets rural poor households, particularly those registered under DAY-NRLM, including Self-Help Group (SHG) members and their families. Priority is given to first-time entrepreneurs from marginalized communities such as SC/ST, women, and youth. Enterprises must be located in rural blocks and engage in non-farm economic activities such as manufacturing, trading, or services.
benefits: Beneficiaries receive financial support in the form of grants and loans, along with comprehensive handholding support including business planning, market linkages, skill training, and mentorship. The programme facilitates access to banking services, helps in preparing detailed project reports, and provides post-sanction support for enterprise sustainability. Additionally, it promotes convergence with other government schemes for infrastructure, marketing, and technology adoption.
financial_support: Financial support under SVEP includes a grant of up to INR 1.5 lakh per enterprise for capital expenditure and a loan component of up to INR 1.0 lakh, which is repayable in installments. The grant is provided directly to the beneficiary, while the loan is facilitated through banking linkages. The total financial assistance per enterprise can go up to INR 2.5 lakh, combining both grant and loan elements, disbursed in stages based on project milestones.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to INR 1.5 lakh
application_process: 1. Identification of potential entrepreneurs by Community Resource Persons - Enterprise Promotion (CRP-EPs) at the village level. 2. Submission of a basic business idea and personal details by the entrepreneur. 3. Feasibility assessment and business plan development with support from CRP-EPs and Block Resource Centres (BRCs). 4. Submission of the Detailed Project Report (DPR) for appraisal. 5. Approval of the proposal by the Block Level Committee. 6. Disbursement of grant and facilitation of loan through bank linkage. 7. Post-sanction handholding support for enterprise establishment and growth.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year based on verification and availability of funds.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India

overview: The IOCL Startup Scheme is a PSU Support initiative by Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL) aimed at fostering innovation and supporting startups in the energy and allied sectors. The scheme provides a platform for startups to collaborate with IOCL on technology development, pilot projects, and commercialization of innovative solutions. It focuses on areas such as green energy, sustainable fuels, digital transformation, and operational efficiency. Startups are encouraged to engage with IOCL through defined channels for potential partnerships, funding, and market access.
eligibility: Startups must be registered entities under the Companies Act, 2013, LLP Act, 2008, or as a partnership firm. They should be working on innovative products, services, or processes in domains such as renewable energy, green hydrogen, biofuels, carbon capture, AI/ML for energy optimization, or sustainable mobility. Preference is given to startups with a working prototype or minimum viable product (MVP). The startup should not be more than 10 years old from the date of incorporation.
benefits: Selected startups gain access to IOCL’s technical expertise, testing facilities, and pilot plant infrastructure. Opportunities for proof-of-concept (PoC) development, joint development projects, and potential commercialization or procurement of solutions. Startups may receive mentorship from IOCL’s R&D and business teams, visibility through IOCL’s platforms, and support in scaling solutions. Financial support may be extended based on project milestones and mutual agreement.
financial_support: Financial support under the IOCL Startup Scheme is provided on a case-to-case basis, typically as grant funding, seed investment, or milestone-based disbursements for joint development projects. The quantum of support depends on the scope, technology readiness level, and commercial potential of the proposed solution. IOCL may also facilitate access to other funding mechanisms or connect startups with investors. Exact financial terms are negotiated post-selection based on the project proposal.
application_process: 1. Visit the official IOCL website and navigate to the Startup Scheme section. 2. Review the eligibility criteria and focus areas. 3. Prepare a detailed proposal including problem statement, solution, technology readiness, team details, and potential impact. 4. Submit the application via the designated online portal or email address as specified. 5. Applications are screened by IOCL’s innovation and R&D team. 6. Shortlisted startups are invited for presentations or discussions. 7. Final selection is made based on innovation, feasibility, and alignment with IOCL’s strategic goals. 8. Selected startups enter into a memorandum of understanding (MoU) or project agreement with IOCL.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year.
implementing_agency: Indian Oil Corporation Limited (IOCL)

overview: The BHEL Innovation Challenge is a scheme under PSU Support aimed at fostering innovation and technological development. It invites startups, MSMEs, and innovators to submit solutions addressing specific problem statements related to BHEL's core sectors such as power, industry, transportation, defence, and renewable energy. The challenge provides a platform for participants to showcase their innovations, with potential opportunities for collaboration, pilot projects, or commercialization with BHEL. It aligns with BHEL's commitment to R&D, indigenisation, and supporting the Make in India initiative.
eligibility: The scheme is open to startups, MSMEs, individual innovators, and entities registered in India. Participants must submit innovative solutions relevant to BHEL's operational domains such as power generation, transmission, transportation, defence, aerospace, renewable energy, or industrial systems. Solutions should be at a stage of development suitable for demonstration, pilot testing, or potential integration with BHEL's products or services.
benefits: Selected participants gain recognition from BHEL, opportunities for technical collaboration, potential pilot projects or proof-of-concept development, access to BHEL's expertise and infrastructure, and possible pathways for commercialization or long-term partnership. The challenge also offers visibility within BHEL's network and support for scaling innovations through mentorship and guidance.
financial_support: The BHEL Innovation Challenge primarily offers non-financial support such as mentorship, technical guidance, access to BHEL's facilities, and collaboration opportunities. There is no explicit mention of direct financial grants, seed funding, or prize money in the provided evidence. Any financial support would be contingent on post-selection agreements for pilot projects or commercialization.
application_process: 1. Visit the official BHEL website or designated innovation portal. 2. Review the problem statements or challenge themes released by BHEL. 3. Prepare and submit a detailed proposal outlining the innovative solution, its relevance, feasibility, and potential impact. 4. Submit the application through the specified online or offline channel. 5. Await evaluation by BHEL's expert panel. 6. Shortlisted candidates may be invited for presentations or discussions. 7. Final selection leads to announcement of winners and initiation of collaboration discussions.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL)
