Pradhan Mantri Matsya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) is a flagship scheme launched by the Government of India under the Aatmanirbhar Bharat Package to bring about holistic development of the fisheries sector. It aims to enhance fish production and productivity, modernize the value chain, ensure socio-economic welfare of fishers and fish farmers, and promote sustainable and responsible fisheries practices. The scheme focuses on infrastructure development, technology infusion, financial inclusion, and entrepreneurship-led models to transform the sector and achieve targets such as doubling fish production and exports by 2024-25.
target_beneficiaries: fishers; fish farmers; entrepreneurs; coastal communities; women; SC/ST; MSME; startups
eligibility: Eligible beneficiaries include fishers, fish farmers, fish vendors, fish workers, SC/ST, women, entrepreneurs, startups, self-help groups, cooperatives, NGOs, private companies, state governments, union territories, and other entities engaged in fisheries and aquaculture activities, subject to state-specific guidelines and implementation through District Fisheries Officers.
benefits: Financial assistance in the form of subsidies and grants for activities such as construction of ponds, cages, hatcheries, biofloc units, ice plants, cold storage, fish kiosks, value addition units, aquaculture in saline/alkaline lands, seaweed cultivation, ornamental fisheries, integrated aquaparks, nucleus breeding centres, fishing vessel upgradation, bio-toilets, safety kits, livelihood and nutritional support during fishing bans, training and skill development, technology adoption (RAS, biofloc, aquaponics, IMTA), market linkage, branding, and export promotion.
financial_support: Financial support is provided as central subsidy ranging from 40% to 60% of the project cost for general category beneficiaries, and 50% to 70% for SC/ST, women, and beneficiaries from North Eastern and Himalayan states, implemented through the Centrally Sponsored Scheme mode with funds released to State Implementing Agencies based on approved Detailed Project Reports (DPRs).
application_process: 1. Identify the activity and prepare a Detailed Project Report (DPR) as per guidelines. 2. Submit the DPR to the District Fisheries Officer (DFO) of the respective district. 3. DFO forwards the proposal to the State Fisheries Department for technical and financial appraisal. 4. State Level Sanctioning Committee (SLSC) approves the proposal. 5. Approved proposal is sent to the Department of Fisheries, Government of India for final approval and fund release. 6. Funds are released to the State Implementing Agency in installments based on physical and financial progress. 7. Beneficiary implements the project and submits utilization certificates and completion reports.
deadlines: Rolling basis — proposals can be submitted throughout the year as per State/UT-specific windows aligned with financial year cycles.
implementing_agency: Department of Fisheries, Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, Government of India

overview: Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY) is a flagship skill development scheme of the Government of India implemented by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE). It aims to enable a large number of Indian youth to take up industry-relevant skill training that will help them in securing a better livelihood. The scheme focuses on Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL), short-term training, and special projects to address skill gaps across various sectors.
target_beneficiaries: youth; unemployed; school/college dropouts; women; SC/ST; persons with disability
eligibility: Indian nationals who are unemployed, school/college dropouts, or seeking to upgrade their skills are eligible. There is no minimum or maximum age limit, though preference is given to youth between 15-45 years. Candidates must possess valid identity proof such as Aadhaar, Voter ID, or PAN. For Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL), individuals with prior work experience or informal learning in a specific skill area can apply for assessment and certification.
benefits: Free skill training in various job roles aligned with industry standards; monetary reward upon successful completion and certification; placement assistance through Rozgar Melas and industry linkages; Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) to certify existing skills; access to entrepreneurship development modules; post-placement tracking and support; training delivered through accredited training centers (TCs) and training providers (TPs).
financial_support: The scheme provides 100% funding for training and assessment costs directly to the training providers. Upon successful completion of training and assessment, candidates receive a monetary reward (average INR 8,000–10,000 per candidate) which is transferred directly to their bank accounts. There is no loan component; the entire cost is borne by the Government of India as a subsidy.
grant_amount_per_entity: Average monetary reward of INR 8,000–10,000 per successfully certified candidate
application_process: 1. Visit the official PMKVY website or contact a nearby PMKVY-affiliated training center. 2. Select a job role from the available list of Qualification Packs (QPs) under the National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF). 3. Enroll in the training program by submitting required documents. 4. Attend the training sessions as per the schedule. 5. Undergo assessment by an affiliated assessment agency. 6. Upon passing, receive certification and monetary reward directly into the bank account.
deadlines: Rolling basis — admissions and training batches are conducted throughout the year based on training center availability.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE), Government of India

overview: FAME India Scheme (Faster Adoption and Manufacturing of Hybrid and Electric Vehicles) is a Government of India initiative launched in 2015 to promote the adoption of electric and hybrid vehicles through financial incentives and support for charging infrastructure. The scheme is implemented in phases, with FAME-I operational from 2015 to 2019 and FAME-II launched in April 2019 with a total outlay of ₹10,000 crores for a period of three years, extendable. It aims to accelerate the uptake of environmentally friendly vehicles by providing upfront incentives to buyers and support for charging infrastructure development.
target_beneficiaries: buyers of electric and hybrid vehicles; manufacturers; service providers; institutional buyers; fleet operators
eligibility: Eligibility varies by vehicle segment and buyer type. For individual buyers, eligibility is based on the purchase of approved electric two-wheelers, three-wheelers, four-wheelers (including passenger cars and light commercial vehicles), and buses that meet specified technical standards and are listed on the FAME portal. Manufacturers must have their models tested and approved by designated agencies (like ARAI) and registered on the FAME portal. Institutional buyers and fleet operators can also avail incentives for qualifying vehicles used for public transport, shared mobility, or commercial purposes.
benefits: Financial incentives are provided at the point of sale to reduce the upfront cost of electric vehicles. The incentive amount varies by vehicle category: up to ₹15,000 per kWh for two-wheelers (capped at 40% of vehicle cost), up to ₹10,000 per kWh for three-wheelers, up to ₹10,000 per kWh for four-wheelers (capped at ₹1.5 lakh for cars and ₹1 lakh for LCVs), and up to ₹20,000 per kWh for buses. Additionally, the scheme supports the installation of charging infrastructure through subsidies for setting up public, semi-public, and private charging stations.
financial_support: Under FAME-II, a total financial outlay of ₹10,000 crores has been allocated for a period of three years (extendable). Of this, ₹8,596 crores is allocated for demand incentives to support the purchase of electric vehicles, ₹1,000 crores for charging infrastructure, ₹200 crores for administrative expenses, and ₹204 crores for publicity, monitoring, and evaluation. The incentives are transferred directly to the manufacturers or sellers, who then pass on the benefit to the end consumers by reducing the vehicle's purchase price.
grant_amount_per_entity: Incentive varies by vehicle type: up to ₹15,000/kWh for two-wheelers (max 40% of cost), ₹10,000/kWh for three- and four-wheelers, and ₹20,000/kWh for buses, with specific caps per vehicle category (e.g., ₹1.5 lakh for electric cars, ₹1 lakh for LCVs, and proportionally higher for buses based on battery size).
application_process: 1. Manufacturers must get their electric vehicle models tested and approved by designated testing agencies (e.g., ARAI, ICAT). 2. Approved models are registered on the FAME portal by the manufacturer. 3. Buyers purchase the eligible vehicle from a registered dealer. 4. The dealer or manufacturer uploads the sale details on the FAME portal. 5. The Department of Heavy Industries verifies the claim and disburses the incentive amount to the manufacturer/dealer. 6. The end consumer benefits from the reduced purchase price at the point of sale.
deadlines: FAME-II was initially launched for a period of three years from April 1, 2019, and has been extended multiple times; currently operational until further notice as per government notifications.
implementing_agency: Department of Heavy Industries, Ministry of Heavy Industries and Public Enterprises

NTPC Limited is India's largest integrated power utility, established in 1975, with a diversified portfolio spanning thermal, hydro, nuclear, and renewable energy sources. The company is committed to sustainable power generation, aiming for 60 GW of renewable capacity by 2032, and operates as a Maharatna CPSE under the Ministry of Power, Government of India. NTPC focuses on innovation, sustainability, and community development through initiatives like green hydrogen, waste-to-energy, and battery storage, while maintaining strong financial and operational performance.
eligibility: As a Maharatna Central Public Sector Enterprise under the Ministry of Power, Government of India, NTPC Limited operates as a government company with the President of India holding a majority stake. The company's operations and initiatives are accessible to stakeholders across India, including communities near project sites, employees, investors, and partners in joint ventures and subsidiaries.
benefits: NTPC provides reliable and affordable power to millions, contributes to national energy security, drives innovation in clean technologies, creates employment opportunities, supports community development through CSR spending (₹363 Cr in FY 2024-25), ensures environmental sustainability via 100% ash utilisation and renewable energy expansion, and delivers strong financial returns to shareholders. The company also offers a progressive work culture, recognized as a 'Great Place to Work' for 19 consecutive years and a Top Employer 2025.
financial_support: NTPC mobilizes funds through equity, debt, and internal accruals for capital expenditure. It has raised capital via IPO and follow-on public offers, with the Government of India reducing its stake from 89.5% to 51.10% over time to encourage public participation. The company accesses domestic and international markets for loans, including rupee term loans (e.g., ₹500 Cr by NTPC Mining Limited and ₹1000 Cr by NTPC) and external commercial borrowings, subject to financial covenants like a 3:1 total liabilities to net worth ratio and EBITDA to interest expense ratio of at least 1.75:1.
application_process: NTPC Limited is a government company and Maharatna CPSE; there is no application process for beneficiaries to join the company as a scheme. Stakeholders engage through investment (equity/debt), procurement, partnerships, or community initiatives. For financial instruments like term loans, banks/FIs submit bids in response to RFPs (e.g., NTPC Mining Limited's RFP for ₹500 Cr), followed by evaluation, sanction, and disbursement per agreed terms.
deadlines: Ongoing operations and initiatives; no fixed deadline for general engagement. Specific financial bids (e.g., term loans) have defined submission dates (e.g., 16-02-2026 by 5:00 PM for NML RFP).
implementing_agency: Ministry of Power, Government of India
contact_details: Email: csntpc@ntpc.co.in | Phone: (011) 24360959 | Address: NTPC Limited, SCOPE Complex, 7 Institutional Area, Lodi Road, New Delhi-110 003

PM-DAKSH (Pradhan Mantri Dakshta Aur Kushalta Sampann Hitgrahi) is a Central Sector Scheme launched by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment to provide skill development training to marginalized groups including Scheduled Castes, Other Backward Classes, Safai Karamcharis, and Denotified Tribes. The scheme aims to enhance employability and income generation through short-term and long-term skill training programs aligned with industry requirements. It focuses on uplifting socio-economically disadvantaged communities by providing access to quality skills training and placement support.
target_beneficiaries: Scheduled Castes (SC); Other Backward Classes (OBC); Safai Karamcharis (including waste pickers); Denotified Tribes (DNT); Sem Nomadic Tribes; Manual Scavengers
eligibility: The scheme targets individuals belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Other Backward Classes (OBC), Safai Karamcharis (including waste pickers), Denotified Tribes (DNT), Sem Nomadic Tribes, and Manual Scavengers. Beneficiaries must be Indian citizens and fall within the age group of 18-45 years (relaxable for certain categories). Priority is given to those from economically weaker sections within these communities.
benefits: Beneficiaries receive free skill development training in sectors such as IT, healthcare, hospitality, construction, and textiles, aligned with National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF). Training includes provision of free course material, stipend during training (where applicable), and post-training placement assistance or support for self-employment. Successful trainees receive nationally recognized certificates upon completion.
financial_support: The scheme provides 100% financial support for training costs, including course fees, training material, and stipends (where applicable), directly paid to empaneled training providers. There is no direct cash transfer to beneficiaries; instead, the government bears the entire cost of training through reimbursement to accredited institutions based on successful completion and placement outcomes.
application_process: 1. Visit the official PM-DAKSH portal at https://pmdaksh.dosje.gov.in 2. Click on the 'Beneficiary Registration' or 'Apply Now' section 3. Fill in the online application form with personal, educational, and category details 4. Upload required documents such as caste certificate, identity proof, and address proof 5. Submit the application and await verification 6. Upon approval, select an approved training center and course 7. Attend training as per schedule 8. Complete assessment and receive certification 9. Avail placement support or self-employment assistance
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year subject to availability of training slots.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India

PM-SETU (Pradhan Mantri Scheme for Enhancing Training and Upskilling) is a skill development initiative by the Directorate General of Training (DGT) under the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship. The scheme aims to enhance the employability of youth by providing industry-aligned vocational training through a hub-spoke model involving Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) and National Skill Training Institutes (NSTIs). It focuses on upgrading training infrastructure, improving training quality, and strengthening industry linkages to meet evolving labor market demands.
target_beneficiaries: youth; ITI trainees; vocational trainees; MSME; industry workers
eligibility: The scheme targets Industrial Training Institutes (ITIs) and National Skill Training Institutes (NSTIs) under the administrative and financial control of State Governments or Union Territory Administrations. Eligible entities include affiliated ITIs (Government and Private) and NSTIs under DGT’s direct control that are willing to participate in hub-and-spoke consortia for training upgradation and industry linkage activities.
benefits: Benefits include improved training quality through industry-aligned curricula, enhanced employability of trainees via exposure to latest technologies, increased placement opportunities, strengthened industry linkages, upgraded infrastructure in ITIs and NSTIs, access to blended learning resources, and certification under NSQF-compliant trades. Trainees gain hands-on skills through industry training components, and instructors receive upskilling under CITS. The scheme also supports data-driven grading of ITIs and strengthens apprenticeship training through industry clusters.
financial_support: Financial support is provided through a Program-for-Results (PforR) financing instrument from the World Bank, with a proposed loan amount of US$900 million. The central government allocates funds to states/UTs based on achievement of disbursement-linked indicators (DLIs). Funding supports ITI upgradation, NSTI transformation, industry partnership facilitation, and system-level reforms. The financial mechanism involves reimbursement against verified results, with central and state sharing as per scheme design (e.g., 75% central, 25% state in similar schemes like LWE).
application_process: 1. States/UTs submit proposals for participation in the PM-SETU program through the Directorate General of Training (DGT). 2. DGT evaluates proposals based on readiness, infrastructure, and commitment to reforms. 3. Selected states/UTs sign a Program-for-Results (PforR) agreement with the Government of India and World Bank. 4. States/UTs identify and nominate ITIs and NSTIs for inclusion in hub-and-spoke consortia (HSICs). 5. HSICs develop and implement upgrading plans, including industry partnerships and curriculum updates. 6. NSTIs develop five-year Strategic Investment Plans (SIPs) for transformation into world-class institutes. 7. Progress is monitored via disbursement-linked indicators (DLIs) related to ITI governance, trainee outcomes, and industry linkage. 8. Funds are released to states/UTs upon verification of achieved results.
deadlines: Rolling basis — proposals are accepted and evaluated periodically as per program cycles.
implementing_agency: Directorate General of Training (DGT), Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship

overview: The Export Promotion Capital Goods (EPCG) Scheme facilitates the import of capital goods for pre-production, production, and post-production at zero customs duty to enhance India's manufacturing competitiveness. It allows import of capital goods for producing quality goods and services, with exemptions from IGST and Compensation Cess up to 31.03.2020. The scheme covers manufacturer exporters, merchant exporters tied to supporting manufacturers, and service providers.
target_beneficiaries: manufacturer exporters; merchant exporters; service providers
eligibility: To apply for an EPCG scheme, an IEC is required. Other pre-requisites as mentioned in Chapter 5 of Foreign Trade Policy and Handbook of Procedures may be referred.
benefits: Import of capital goods for pre-production, production, and post-production at zero customs duty. Capital goods imported under EPCG for physical exports are exempt from IGST and Compensation Cess up to 31.03.2020. Alternatively, exporters may procure capital goods from the domestic market in accordance with paragraph 5.07 of FTP.
financial_support: The scheme provides zero customs duty on import of capital goods for pre-production, production, and post-production. Capital goods imported under EPCG for physical exports are exempt from IGST and Compensation Cess up to 31.03.2020. Exporters may also procure capital goods from the domestic market per paragraph 5.07 of FTP.
application_process: 1. Ensure you have an IEC (Importer Exporter Code). 2. Refer to Chapter 5 of Foreign Trade Policy and Handbook of Procedures for pre-requisites. 3. Apply for EPCG via ANF-5A form. 4. Access services such as amendment, invalidation, revalidation, clubbing, closure, installation certificate, license status, EO/block extension, authorisation transmission, manual EODC status update, EPCG committee request, review application, transfer of authorisation, annual reporting of EO fulfilment, and help/FAQs through the DGFT portal.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT), Ministry of Commerce and Industry

The SMILE Scheme (Support for Marginalized Individuals for Livelihood and Enterprise) is a Central Sector Scheme under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India, aimed at providing rehabilitation, livelihood support, and enterprise development to marginalized groups including transgender persons and persons engaged in begging. It focuses on comprehensive welfare through shelter, skill development, medical support, and financial assistance for self-employment and entrepreneurship.
target_beneficiaries: transgender persons; persons engaged in begging; marginalized individuals; SC/ST; OBC
eligibility: The scheme covers transgender persons and persons engaged in begging who are identified through surveys conducted by State Governments/UT Administrations or implementing agencies. Beneficiaries must be Indian citizens and belong to the target groups as defined under the scheme. There is no income ceiling specified, but priority is given to those living in extreme poverty or without sustainable livelihood.
benefits: Beneficiaries receive financial assistance for setting up self-employment ventures, access to shelter homes with food and sanitation facilities, skill development training, medical health cards, counselling services, legal aid for identity documentation, and support for education and scholarships. The scheme also provides seed capital and margin money assistance for entrepreneurial activities.
financial_support: Under the SMILE Scheme, financial support is provided in the form of subsidies and seed capital for self-employment and enterprise development. Eligible individuals can receive one-time financial assistance for income-generating activities, with specific amounts varying based on the nature of the venture and State-level implementation guidelines. The support is disbursed through implementing agencies after verification of business plans and training completion.
application_process: 1. Identification of beneficiaries through surveys conducted by State Governments/UT Administrations or NGOs. 2. Verification of eligibility and enrollment in the scheme. 3. Provision of temporary shelter and basic amenities in shelter homes. 4. Enrollment in skill development and vocational training programs. 5. Submission of business plan for self-employment or enterprise. 6. Assessment and approval of financial assistance by the implementing agency. 7. Disbursement of seed capital or subsidy for setting up the venture. 8. Post-disbursement monitoring and mentorship support.
deadlines: Rolling basis — identification and enrollment are ongoing through periodic surveys.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India

overview: PM E-DRIVE is a Government of India scheme launched by the Ministry of Heavy Industries to promote electric mobility through demand incentives, charging infrastructure, and manufacturing upgrades. It provides financial support for electric two-wheelers, three-wheelers, e-ambulances, e-trucks, e-buses, and EV public charging stations, with a total outlay of ₹10,900 crore. The scheme aims to incentivize the adoption of approximately 28.26 lakh electric vehicles and related infrastructure over its implementation period.
target_beneficiaries: individual buyers; government departments; public transport agencies; OEMs; charging infrastructure providers
eligibility: Vehicles must be registered under CMVR, 1989 and comply with PM E-DRIVE eligibility criteria. All e-2W/e-3W must be manufactured and registered within the validity period of PM E-DRIVE certificate. For e-trucks, incentive is provided only against furnishing scrapping certificate issued by MoRTH authorised registered vehicle scrapping facility(ies) (RVSF) for ICE trucks of equal or higher gross vehicle weight (GVW). Privately or corporately owned registered e-2Ws are eligible in addition to commercial use. e-3Ws (e-rickshaws & e-carts) are eligible solely for commercial use. e-Ambulance eligibility criteria are being developed in consultation with MoHFW.
benefits: Demand incentive in the form of upfront reduced purchase price, reimbursed to OEMs by MHI. Financial support for EV public charging stations, battery swapping stations, and battery charging stations. Upgradation of testing agencies under MHI. Comprehensive warranty requirements for vehicles (e.g., 5 years or 5,00,000 km for e-truck battery). Access to e-Voucher system for EV customers to avail demand incentive at time of purchase.
financial_support: Demand incentive is the lowest of: (a) ₹5,000 × battery capacity in kWh, (b) 10% of ex-factory price (excluding trailer for e-trucks), (c) Maximum incentive based on GVW. For e-2W/e-3W registered in FY 2024-25: ₹5,000 per kWh; for FY 2025-26: ₹2,500 per kWh. For e-buses: ₹10,000 per kWh. Subsidy for EVPCS is calculated as a percentage of benchmark costs or actual cost, whichever is lower, with 100% on upstream infrastructure and EVSE for Category A locations, 80% upstream and 70% EVSE for Category B, and 80% upstream for Category C and D (BSS/BCS).
grant_amount_per_entity: Varies by vehicle segment: e-2W up to ₹10,000 per vehicle (FY 2024-25) or ₹5,000 (FY 2025-26); e-rickshaw/e-cart up to ₹25,000 per vehicle (FY 2024-25) or ₹12,500 (FY 2025-26); e-3W L5 up to ₹50,000 per vehicle (FY 2024-25) or ₹25,000 (FY 2025-26); e-bus up to ₹35,00,000 per vehicle (standard bus); e-truck up to ₹9.3 lakh per vehicle (GVW >35 & <=55 tonnes).
application_process: 1. OEMs apply for registration with MHI by submitting application form, company registration certificate, trade license, GST registration, PAN card, testing/homologation certificate, pre-registration of EV model, MOA/AOA, proof of sales/service network, and photographs of type-approved vehicle. 2. After MHI approval, OEMs upload production data of EVs for which they intend to claim incentives. 3. At time of sale, OEMs must deduct incentive after all taxes and ensure invoice amount is charged only from dealer. 4. OEMs submit reimbursement claims weekly but not later than 120 days from sale of vehicle at dealer end, accompanied by an undertaking on company letterhead. 5. Claims are submitted online via the PM E-DRIVE portal. 6. For e-trucks, buyers must furnish Certificate of Deposit (CD) from MoRTH-authorised RVSF for ICE trucks of equal or higher GVW; dealer verifies CD authenticity via portal. 7. For EVPCS, eligible entities appoint nodal agency to aggregate demand, submit proposal via online portal specifying locations, number of chargers, vehicle segments, charger capacities, subsidy requested, and implementation mechanism. 8. MHI reviews and approves proposal with modifications if needed. 9. Upon approval, nodal agency/CPO initiates procurement, applies for electricity connections, and requests first tranche (70% of eligible subsidy) after submitting undertaking on demand notes, PMP compliance, and charging standards. 10. After commissioning and energization, second tranche (30%) is released after submitting undertaking on successful commissioning, PMP compliance, and onboarding on National Unified Hub.
deadlines: The scheme is implemented from 1st October 2024 to 31st March 2026, with further extension: terminal date for registered e-2Ws is 31st July 2026; for registered e-3Ws (e-rickshaws & e-carts) is 31st March 2028; e-3W (L5) closed on 26-12-2025. Claims must be submitted within 120 days of sale of vehicle.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Heavy Industries, Government of India

overview: Pradhan Mantri Anusuchit Jaati Abhyuday Yojana (PM-AJAY) is a Centrally Sponsored Scheme launched by the Government of India to promote socio-economic development of Scheduled Castes (SCs) through integrated development of SC-dominated villages, income generation, skill development, and infrastructure support. The scheme merges three earlier schemes—Special Central Assistance to Scheduled Castes Sub Plan (SCA to SCSP), Babu Jagjivan Ram Chhatrawas Yojana (BJRCY), and Pradhan Mantri Adarsh Gram Yojana (PMAGY)—into a single framework for better convergence and optimal utilization of resources. It focuses on reducing poverty, improving socio-economic indicators, and increasing literacy and enrolment of SCs in educational institutions by providing residential facilities and skill development opportunities.
target_beneficiaries: Scheduled Castes (SC); SC communities; SC dominated villages; SC students; SC families below poverty line
geographic_scope: Pan-India (implemented in 28 States/UTs; not covered: Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Meghalaya, Mizoram, A&N Islands, Dadra Nagar Haveli, Daman & Diu, Ladakh, Lakshadweep)
eligibility: Scheduled Castes persons living below the poverty line are eligible for benefits under Income Generating Schemes and Skill Development Programmes. For Infrastructure Development, villages having 50% or more SC population are eligible for grants under the Scheme. Guidelines from the erstwhile Planning Commission and procedures laid down by the Ministry of Rural Development for selecting beneficiaries through Panchayati Raj institutions may be adopted for defining poverty line and selecting SC families below poverty line.
benefits: Benefits include access to basic services in Adarsh Grams, grants-in-aid for livelihood projects, skill development training, construction/repair of hostels for SC students, financial assistance for income-generating activities (subsidy of Rs 10,000 per beneficiary or 50% of loan), infrastructure development in SC-majority villages, monitoring and evaluation support, and convergence with other central/state schemes for holistic development.
financial_support: Financial support is provided as 100% central funding under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme. Under the Grants-in-Aid component, a subsidy of Rs 10,000 per beneficiary or 50% of the loan amount is provided for income-generating schemes. Funds are released to State/UT governments based on notional allocation, SC population weightage (50%), and State SCSP to Annual Plan ratio (50%). Up to 50% of funds are allocated for Adarsh Gram (gap-filling), up to 5% for Administration, Monitoring and Evaluation, up to 2% for Hostel construction/repair, and the balance for Grants-in-aid at District/State level. States/UTs may incur up to 1% of total Central Assistance released for administrative expenses of State Scheduled Caste Corporations.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs 10000/- per beneficiary or 50% of loan
application_process: 1. State/UT Governments prepare an Action Plan (AP) before the beginning of each financial year, including separate proposals under Adarsh Gram, Grants-in-Aid, and Administrative Expenses components. 2. For Grants-in-Aid component, States/UTs submit a Perspective Plan for the entire approved scheme period (up to 2025-26) via the online portal https://pmajay.dosje.gov.in, indicating activity-wise targets and financial requirements. 3. The Perspective Plan is placed before the Project Appraisal cum Convergence Committee (PACC) for consideration and approval. 4. Approved projects are conveyed to respective States/UTs. 5. States/UTs may expedite well-performing projects while keeping fund requirements within notional allocation for the financial year. 6. Based on progress of the previous year, States may propose modifications to the Perspective Plan with revised physical targets and financial requirements. 7. District-level projects are appraised by DL-PACC and recommended to SL-PACC (headed by Principal Secretary of SC Welfare Department) for inclusion in the Annual Action Plan (AAP). 8. Funds projection in Perspective Plan should be about 3.5-4 times the Notional Allocation to avoid approval shortfalls if projects are rejected by PACC. 9. Submission of Utilization Certificates (UCs) for funds released earlier is required as part of the AP.
deadlines: Action Plans (APs) must be submitted before the beginning of each financial year. Perspective Plans for Grants-in-Aid component are to be uploaded on the online portal for consideration by PACC. No fixed rolling deadline mentioned; submission is annual and tied to financial year cycle.
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India

overview: Atal Vayo Abhyuday Yojana (AVYAY) is a centrally sponsored scheme under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment aimed at supporting senior citizens through various sub-schemes including the Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens (IPSrC), which provides financial assistance to NGOs for setting up and running Senior Citizen Homes and Continuous Care Homes. The scheme focuses on improving the quality of life of elderly persons, especially in underserved areas, by supporting residential care, healthcare, and recreational facilities. AVYAY also includes components like the National Action Plan for Senior Citizens (NAPSrC) and supports projects in Gap districts across India.
target_beneficiaries: senior citizens; NGOs; Trusts; Charitable institutions
eligibility: The Organisation / Implementing agency should have been registered for at least two years, and should have had at least two years' experience in the social sector at the time of applying for grant under the scheme. Applicant organisations must apply through the e-Anudaan portal only (https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login). For IPSrC projects in Gap districts, organisations must verify with District Administration whether the proposed district is a GAP district.
benefits: Financial assistance in the form of Grant-in-Aid for setting up and running Senior Citizen Homes (SrCH-25, SrCH-50) and Continuous Care Homes (CCH) for Alzheimer's/Dementia patients. Support includes funding for infrastructure, staffing, operational costs, and program implementation. RRTCs receive funding for infrastructure, training facilities, outreach, stakeholder management, and financial sustainability to support senior citizen programs across states.
financial_support: Financial assistance is provided as Grant-in-Aid through the e-Anudaan portal. The quantum and conditions of assistance are detailed in the AVYAY scheme guidelines. Cost norms for projects are specified on pages 45 to 47 of the Scheme Guideline. Assistance covers both recurring and non-recurring expenses for setting up and running senior citizen care facilities.
application_process: 1. Visit the e-Anudaan portal at https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login 2. Ensure NGO is registered for at least two years with two years' experience in the social sector 3. Verify if the district is a GAP district (for IPSrC projects) with District Administration 4. Login to the portal and submit the online application 5. Upload required documents including NGO Unique ID, PAN, and project details 6. Submit application before the deadline 7. Await verification, inspection, scrutiny, recommendation, financial approval, and GIA sanction through the portal workflow
deadlines: Applications for new projects under IPSrC/AVYAY for 2024-25 were accepted from 01st July 2024 till 15th August, 2024 (as per notice dated 01st July, 2024). Deadlines vary by sub-scheme and year; applicants must check current notices on the portal.
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India

overview: The National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) is a Government of India undertaking under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, established on February 8, 1989, to promote socio-economic development of Scheduled Castes through financial assistance at concessional interest rates. It provides loans and grants via State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs) and other channel partners for income-generating activities, education, skill development, and fellowships. NSFDC implements various credit and non-credit schemes including Term Loan, Micro Finance Scheme, Educational Loan Scheme, Udyam Nidhi Yojana, Aajeevika Microfinance Yojana, and the National Fellowship for Scheduled Caste Students (NFSC).
eligibility: The beneficiary should be from the Scheduled Caste Community. Individuals, Partnership Firms and Co-operative Societies are eligible to undertake income generating activities. However, proposals submitted by Partnership Firms and Co-operative Societies shall be considered subject to the following: (a) All the members of Partnership Firms/Co-operative Societies belong to Scheduled Caste community (b) Annual family income of each member/applicant should not exceed Rs.5.00 lakh. Annual family income of the applicants should be within Rs.5.00 lakhs (for both rural and urban areas w.e.f. 07th January' 2026) under Credit Based Schemes. There is no income criterion under Skill Development Training Programmes. The verification of eligibility criteria shall be the sole responsibility of the SCAs/CAs. NSFDC shall, however, be entitled to re-verify the eligibility of the applicants, if it so desires.
benefits: Financial assistance at concessional interest rates under various credit schemes; loans up to 90% of project cost with varying maximum limits per scheme; interest rates charged to SCAs/CAs ranging from 2.5% to 5%, which in turn charge beneficiaries from 6.5% to 15%; repayment periods ranging from 3 to 12 years; moratorium periods from 3 months to 1 year; for non-credit schemes: 100% grant to Training Institute and stipend of Rs.1500 per month per trainee subject to 80%+ attendance; under NFSC: monthly fellowship of Rs.31,000 (initial 2 years) and Rs.35,000 (remaining tenure), contingency grants, HRA @8%-24%, escort/reader assistance of Rs.2,000/p.m. for disabled candidates.
financial_support: NSFDC provides loans up to 90% of the project cost with scheme-specific maximum limits: Term Loan (>Rs.1.25 lakh & upto Rs.45.00 lakh), Micro Finance Scheme (upto Rs.1.25 lakhs), Educational Loan Scheme (upto Rs.40.00 Lakh or 90% of course fee), Udyam Nidhi Yojana (upto Rs.4.50 lakhs), Aajeevika Microfinance Yojana (upto Rs.1.25 lakhs). Interest charged to SCAs/CAs ranges from 2.5% to 5% p.a., which is passed on to beneficiaries at 6.5% to 15% p.a. Repayment is in quarterly instalments with moratorium periods. For NFSC, fellowship is disbursed monthly via bank account after Aadhaar seeding and verification.
application_process: 1. Eligible target group (Scheduled Castes persons with annual family income up to Rs.5.00 lakhs) submits loan application to District Offices of State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs). 2. District Offices of SCAs/CAs scrutinize applications and forward them to their Head Offices. 3. SCAs/CAs appraise project viability and forward viable projects to NSFDC with recommendations. 4. NSFDC's Project Desk appraises proposals and submits report to Project Clearance Committee (PCC) for concurrence. 5. PCC-recommended proposals are sanctioned. 6. Sanction Letters (Letter of Intent - LOI) with Terms & Conditions are issued to SCAs/CAs for acceptance. 7. After acceptance of terms and fulfillment of Prudential Norms, funds are disbursed to SCAs/CAs on receipt of demand. 8. SCAs/CAs disburse funds to beneficiaries. 9. Beneficiaries repay loans as per stipulated repayment schedule.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC)

National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC) is a wholly owned Government of India undertaking under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, established on 24th January 1997 and operational since October 1997. It serves as an apex corporation for the socio-economic upliftment of Safai Karamcharis, Scavengers, and their dependents throughout India through various loan and non-loan based schemes. NSKFDC has also been designated as the nodal agency for implementing the Central Sector National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem (NAMASTE) scheme under the same ministry.
target_beneficiaries: Safai Karamcharis; Scavengers; dependents of Safai Karamcharis and Scavengers; registered co-operative societies of Safai Karamcharis; legally constituted associations/firms promoted by the target group
eligibility: Safai Karamcharis (including Wastepickers), identified Manual Scavengers and their dependents. Registered co-operative societies of Safai Karamcharis. Legally constituted association/firm promoted by the target group. All those who produce a certificate from local Revenue Officer/local Municipal Officer/Cantonment Executive Officer/Railway Officer, Head of Govt. Departments (Schools, Colleges, Forest, Health, Education, Animal Husbandry) having rank not less than Gazetted Officer, Elected Members of Municipal Body & Pradhan of Gram Panchayats, Regional Managers of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs)/Nationalised Banks. Under the MS Act, 2013, a person identified as Manual Scavenger in a survey need not provide any certificate once their name appears in the final list of Manual Scavengers prepared by State Governments/Union Territory Administration. No income limit is fixed for availing financial assistance. However, priority is accorded to economic development and rehabilitation of Scavengers, those with income below double the poverty line, Women from the target group, and Disabled persons among the target group. Financial viability and income-generating capacity of the project are considered while evaluating projects for financing.
benefits: Financial support through various loan schemes including General Term Loan (up to Rs.15.00 Lac), Education Loan (up to Rs.10.00 Lac for study in India, Rs.20.00 Lac for abroad), Mahila Samridhi Yojana (up to Rs.1.00 Lac), Mahila Adhikarita Yojana (up to Rs.2.00 Lac), Micro Credit Finance (up to Rs.1.00 Lac), Scheme for 'Pay and use' community toilets (up to Rs.25.00 Lac), Sanitary Marts Scheme (up to Rs.15.00 Lac), Green Business Scheme (up to Rs.15.00 Lac), and Swachhta Udyami Yojana (up to Rs.50.00 Lac for SHG/JRG/Cooperative). Non-loan based benefits include awareness camps, workshops, job fairs, marketing linkages/melas/exhibitions/trade fairs with free stalls, reimbursement of actual to & fro train/bus fare, lodging charges @ Rs.300/-, food charges @ Rs.150/-, and local conveyance expenses @ Rs.150/- per day/per beneficiary for exhibition participation. Interest rates range from 2% to 9% p.a. depending on the scheme and beneficiary category, with rebates for women and timely repayment. Repayment periods vary from 3 to 10 years with moratorium periods. Education loan interest is reimbursable under Ministry of Education scheme for beneficiaries with family annual income up to Rs.4.50 lac.
financial_support: NSKFDC provides financial assistance through various loan schemes with specific limits: General Term Loan up to Rs.15.00 Lac (90% of unit cost, 10% by SCA), Education Loan up to Rs.10.00 Lac for study in India and Rs.20.00 Lac for abroad, Mahila Samridhi Yojana up to Rs.1.00 Lac, Mahila Adhikarita Yojana up to Rs.2.00 Lac, Micro Credit Finance up to Rs.1.00 Lac, 'Pay and use' community toilets up to Rs.25.00 Lac, Sanitary Marts Scheme up to Rs.15.00 Lac, Green Business Scheme up to Rs.15.00 Lac, and Swachhta Udyami Yojana up to Rs.15.00 Lac for individual/SHG/JRG/Cooperative and Rs.50.00 Lac for SHG/JRG/Cooperative. Promoter's contribution is not required for projects up to Rs.2.00 Lac; for higher costs, minimum 5% promoter's contribution and 5% from SCA is required. Interest rates range from 2% to 9% p.a., with repayment periods from 3 to 10 years and moratorium periods. Education loan interest is reimbursable under Ministry of Education scheme for family income up to Rs.4.50 lac per annum. Non-loan based support includes reimbursement of travel, lodging, food, and local conveyance for exhibition participation.
application_process: 1. Identify eligibility as Safai Karamchari, Manual Scavenger, dependent, or registered entity promoted by target group. 2. Obtain required certificate from local Revenue Officer, Municipal Officer, Cantonment Executive Officer, Railway Officer, Head of Govt. Department (Gazetted Officer rank or above), Elected Member of Municipal Body, Pradhan of Gram Panchayat, or Regional Manager of RRB/Nationalised Bank (unless identified as Manual Scavenger under MS Act, 2013 survey). 3. Approach State Channelising Agency (SCA), Regional Rural Bank (RRB), or Nationalised Bank for loan submission. 4. Submit loan application form with project details. 5. Undergo evaluation based on financial viability, income-generating capacity, and priority criteria (income below double poverty line, women, disabled). 6. Upon approval, receive disbursement through SCA/RRB/Nationalised Bank. 7. For non-loan based schemes, participate in awareness camps, workshops, job fairs, or exhibitions with free stall provision and reimbursement of expenses.
deadlines: Rolling basis
implementing_agency: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC), a wholly owned Govt. of India Undertaking under the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (M/o SJ&E)

scheme_name: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana
overview: PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana is a Government of India scheme launched to promote rooftop solar installations in residential households. With an investment of over Rs. 75,000 crores, it aims to provide up to 300 units of free electricity per month to 1 crore households. The scheme offers financial subsidies for solar rooftop systems and enables consumers to benefit from reduced electricity bills through solar energy generation.
eligibility: Residential households across India are eligible to apply for the PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana. Applicants must register on the national portal and provide valid consumer details including mobile number, name, email, address, state, district, PIN code, and electricity consumer account number. Applications can be submitted either directly by the consumer or through empanelled vendors.
benefits: Financial subsidy for residential households: Rs. 30,000 per kW up to 2 kW, Rs. 18,000 per kW for additional capacity up to 3 kW, and a total subsidy of Rs. 78,000 for systems larger than 3 kW. For Group Housing Societies (GHS) and Resident Welfare Associations (RWA), subsidy is Rs. 18,000 per kW for common facilities (including EV charging) up to 500 kW capacity. Special states receive an additional 10% subsidy per kW. Beneficiaries also receive up to 300 units of free electricity monthly after installation.
financial_support: The scheme provides a central financial subsidy for rooftop solar installations. For individual households: Rs. 30,000 per kW for the first 2 kW, Rs. 18,000 per kW for the next kW (up to 3 kW), and a capped total subsidy of Rs. 78,000 for systems above 3 kW. For GHS/RWA, Rs. 18,000 per kW is provided for common facilities up to 500 kW. Special states receive an additional 10% subsidy per kW. The subsidy is disbursed after successful installation and verification.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 78,000 (capped subsidy for systems above 3 kW)
application_process: Step 01: Visit the website at https://pmsuryaghar.gov.in/. Step 02: Go to consumer page and select 'Apply Now' or open the Login dropdown and select 'Consumer Login'. Step 03: Enter valid registered consumer mobile number, enter captcha, select 'Yes, I have read all the guidelines of PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana', then click 'Verify'. Step 04: Enter valid mobile OTP received via SMS and click 'Login'. Step 05: After successful login, enter profile details (consumer name, email, address, state, district, PIN code) and click 'Save'. Step 06: Submit application either by clicking 'Apply for Solar Rooftop' or through vendor selection. Select state, district, electricity distribution company/utility, enter consumer account number, click 'Fetch Details', then click 'Next' to start filling the application.
deadlines: Scheme closes on 31 March 2027
implementing_agency: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy

The SEED Scheme for DNTs (Scheme for Economic Empowerment of De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Tribes) is a Central Sector Scheme of the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, implemented by the Development and Welfare Board for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Communities (DWBDNC). It aims to provide economic support and welfare services through four components: Educational Empowerment (free coaching), Health Insurance (via PMJAY), Livelihoods initiative (through NRLM/SRLM), and Land & Housing assistance (via PMAY). The scheme targets DNT, NT, and SNT communities with a total family income of Rs. 2.5 lakh or less per annum who are not availing similar benefits from central or state schemes.
eligibility: The DNT Communities having total family income from all sources of Rs.2.50 lakh or less per annum and not availing any such benefits from similar Scheme of Centre Government or the State Government will be eligible for benefits under the Scheme.
benefits: Free coaching for competitive examinations (UPSC, SSC, NEET, JEE, etc.), monthly stipend (Rs. 1500 for local students, Rs. 3000 for outstation students), special allowance for students with disabilities (Rs. 2000/month), health insurance coverage of Rs. 5 lakh annually under PMJAY, livelihood support through SHG formation and revolving fund loans, housing assistance via PMAY, and assistance in obtaining essential documents like Aadhaar, bank account, caste certificate, income certificate, etc.
financial_support: Financial assistance includes revolving fund loans to SHGs (e.g., ₹10,000 to ₹20,000 at 1% interest as seen in success stories), stipends for coaching students, and support for housing under PMAY. The scheme has an approximate total cost of Rs. 200 crore to be spent over 5 years starting from Financial Year 2021-22.
application_process: 1. Assess eligibility based on DNT/NT/SNT status and family income ≤ Rs. 2.5 lakh/annum. 2. Apply online via the SEED portal (coaching.dosje.gov.in for education component, or https://dwbdnc.dosje.gov.in/register for livelihood/health/housing components). 3. Upload required documents (Aadhaar, income certificate, caste certificate, bank details, etc.). 4. Applications are scrutinized in order of merit. 5. Selected candidates receive an 'Open Assurance' letter. 6. For coaching: first installment (50% of course fee) released via DBT upon admission confirmation; remaining fee reimbursed after joining institute and submitting proof. 7. For livelihood: SHGs receive revolving fund support; individuals get assistance for bank account linkage, skill training, and marketing support. 8. For health: facilitate enrollment in PMJAY for Rs. 5 lakh annual coverage. 9. For housing: assist in availing benefits under PMAY-G/U.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications accepted throughout the year for livelihood, health, and housing components; for free coaching, portal opens in July and January each year for one month each.
implementing_agency: Development and Welfare Board for De-notified, Nomadic and Semi-Nomadic Communities (DWBDNC)

overview: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthan Mahabhiyan (PM-KUSUM) is a Government of India scheme aimed at de-dieselising the farm sector and enhancing farmers' income by promoting solar energy. It provides central government subsidy for installation of standalone solar pumps, solarization of grid-connected agricultural pumps, and installation of grid-connected solar power plants up to 2MW on barren/fallow land. Farmers can sell electricity generated to local DISCOM at tariffs determined by state regulators. The scheme is implemented by designated departments of State Governments.
eligibility: Farmers seeking to install standalone solar pumps, solarize existing grid-connected agricultural pumps, or install grid-connected solar power plants up to 2MW on their barren/fallow land are eligible under the scheme, as implemented by designated departments of State Governments.
benefits: Central government subsidy up to 30% or 50% of total cost for installation of standalone solar pumps and solarization of grid-connected agricultural pumps; opportunity to install grid-connected solar power plants up to 2MW on barren/fallow land and sell electricity to local DISCOM at tariff determined by state regulator; enhanced income for farmers; reduction in diesel dependence in agriculture.
financial_support: Under the Scheme, central government subsidy upto 30% or 50% of the total cost is given for the installation of standalone solar pumps and also for the solarization of existing grid-connected agricultural pumps. Further, farmers can also install grid-connected solar power plants up to 2MW under the Scheme on their barren/fallow land and sell electricity to local DISCOM at a tariff determined by state regulator.
application_process: 1. Visit the official PM-KUSUM portal or MNRE website for information. 2. Beware of fraudulent websites and mobile applications impersonating PM-KUSUM. 3. Apply through designated departments of the State Government. 4. Submit required documents as per state-level guidelines. 5. Await approval and installation of solar pumps or power plants. 6. For grid-connected systems, sell electricity to local DISCOM at state-determined tariff.
implementing_agency: Designated departments of the State Government
