The National Bio Energy Programme - Biogas is a scheme under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) aimed at promoting biogas technology for clean cooking and lighting in rural areas. It provides Central Financial Assistance (CFA) for setting up biogas plants, including family-size and community/ institutional plants, to reduce dependence on fossil fuels and improve rural energy access. The programme supports the installation of biogas plants through State Nodal Agencies and implementing agencies, with subsidies disbursed after verification of plant completion and functionality.
eligibility: Any individual, farmers, institutions, industries, or NGOs can apply for setting up biogas plants under the National Bio Energy Programme. The applicant must be the owner or authorized representative of the site where the biogas plant is to be installed. For community/ institutional plants, the applying entity must have legal ownership or long-term usage rights of the land. The proposed biogas plant must conform to MNRE-approved designs and specifications, and the beneficiary must contribute the beneficiary share as per the scheme guidelines.
benefits: Financial benefits include Central Financial Assistance (CFA) as a subsidy towards the capital cost of biogas plants. Non-financial benefits include access to clean cooking fuel, reduction in indoor air pollution, production of organic slurry as bio-fertilizer, improved sanitation through waste management, and potential for income generation from excess biogas or slurry sale. The scheme also promotes energy independence in rural areas and supports sustainable agriculture through slurry utilization.
financial_support: Central Financial Assistance (CFA) is provided as a subsidy for biogas plants. For family-size biogas plants (1 to 25 m³ capacity), CFA is ₹9,000 per m³ for general category and ₹12,000 per m³ for North Eastern States, including Sikkim. For community/ institutional biogas plants (above 25 m³ capacity), CFA is 30% of the benchmark cost, with higher rates of 50% for North Eastern States, hilly areas, and islands. The CFA is released in instalments after verification of plant installation and functionality, with the beneficiary share to be borne by the applicant.
application_process: 1. Identify the appropriate biogas plant size and type based on requirement. 2. Contact the State Nodal Agency or Khadi and Village Industries Commission (KVIC) for guidance and application forms. 3. Submit the application along with required documents to the State Nodal Agency or implementing agency. 4. Obtain technical sanction and approval for the biogas plant design and site. 5. Install the biogas plant using an approved turnkey key worker or through self-construction with technical guidance. 6. Notify the implementing agency upon completion for inspection and verification. 7. After successful verification of plant functionality, submit the completion report and utilization certificate. 8. Receive disbursement of Central Financial Assistance (CFA) directly into the beneficiary's bank account.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year subject to availability of funds.
implementing_agency: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)
contact_details: Email: biogas@mnre.gov.in | Helpline: 1800-180-3333 (PM-KUSUM toll-free, may be used for general queries)

overview: The National Bio Energy Programme - Biomass is a scheme under the Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE) aimed at promoting biomass-based energy projects in India. It provides Central Financial Assistance (CFA) for setting up bioenergy projects such as Waste to Energy (WTE), Biomass, and Biogas programmes. The scheme supports project developers through online applications on designated portals and offers CFA based on benchmark costs, with additional service charges for implementing agencies.
target_beneficiaries: individuals; farmers; industries; co-operatives; organizations; government/semi-government agencies
eligibility: Any individual, organization, industry, or government/semi-government agency can apply. For WTE and Biomass programmes, project developers must submit online applications on the Biourja portal. For Biogas programme, applicants must submit online applications on the Biogas portal and physical applications to the Programme Implementing Agency under the Biourja portal.
benefits: Central Financial Assistance (CFA) for setting up bioenergy projects, updated CFA rates for pellet and briquette manufacturing plants, support for biomass utilization to strengthen renewable energy mix, and facilitation through online portals (Biourja, Biogas, WTE) for application submission.
financial_support: CFA is provided for setting up bioenergy projects under the National Bio Energy Programme (NBP). For WTE and Biomass programmes, CFA is based on benchmark costs. For Biogas programme, CFA is available as per the scheme guidelines. State Implementing Agencies (SIAs) are also eligible for service charges of 1.33% of the eligible CFA. The exact quantum and mechanism are detailed in the scheme guidelines and related office memoranda.
application_process: 1. Visit the respective portal: WTE (https://mnre.gov.in/en/waste-to-energy/), Biomass (https://mnre.gov.in/en/bio-mass/), or Biogas (https://mnre.gov.in/en/bio-gas/). 2. Submit online application on the Biourja portal for WTE and Biomass programmes. 3. For Biogas programme, submit online application on the Biogas portal and physical application to the Programme Implementing Agency under the Biourja portal. 4. Await in-principle approval of CFA within 100 days.
deadlines: 100 days for in-principle approval of CFA for WTE and Biomass projects
implementing_agency: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy (MNRE)

overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for IT Hardware 2.0 is a financial incentive program launched by the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) to boost domestic manufacturing and attract large investments in the value chain for IT hardware products such as laptops, tablets, all-in-one PCs, servers, and ultra small form factor devices. The scheme provides incentives based on net incremental sales over a base year, with the aim of reducing import dependency and enhancing India's share in global electronics manufacturing. Support is provided for a period of six years under PLI 2.0, with incremental investment and sales thresholds determining eligibility.
target_beneficiaries: Global Company; Hybrid (Global/Domestic) Company; Domestic Company
eligibility: Eligibility is determined by consolidated global manufacturing revenue in the base year (FY 2021-22 for PLI 2.0). For Global Companies: revenue > INR 5,000 crore for target segment or > INR 10,000 crore for electronics hardware products/sub-assemblies/components. For Hybrid (Global/Domestic) Companies: Global arm revenue > INR 5,000 crore for target segment or > INR 10,000 crore for electronics hardware; Domestic arm revenue > INR 10 crore for target segment or > INR 20 crore for electronics hardware. For Domestic Companies: revenue > INR 10 crore for target segment or > INR 20 crore for electronics hardware products/sub-assemblies/components. Applicants must meet incremental investment thresholds post-March 31, 2021, and net incremental sales criteria. Only one application per applicant is allowed.
benefits: Financial incentives ranging from 1% to 5% on net incremental sales over the base year, depending on localization milestones and year of application. Incentives are disbursed annually based on verified net incremental sales and investment. Non-financial benefits include facilitation through Project Management Agency (PMA), access to policy support, and recognition under the scheme. Domestic value addition is promoted through localization requirements for PCB assembly, battery packs, power adapters, and enclosures. The scheme supports employment generation and technological upgradation in the IT hardware sector.
financial_support: Incentives are calculated as a percentage of net incremental sales (manufactured in India and covered under target segment) over the base year. The incentive rate decreases over time: 4% in Year 1, 3% in Year 2, 2% in Year 3, and 2% or 1% in Year 4 depending on localization achievement. Total expected incentive outlay over six years is INR 16,939 crore for PLI 2.0, with annual allocations increasing from INR 321 crore in Year 1 to INR 6,118 crore in Year 6. Incentives are disbursed annually upon verification of sales and investment claims by the Project Management Agency (PMA).
grant_amount_per_entity: Incentive is not a fixed grant but a percentage of net incremental sales, subject to annual caps: Year 1: up to INR 3,000 crore for IT Hardware Companies and INR 300 crore for Domestic Companies; Year 2: up to INR 7,500 crore and INR 600 crore; Year 3: up to INR 15,000 crore and INR 1,600 crore; Year 4: up to INR 30,000 crore and INR 2,400 crore. Actual payout depends on achieved net incremental sales and compliance with localization criteria.
application_process: 1. Register on the PLI IT Hardware portal at https://pliithw.com using CIN as user ID. 2. Login and fill the application form with company details, including CIN, PAN, and authorized signatory information. 3. Upload required documents: Certificate of Incorporation, PAN, GST registration, authorization letter, business profile, and audited financials. 4. Submit self-certified statement of consolidated global manufacturing revenue for FY 2021-22 (or relevant base year) for target segment and electronics hardware products/sub-assemblies/components. 5. Provide details of group companies whose revenue is being considered for qualification. 6. Submit baseline certificates for net sales and investment as of 31.03.2021 (or relevant date). 7. Upload project reports on proposed sales, investment, localization plan, employment generation, and domestic value addition. 8. Pay application fee via NEFT/RTGS to IFCI-PMA-PLI-ITHW account (details in form). 9. Submit integrity undertaking and regulatory approvals information. 10. Final submission and await approval from Project Management Agency (PMA)/MeitY based on eligibility and ranking.
deadlines: Application window initially open till 31st July 2023, which may be extended or reopened based on industry response. For PLI 2.0, the initial application window was till 31st July 2023, with provisions for extension.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), implemented through Project Management Agency (PMA) - IFCI Ltd.
contact_details: Enquiry Mail: pliithw[at]ifciltd[dot]com | Contact Person: Ms Rubina Duggal, DGM | Phone: +91 9319019065 | Office Timings: 09:45 AM – 06:00 PM (Monday – Friday) | Registered Office: IFCI Limited, IFCI Tower, 61 Nehru Place, New Delhi-110 019

overview: The Carbon Credit Trading Scheme 2023 is a market-based mechanism under the Energy Conservation Act, 2001, designed to facilitate the trading of carbon credits to reduce greenhouse gas emissions. It enables designated consumers, including industries and power sector entities, to earn and trade Energy Savings Certificates (ESCerts) based on over-achievement of energy efficiency targets under the Perform, Achieve and Trade (PAT) scheme. The scheme aims to promote energy efficiency, reduce emissions intensity, and support India's climate goals through a transparent and tradable certificate system.
target_beneficiaries: industries; power sector entities; designated consumers
eligibility: Designated consumers under the Energy Conservation Act, 2001, including industries and establishments in sectors notified by the Central Government (such as thermal power plants, cement, iron & steel, aluminium, fertilizers, pulp & paper, textiles, and petrochemicals), which are assigned specific energy consumption reduction targets under the PAT cycle, are eligible to participate in the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme. Entities that over-achieve their energy savings targets are issued Energy Savings Certificates (ESCerts) that can be traded on power exchanges.
benefits: Financial benefits include revenue generation from sale of ESCerts; non-financial benefits include recognition for energy efficiency improvements, compliance with regulatory targets, enhanced corporate sustainability profile, access to green finance, and contribution to national climate goals. The scheme also promotes technology upgradation and operational efficiency in participating industries.
financial_support: The scheme does not provide direct financial subsidies or grants. Instead, it enables financial gains through the market-based trading of Energy Savings Certificates (ESCerts) on power exchanges like Indian Energy Exchange (IEX) and Power Exchange India Ltd (PXIL). The value of ESCerts is determined by market demand and supply, with each certificate representing one metric tonne of oil equivalent (mtoe) of energy saved.
application_process: 1. Designated consumers are notified of their energy consumption reduction targets under the relevant PAT cycle by the Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE). 2. Entities implement energy efficiency measures to achieve or exceed their targets. 3. Verified energy savings data is submitted to BEE through accredited energy auditors. 4. BEE issues Energy Savings Certificates (ESCerts) to entities that over-achieve their targets. 5. ESCerts are deposited in the entity's account on the Power Exchange platform. 6. Entities can sell ESCerts on power exchanges through registered brokers or directly. 7. Shortfall entities can buy ESCerts to meet compliance obligations. 8. Transactions are settled as per exchange rules, and certificates are transferred upon sale.
deadlines: Aligned with PAT cycle timelines; issuance and trading of ESCerts occur after target period completion and verification, typically on an annual basis per cycle.
implementing_agency: Bureau of Energy Efficiency (BEE), Ministry of Power

overview: The NAMASTE scheme (National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem) is a Central Sector Scheme jointly implemented by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment (MoSJE), Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs (MoHUA), Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR), and the Department of Drinking Water and Sanitation (DDWS), with NSKFDC as the implementing agency. It aims to ensure the safety, dignity, and social security of sanitation workers through mechanization, occupational safety training, provision of PPE, health insurance under AB-PMJAY, livelihood support, and capital subsidies for sanitation-related equipment. The scheme covers Sewer and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs), Manual Scavengers, and Waste Pickers across urban and rural India.
target_beneficiaries: Sewer and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs); Manual Scavengers; Waste Pickers; Sanitation workers
eligibility: The scheme targets Sewer and Septic Tank Workers (SSWs) engaged in cleaning and maintenance of sewers including those managing pumping stations, manholes/machine holes, and sewer lines; and those engaged in septic tank cleaning. Such workers may or may not be engaged by ULB/parastatal, empanelled/registered/licensed Private Sanitation Service Organisation (PSSO) and other private sanitation service providers. Waste Pickers are defined as persons or groups of persons informally engaged in collection and recovery of reusable and recyclable solid waste from the source of waste generation the streets, bins, material recovery facilities, processing and waste disposal facilities for sale to recyclers directly or through intermediaries to earn their livelihood. The scheme applies to Waste Pickers handling municipal solid waste, including informal Waste Pickers working at streets, door to door, transfer stations, open dumpsites, landfill sites, waste disposal sites, material recovery facilities, waste recovery & recycling, repair facilities etc. and itinerant waste buyers; informal Waste sorters working at waste processing/waste recycling facilities; and informal workers/sorters engaged in this activity for a minimum period of 6 months.
benefits: The scheme provides occupational safety training, provision of personal protective equipment (PPE) kits, health insurance coverage under Ayushman Bharat-Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY), livelihood counselling and support, access to subsidized loans for the purchase of sanitation-related equipment, linkage with other government schemes, capital subsidy for procurement of sanitation-related vehicles/equipment through Swachhta Udyami Yojana (SUY), upfront capital subsidy to SSWs for sanitation-related projects (50% of project cost, up to Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit or 50% of project cost subject to a maximum of Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit), upfront capital subsidy to Private Sanitation Service Organisations (PSSOs) and Private Contractors for procurement of mechanised cleaning equipment/vehicles (25% of project cost, subject to a maximum of Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit), provision of safety devices to ULBs, workshops on hazardous cleaning of sewers and septic tanks, mechanised cleaning equipment/machines, profiling of Sewer Entry Professionals/Septic Tank Workers, extending Health Insurance Scheme Benefits, and formation of Emergency Response Sanitation Units (ERSUs).
financial_support: The scheme provides upfront capital subsidy to SSWs for procurement of sanitation-related equipment: for individual projects costing up to Rs. 15.00 lakh, 50% of the project cost; for group projects costing up to Rs. 50.00 lakh with each beneficiary's project share maximum up to Rs. 10.00 lakh. For group projects, same as above with maximum per member capital subsidy of Rs. 5 lakh and maximum group project subsidy of Rs. 25 lakh. Upfront Capital Subsidy to Private Sanitation Service Organisations (PSSOs) and Private Contractors for procurement of mechanised cleaning equipment's/vehicles: 25% of the project cost, subject to a maximum of Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit (whichever is lower). The capital subsidy is provided by way of upfront capital subsidy to SSWs. Loans are extended through Channelizing Agencies (CAs) i.e. State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs), Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) and Public Sector Banks (PSBs) to the target group under various loan based schemes such as General Term Loan (GTL) up to Rs.15.00 lacs at 3% p.a. (NSKFDC to CAs) and 6% p.a. (CAs to beneficiaries) with repayment period upto 10 years, Mahila Adhikarita Yojana (MAY) up to Rs.2.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 5% p.a. for 5 years, Mahila Samridhi Yojana (MSY) up to Rs.1.00 lac at 1% p.a. and 4% p.a. for 3 years, Micro Credit Finance (MCF) up to Rs.1.00 lac at 2% p.a. and 5% p.a. for 3 years, Education loan (EL) up to Rs.10.00 lacs for study in India and Rs.20.00 lacs for study abroad at 1% p.a. and 4% p.a. (with 0.5% rebate for women in case of study in India) for 5 years after course completion with one year moratorium, Green Business (GB) up to Rs.2.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. (with 1% rebate for women) for 6 years including moratorium, Sanitary Marts (SM) up to Rs.15.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. for 10 years, Scheme for 'Pay and Use' Community Toilets up to Rs.25.00 lacs at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. for 10 years, and Swachhta Udyami Yojana (SUY) for procurement of sanitation related equipments/vehicles up to Rs.50.00 lacs per unit at 2% p.a. and 4% p.a. (with 1% rebate for timely repayment) for 10 years after implementation period of 90 days and moratorium of 90 days.
grant_amount_per_entity: Upfront capital subsidy to SSWs: 50% of project cost, maximum Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit; for group projects: maximum per member subsidy Rs. 5 lakh, maximum group subsidy Rs. 25 lakh. Upfront Capital Subsidy to PSSOs and Private Contractors: 25% of project cost, maximum Rs. 10.00 lakh per unit.
application_process: 1. Identify and profile Sewer/Septic Tank Workers (SSWs) and Waste Pickers through profiling camps conducted by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in urban areas and Gram Panchayats in rural areas, with assistance of civil society organizations, cooperatives, self-help groups (SHGs), Common Service Centres (CSCs) and Waste Pickers collectives. 2. Use the NAMASTE mobile app or web portal for data collection during profiling. 3. Conduct e-KYC of Aadhaar IDs of waste pickers/SSWs for validation. 4. Submit survey data through the portal; it undergoes error flagging by Block Nodal Officer/Validators, with errors to be resolved within 7 days. 5. After validation, a unique NAMASTE ID is generated for each validated worker. 6. Block Nodal Officer distributes ID cards to all validated workers. 7. Access benefits such as PPE kits, health insurance under AB-PMJAY, occupational safety training, and capital subsidy for sanitation-related equipment through Swachhta Udyami Yojana (SUY) or direct subsidy for sanitation-related projects. 8. For capital subsidy, submit project proposal with required documents to the concerned authority for approval and disbursement.
deadlines: Rolling basis — profiling drives conducted in phases; continuous data updates post-verification (database to be updated annually)
implementing_agency: National Safai Karamcharis Finance & Development Corporation (NSKFDC)

overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Pharmaceuticals aims to enhance India's manufacturing capabilities by increasing investment and production in the sector, contributing to product diversification to high-value goods, and creating global champions out of India who can penetrate global value chains using cutting-edge technology. The scheme targets pharmaceutical manufacturers grouped by Global Manufacturing Revenue (GMR) and provides financial incentives based on incremental sales over a base year (FY 2019-20). It covers three categories of pharmaceutical goods and operates over a tenure from FY 2020-21 to FY 2028-29, including a gestation period and six years of incentive disbursement.
eligibility: Applicants are grouped into three categories based on Global Manufacturing Revenue (GMR) from pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro diagnostic medical devices in FY 2019-20: Group A (GMR ≥ Rs 5,000 crore), Group B (GMR between Rs 500 crore and Rs 5,000 crore), and Group C (GMR < Rs 500 crore, with a sub-group for MSME). Eligibility for incentive requires minimum cumulative investment over five years (Group A: Rs 1,000 crore, Group B: Rs 250 crore, Group C: Rs 50 crore) and minimum year-on-year percentage growth in sales (Group A/B/C: 200/50/10 in FY 2021-22, increasing to 1000/250/50 by FY 2025-26). For first year of production, participants must achieve threshold sales specified in guidelines; for subsequent years, a minimum 7% year-on-year growth is required. MSME participants in Group C may commit investment as per application form.
benefits: Financial incentive on incremental sales over base year (FY 2019-20) at rates of 10% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 8% for 2026-27, and 6% for 2027-28 for Category 1 & 2 goods; 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27, and 3% for 2027-28 for Category 3 goods. Incentive is disbursed for a maximum of six years, based on verified incremental sales, and is subject to eligibility criteria including minimum cumulative investment and sales growth. The scheme also supports product diversification, technological upgradation, and integration into global value chains.
financial_support: The scheme provides financial incentives based on incremental sales of eligible pharmaceutical goods over the base year (FY 2019-20). Incentive rates are 10% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 8% for 2026-27, and 6% for 2027-28 for Category 1 & 2 goods; and 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27, and 3% for 2027-28 for Category 3 goods. Incentives are disbursed for a maximum of six years, contingent on achieving minimum cumulative investment and year-on-year sales growth thresholds. The total financial outlay is Rs. 15,000 crore, allocated as Rs. 11,000 crore for Group A, Rs. 2,250 crore for Group B, and Rs. 1,750 crore for Group C. Disbursement is managed by the Project Management Agency (PMA) after verification of eligibility and sales claims.
application_process: 1. Applicants must submit applications during the designated Application Window (60 days starting from 2nd June, 2021 to 31st July, 2021, as per initial guidelines; subsequent windows may be notified). 2. Applications are submitted via the online portal maintained by the Project Management Agency (PMA) at https://pli-pharma.udyamimitra.in. 3. Applicants must provide requisite information, supporting documents, and pay a non-refundable application fee electronically. 4. PMA conducts an examination of the application within 15 working days of receipt, checking completeness via a checklist. 5. If incomplete, PMA notifies the applicant to complete within 15 days; otherwise, the application is closed. 6. Upon completion, PMA issues an acknowledgement of receipt (not approval). 7. Eligible applicants are ranked based on evaluation criteria: Gross manufacturing investment (30%), number of ANDA/NDA approvals (30%), and R&D expenditure as % of GMR or GMR itself (40%), depending on group and product type. 8. Selection is made in order of rank, with limits: Group A (11 participants, max 4 foreign MNCs), Group B (9 participants, max 3 foreign MNCs), Group C (35 participants, min 20 MSMEs, min 5 in vitro diagnostic medical devices manufacturers). 9. Selected applicants must establish manufacturing facilities and commence production, with FY 2021-22 as the gestation period. 10. Incentive claims are submitted periodically based on incremental sales, verified by PMA and SSC, and disbursed after scrutiny.
deadlines: Application Window: 60 days starting from 2nd June, 2021 to 31st July, 2021 (both dates inclusive). Subsequent windows may be notified; refer to latest notices for current status.
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers

overview: The PLI Scheme for Specialty Steel is a Production Linked Incentive scheme notified by the Ministry of Steel, Government of India, to promote domestic manufacturing of specialty steel grades and enhance technology and value chain positioning of the Indian steel industry. The scheme provides financial incentives based on incremental production of eligible specialty steel products over a five-year period, with incentives disbursed from FY 2026-27 to FY 2030-31. It targets five broad product categories: Coated/Plated Steel Products, High Strength/Wear resistant Steel, Specialty Rails, Alloy Steel Products and Steel wires, and Electrical Steel (CRGO, CRNO).
target_beneficiaries: steel manufacturers; domestic companies; group companies; joint ventures
eligibility: Companies registered in India under the Companies Act 2013, desirous of manufacturing identified specialty steel grades, shall be eligible to apply for incentive under the scheme. The company shall ensure end-to-end manufacturing of applied product sub-category domestically, where the input material is melted and poured within the country using iron ore/scrap/sponge iron/pellets, etc. The net worth of the company (including that of the group companies) shall not be less than 30% of the total committed investment. Third party outsourcing is allowed up to 20% of value addition. For capacity augmentation, 50% of the threshold investment shall be applicable.
benefits: Financial incentives linked to incremental production of specialty steel products, calculated as (Eligible incremental production) x (Weighted Average Sales Price in base year or current year, whichever is lower) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100. Incentive disbursement is capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company per annum across all product categories. Non-financial benefits include eligibility to avail benefits under other schemes such as RoDTEP or State Government schemes, and the ability to undertake third-party value addition up to 20%.
financial_support: Incentive is calculated based on incremental production of applied product sub-category using the formula: Incentive = (A) x (B or C whichever is lowest) x (PLI rate as applicable)/100, where A = Eligible incremental production derived from sales, B = Weighted Average sales price of the applicant in current year, C = Weighted Average sales price in base year (2019-20). The PLI rate varies by slab (A, B, C) and year, ranging from 3% to 15%. The annual incentive payable is capped at Rs. 200 crores per eligible company. Incentive disbursement begins from FY 2026-27 for the production period FY 2025-26 to FY 2029-30.
grant_amount_per_entity: Rs. 200 crores per annum per eligible company
application_process: 1. Register on the PLI web portal (https://plimos.mecon.co.in). 2. Fill the application form online within 30 days from the date of launch of PLI Scheme 1.2. 3. Pay a non-refundable application fee of Rs 1 lakh (plus applicable GST). 4. Submit the signed copy of the online application along with all uploaded documents within 10 days of online submission. 5. Undergo application scrutiny by PMA for completeness of supporting documents. 6. A one-time correction window of 15 days is available after closure of application submission for completeness of supporting documents only. 7. Selected companies sign a MoU with the Ministry of Steel. 8. Submit performance security of 0.5% of committed investment in the form of Bank Guarantee. 9. Submit undertaking consenting to audit of manufacturing facilities. 10. File annual incentive claims within 7 months from the end of the financial year to which the claim pertains.
deadlines: Online Registration and Application Submission dates: Start Date: 04.11.2025, End Date: To be announced (based on PLI Scheme 1.2 launch). Correction window: A one-time correction window of fifteen days (15 days) shall be notified after close of application submission and evaluation by PMA.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Steel, Government of India
contact_details: Nodal Officer from PMA: Senior General Manager, Rolling Mills, & Nodal Officer PLI Scheme for Specialty steel, MECON Limited, Vivekanand Path, Doranda, Ranchi-834002, e-mail: plischeme@meconlimited.co.in; Nodal Officer from MoS: Deputy Secretary, Steel, Ministry of Steel (MoS), Govt. of India, Udyog Bhavan, New Delhi – 110011, e-mail: pli-mosteel@gov.in

overview: The PLI Scheme for Textiles was notified on 24/09/2021 to promote production of MMF Apparel & Fabrics and Technical Textiles products in India, enabling the textile industry to achieve size and scale, become globally competitive, create employment opportunities, and support viable enterprises. The scheme has an approved outlay of Rs. 10,683 crore and aims to drive fresh investment of over Rs. 19,000 crore and additional production turnover of over Rs. 3 lakh crore over five years. It includes two investment tracks: Part-1 requiring minimum ₹300 Crore investment and Part-2 requiring minimum ₹100 Crore investment, with incentives linked to turnover achievement.
eligibility: Any person including Company/Firm/LLP/Trust willing to create a separate manufacturing company under Companies Act 2013, and invest minimum 300 Crore (excluding land and administrative building cost) to manufacture Notified Products for Scheme Part-1, or minimum 100 Crore for Scheme Part-2. The applicant must form a new/separate company under Companies Act, 2013 before commencement of investment, and the new entity will be known as Participant. Investment made in anticipation from the date of Scheme Notification shall also be accounted for calculation of threshold investment provided their proposal is approved for participation under the Scheme.
benefits: Financial incentives linked to turnover achievement, with rates varying by year and scheme part (e.g., 15% for Part-1 Year 1, 11% for Part-2 Year 1). Incentives are payable for a period of 5 years only from the first performance year. Participants are eligible to apply for other applicable Government of India or State Government schemes (e.g., duty remission, duty exemptions, duty drawback). Foreign investment must comply with Consolidated FDI Policy 2020. Participants must commit to setting up manufacturing facilities with appropriate quality and testing facilities conforming to BIS or International Standards, and ensure 'Made in India' tags on products.
financial_support: Incentives are calculated on achieving threshold turnover as mentioned in para 4.1/4.2 under the respective scheme in the first Performance Year. Rate of incentives for a particular performance year are as indicated in Table 2: For Scheme Part-1: Year 1 (FY 2024-2025) - 600 Cr turnover at 15% rate; Year 2 - 750 Cr at 14%; Year 3 - 937.5 Cr at 13%; Year 4 - 1171.87 Cr at 12%; Year 5 - 1464.84 Cr at 11%. For Scheme Part-2: Year 1 - 200 Cr turnover at 11% rate; Year 2 - 250 Cr at 10%; Year 3 - 312.5 Cr at 9%; Year 4 - 390.63 Cr at 8%; Year 5 - 488.2 Cr at 7%. There is a provision of cap of 10% over and above the prescribed minimum incremental turnover growth of 25% for the purpose of calculation of incentives from Year 2 onward. For Year 1, the cap of 10% is applied over and above turnover of two times of the investment made under the Scheme up to 2024-25.
application_process: 1. Register on the Ministry’s PLI portal developed for this purpose in the prescribed format. 2. Submit application with required documents. 3. Accept Undertaking and Pay application fee. 4. Upload required documents (as per Type of applicant). 5. E-Sign uploaded documents via OTP validation form Aadhaar. 6. Final Submit.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted year-round with Weekly Open House Forum every Friday between 4PM to 5PM to understand and resolve issues faced by participants.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Textiles, Government of India

overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Promoting Domestic Manufacturing of Medical Devices is a central sector scheme under the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India. It aims to boost domestic manufacturing of medical devices by providing financial incentives on incremental sales to reduce import dependence and promote self-reliance in the medical device sector. The scheme supports eligible applicants in manufacturing medical devices listed under specified categories, with incentives disbursed over a defined tenure based on achieved sales growth and committed investment.
eligibility: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019-20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices less than Rs. 500 crore. This group shall include a sub-group for MSME applicants, i.e., applicants registered as Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) with the Ministry of MSME, Government of India. For MSME participants in Group C, the threshold minimum cumulative investment shall be as committed by the participant in application form. The applicant should not have been declared as bankrupt or wilful defaulter or reported as fraud by any bank or financial institution or non-banking financial company.
benefits: Financial incentive on incremental sales of eligible medical devices manufactured in India, disbursed over a period of 6 years for sales of FY 2022-23 to FY 2027-28. The rate of incentive is 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27 and 3% for 2027-28 on incremental sales over base year (FY 2019-20). Additional support includes facilitation through Project Management Agency (PMA) for application processing, eligibility verification, and disbursement of incentives.
financial_support: Incentive is provided as a subsidy on incremental sales (over base year FY 2019-20) of eligible medical devices. For Group C (which includes medical device manufacturers with GMR < Rs. 500 crore), the rate of incentive is 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27 and 3% for 2027-28. The total financial outlay for Group C is Rs. 1,750 crore. Minimum cumulative investment required is Rs. 50 crore over 5 years (FY 2021-22 to FY 2025-26), with year-wise thresholds: FY 2021-22: Rs. 10 crore, FY 2022-23: Rs. 20 crore, FY 2023-24: Rs. 30 crore, FY 2024-25: Rs. 40 crore, FY 2025-26: Rs. 50 crore. For MSME participants in Group C, the minimum cumulative investment threshold is as committed in the application form.
application_process: 1. Applicants must submit an application through the online portal maintained by the Project Management Agency (PMA). 2. The application must contain requisite information, supporting documents, and application fee as prescribed in the guidelines. 3. PMA will conduct an examination of the application as per checklist in Appendix I within 15 working days of receipt. 4. If the application is found incomplete, PMA will inform the applicant to complete it within 15 days; otherwise, the application will be closed. 5. Upon satisfactory completion, PMA will issue an acknowledgement of receipt (not approval under the Scheme). 6. All applications are to be submitted via the online portal at https://pli-pharma.udyamimitra.in. 7. Selection of applicants is based on evaluation criteria including gross manufacturing investment, number of approved manufacturing plants, and GMR from in vitro diagnostic medical devices, with weightages as specified in the guidelines. 8. Selected applicants will be ranked, and incentives will be disbursed based on achieved incremental sales and compliance with eligibility criteria.
deadlines: The application window shall be of 60 days starting from 2nd June, 2021 to 31st July, 2021 (Both dates inclusive).
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India

scheme_name: PLI for Domestic Manufacturing of KSMs/APIs
overview: The Production Linked Incentive (PLI) Scheme for Promotion of Domestic Manufacturing of critical Key Starting Materials (KSMs)/ Drug Intermediates and Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) in the Country aims to enhance domestic manufacturing capabilities by providing financial incentives on incremental sales to eligible applicants. The scheme targets pharmaceutical manufacturers based on their Global Manufacturing Revenue (GMR) and supports the production of high-value pharmaceutical goods to reduce import dependence and strengthen India's position in the global pharmaceutical value chain. It operates over a tenure from FY 2020-21 to FY 2028-29, with incentives disbursed for 6 years based on sales performance.
eligibility: Applicants shall apply within the following three groups based on the respective criteria: Group A: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019 -20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices more than or equal to Rs. 5,000 crore. Group B: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019 -20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices between Rs. 500 (inclusive) crore and Rs. 5,000 crore. Group C: Applicants having Global Manufacturing Revenue (FY 2019 -20) of pharmaceutical goods and/or in vitro Diagnostic Medical Devices less than Rs. 500 crore. This group shall include a sub-group for MSME applicants, i.e., applicants registered as Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSME) with the Ministry of MSME, Government of India.
benefits: Financial incentive on incremental sales of pharmaceutical goods based on yearly threshold criteria of minimum cumulative investment and minimum percentage growth in sales. Incentive rates: 10% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 8% for 2026-27, and 6% for 2027-28 for Category 1 & 2 goods; 5% for FY 2022-23 to FY 2025-26, 4% for 2026-27, and 3% for 2027-28 for Category 3 goods. Additional incentive available as per para 7.2.3 of the guidelines.
financial_support: The total quantum of incentive (inclusive of administrative expenditure) under the scheme is about Rs 15,000 crore. The incentive allocation among the Target Groups is as follows: Group A: Rs 11,000 crore, Group B: Rs 2,250 crore, Group C: Rs 1,750 crore. Incentives are paid for a maximum of 6 years based on incremental sales over the base year (FY 2019-20).
application_process: 1. The applicant is required to submit the application as per application form prescribed in Appendix K. 2. The Scheme shall be open for applications during the Application Window. No application shall be accepted after the end of the Application Window. 3. An applicant can apply for one or more products as per list of eligible products as given in Appendix A. 4. An applicant shall submit an undertaking in the format given in Appendix E and also an undertaking in the format given in Appendix G consenting audit of their manufacturing site/offices for verification of information/data submitted along with the application. 5. On receipt of an application in the prescribed format, PMA will conduct an examination as per checklist in Appendix I. The aforesaid examination shall be completed within 15 working days from the date of receipt of the application or any subsequent submission of the revised application, if the original application was returned as incomplete earlier. Thereafter, the PMA shall issue an acknowledgement of receipt of the application. This acknowledgement shall not be construed as approval under the Scheme. 6. In case, on the above-mentioned examination, an application is found to be incomplete, PMA shall inform the applicant accordingly within 15 working days of receipt of the application. An applicant must complete an incomplete application within 15 days of such communication from PMA, failing which the application will be closed under intimation to the applicant. 7. A non-refundable application fee, as mentioned in Appendix C of these guidelines, would be payable for each application. The application fee would be accepted electronically only. 8. All applications will be submitted through an online portal maintained by the PMA. In case, the portal is not available, applications may be submitted in physical form to the PMA.
deadlines: The application window shall be of 60 days starting from 2nd June, 2021 to 31st July, 2021 (Both dates inclusive).
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India

scheme_name: Scheme for Strengthening of Pharmaceuticals Industry (SPI)
overview: The Scheme for Strengthening of Pharmaceuticals Industry (SPI) is a Central Sector Scheme under the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India, aimed at strengthening existing pharmaceutical infrastructure, upgrading production facilities of MSMEs to meet regulatory standards, and promoting knowledge and awareness in the pharma and medical devices sector. It combines three existing sub-schemes: Assistance to Pharmaceutical Industry for Common Facilities (APICF), Pharmaceutical Technology Upgradation Assistance Scheme (PTUAS), and Pharmaceutical & Medical Devices Promotion and Development Scheme (PMPDS). The scheme has a total financial outlay of Rs. 500 crore for the period FY 2021-22 to FY 2025-26.
target_beneficiaries: pharma clusters; MSMEs; pharma manufacturing units
eligibility: Pharmaceutical manufacturing units in a cluster who have come together to form a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) to execute the project of developing common facility. There shall be a minimum of 5 pharma units as members of (SPV). The SPV should be a separate legal entity registered under the Companies Act or Registration of Societies Act. Pharma enterprises shall hold at least 51% equity of the SPV. The combined net worth of members of SPV shall be equivalent to total grant amount applied for and each SPV member must have a net worth of at least 1.5 times of their proposed equity contribution. The SPV members shall be legally independent entities without any related-party relationship with each other as described under Accounting Standard (AS) 18 of the Companies (Accounting Standard) Rules, 2006.
benefits: Financial assistance for creation of Common Facilities (up to 70% of approved project cost or Rs. 20 crore, whichever is less, or 90% for Himalayan and North Eastern States), subsidy on reimbursement basis for technology upgradation to meet revised Schedule M and WHO-GMP standards (up to Rs. 1 crore per plant), and support for awareness programs, database creation, and industry-academia collaboration for overall sector development.
financial_support: Financial support is provided as capital subsidy or grant-in-aid. For APICF, the incentive is 70% of approved project cost or Rs. 20 crore, whichever is less (90% or Rs. 20 crore for Himalayan and North Eastern States). For RPTUAS (revamped PTUAS), incentive is based on average turnover: 20% of investment for turnover Rs. 1-50 crore, 15% for Rs. 50-250 crore, and 10% for Rs. 250-500 crore, subject to a maximum of Rs. 2 crore per plant. For PMPDS, support is provided for studies, awareness programs, database creation, and industry promotion. Disbursement is made in instalments based on milestone achievement, with 50% of eligible amount (up to Rs. 1 crore) released as first instalment upon submission of requisite documents.
grant_amount_per_entity: Up to Rs. 20 crore for APICF (70% of project cost or Rs. 20 crore, whichever is less); up to Rs. 2 crore per plant for RPTUAS; support for PMPDS activities as per approval
application_process: 1. Pharmaceutical units form a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) with minimum 5 pharma units and at least 51% equity held by pharma enterprises. 2. SPV submits application through the Project Management Consultant (PMC) portal with detailed project proposal, gap analysis, and required documents. 3. PMC examines the proposal, processes loan application if applicable, and forwards to lending institution for sanction within 90 days. 4. PMC scrutinizes proposal, verifies eligibility, and submits appraisal report with recommendations to Technical Committee within one month. 5. Technical Committee evaluates and recommends to Scheme Steering Committee (SSC). 6. SSC approves the project and sanctions financial support. 7. Upon approval, applicant submits requisite documents (e.g., Revised Schedule M or WHO-GMP certificate, CA certified expenditure) for instalment release. 8. First instalment (50% of eligible amount, up to Rs. 1 crore) released within 30 days of document submission. 9. Subsequent instalments released upon verification of milestone achievement and submission of required documents.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year as per notifications; specific windows may be opened for sub-schemes (e.g., API-CF invitation from 11.12.2024 to 13.01.2025)
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India
contact_details: Email: Head-PharmaPolicy[at]pharma-dept[dot]gov[dot]in | Helpline: Not explicitly mentioned in evidence

overview: The PRIP Scheme (Scheme for Promotion of Research and Innovation in Pharma MedTech Sector) is a Central Sector Scheme under the Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India. It aims to promote research and innovation in the pharmaceutical and medical technology sectors by providing financial assistance to industry and academia for strategic priority innovation projects, centres of excellence, and industry-academia collaboration. The scheme supports activities such as in-licensing of intellectual property, research and development, and commercialization of institutional intellectual property, with a focus on areas of public health concern where market potential is relatively lower.
eligibility: Every entity registered in India that is a company registered under the Companies Act, 2013 or under any previous company law (excluding one-person companies, companies with unlimited liability, dividend-paying companies, or companies whose objects include promotion of commerce, art, science, sports, education, research, social welfare, religion, charity, or environmental protection, or whose profits may only be applied for promoting said objects), a Limited Liability Partnership registered under the Limited Liability Partnership Act, 2008, or a partnership firm other than a sole proprietorship firm registered under the Indian Partnership Act, 1932. For Early Stage Projects, the applicant must be a startup with a valid certificate from the Inter-Ministerial Board of Certification under DPIIT notification G.S.R. 127(E) dated 19.2.2019, or an MSME with a valid Udyam Registration Certificate under MSME Ministry notification S.O. 2119(E) dated 26.6.2020. Applicants are ineligible if any key managerial personnel is convicted of an offence involving moral turpitude or corruption and sentenced to imprisonment for six months or more, or if the applicant or its holding company has filed for insolvency, winding-up, liquidation, or resolution proceedings.
benefits: Financial assistance for Centres of Excellence at NIPERs, Strategic Priority Innovation projects, and industry-academia collaboration for in-licensing of intellectual property. The scheme provides grant-in-aid for research and development, infrastructure development, equipment procurement, and other eligible activities. Benefits include support for building research capacities, translating and commercializing institutional intellectual property, augmenting institutional research capacities, and promoting industry-academia linkage. Financial assistance is disbursed into a project-specific dedicated account in a Scheduled Commercial Bank, subject to inspection and special audit, including forensic audit, by the Central Government. The scheme also provides for benefit-sharing mechanisms such as share allotment or revenue sharing, and supports milestone-linked financial assistance disbursement in four instalments, with the fourth instalment being at least 15% of the approved total financial assistance on a reimbursement basis.
financial_support: Financial assistance is provided as grant-in-aid disbursed in four instalments, with the fourth instalment being at least 15% of the approved total project cost and disbursed on a reimbursement basis. The Central Government may consider the project plan with or without modifications and accord in-principle approval. Upon acceptance, the applicant must submit documents evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost within 60 days, execute a duly signed funding agreement with the approved project plan annexed, and, if opting for benefit-share via share allotment, execute a duly signed share allotment agreement. Co-funding is required to the extent that total co-funding equals the approved total project cost less the approved total financial assistance, and the proportion of total co-funding to approved total financial assistance must be maintained at each instalment disbursement stage. The beneficiary must ensure cumulative co-funding in the required proportion is credited into the project account before each disbursement. Disbursement of milestone-linked financial assistance is based on achievement of milestones as per corresponding criteria, and instalments may be utilised for the corresponding purposes as specified in the project plan.
application_process: 1. The applicant submits the application as per the application form prescribed in Appendix K through the online portal maintained by the Project Management Agency (PMA). 2. The PMA conducts an examination as per the checklist in Appendix I within 15 working days of receipt of the application or any subsequent submission of a revised application if the original was returned as incomplete. 3. If the application is found to be incomplete, the PMA informs the applicant accordingly, and the applicant must complete the incomplete application within 15 days of such communication, failing which the application will be closed. 4. A non-refundable application fee, as mentioned in Appendix C, is payable electronically for each application. 5. All applications are submitted through the online portal maintained by the PMA; if the portal is not available, applications may be submitted in physical form to the PMA. 6. The applicant submits an undertaking in the format given in Appendix E and also an undertaking in the format given in Appendix G, consenting to audit of their manufacturing site/offices for verification of information/data submitted along with the application. 7. As part of the application, the applicant submits a plan based on sequential milestones to be achieved, including associated expenditure and timeframes ('project plan'), with each milestone consisting of objective, measurable technical and non-technical markers ('milestone descriptors') selected from a catalogue accessible on the Scheme portal. 8. The Central Government considers the project plan without modification or with such modifications as to timeframes, project cost, milestone descriptor, or milestone as it may deem fit and accords its in-principle approval to such submitted or modified project plan ('in-principle approved project plan'), or includes it as a waitlisted project, or rejects the same. 9. If in-principle approval is accorded with modifications, the communication conveying the same may specify such modifications or require the applicant to carry out changes in the project plan in line with directions contained in such communication and resubmit the plan. 10. Upon conveying acceptance and submission (to the satisfaction of the Central Government), the project plan or the modified project plan is referred to as the approved project plan. 11. Within a period of 60 days from the date of conveying acceptance and submissions, the applicant submits: (i) documents evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost, (ii) duly signed funding agreement with the approved project plan annexed thereto, (iii) if the applicant has opted for benefit-share by way of share allotment, duly signed share allotment agreement, and (iv) if the Central Government has required a report to validate that the product or technology which the project proposed to take to higher TRLs is at TRL claimed in the application, such report furnished by an agency from among those specified on the Scheme portal for this purpose. 12. Failure to convey acceptance or make any submission as aforesaid within the specified period (or such extended period or periods as the Central Government may permit) renders the in-principle approval liable to be withdrawn. 13. A waitlisted project is retained in the waitlist for a period of 180 days from the date on which its waitlisting is communicated to the applicant, along with the approved project plan, for acceptance of the waitlisting within a period of 10 days of such communication. 14. Every beneficiary and waitlisted applicant is required to implement the approved project plan as per the milestone descriptors and associated expenditure and timeframe specified therein, in accordance with the provisions of the Scheme and these guidelines, unless the Central Government permits modification of the same in writing. 15. In case the Central Government permits any modification, the reference to 'approved project plan' is construed as a reference to the modified project plan. 16. In case of the share allotment option for benefit-share, an applicant company may either allot its own shares or the shares of a company incorporated by it as a dedicated, special purpose vehicle for purposes of implementation of the project and fulfilment of all obligations of the applicant/beneficiary under the Scheme and these guidelines. 17. Disbursement of milestone-linked financial assistance is done in the following manner: (a) Number of instalments: Disbursement of financial assistance is made in four instalments. The fourth instalment will be at least 15% of the approved total financial assistance and will be disbursed on a reimbursement basis. (b) Co-funding: Total co-funding will be required to the extent specified in the Scheme and would equal the approved total project cost less the approved total financial assistance, and the proportion of total co-funding to the approved total financial assistance is required to be maintained at the stage of disbursement of each instalment. Accordingly, the beneficiary is required to ensure that cumulative co-funding in said proportion to the sum of such instalment and financial assistance previously disbursed is credited into the project account before such disbursement. (c) Criteria for disbursement: On achievement of the milestones as per corresponding criteria, instalments as specified below will be disbursed, which may be utilised for the corresponding purposes: [Milestone, Criteria for achievement of milestone, Milestone-linked instalment to be disbursed, Purposes for which instalments disbursed may be utilised] Milestone 0 (M0): 1. For all applicants: 1.1 Evidencing financial closure for the approved total project cost, 1.2 Execution of funding agreement. 2. Further, for applicants selecting share allotment option of benefit-share: 2.1 Execution of share allotment agreement. 3. Also, for all projects for [details truncated due to length, but process is fully described in evidence].
deadlines: The Scheme shall be open for applications during the Application Window. No application shall be accepted after the end of the Application Window.
implementing_agency: Department of Pharmaceuticals, Ministry of Chemicals and Fertilizers, Government of India

overview: The Production Linked Incentive Scheme for Food Processing Industry (PLISFPI) is a central sector scheme under the Ministry of Food Processing Industries aimed at boosting domestic manufacturing and exports in the food processing sector. It provides financial incentives based on incremental sales and investments made by eligible entities over a five-year period. The scheme focuses on promoting value addition, brand development, and innovation in food processing, particularly for SMEs and innovative/organic products.
target_beneficiaries: SME; innovative/organic products; food processing units
eligibility: Eligible entities include food processing units engaged in manufacturing, with a focus on SMEs, innovative/organic products, and those undertaking branding and marketing activities. Specific eligibility criteria are defined under the scheme guidelines for each incentive category.
benefits: Financial incentives based on incremental sales and investment; support for branding and marketing; incentives for innovative and organic products; assistance in scaling up operations and market access.
financial_support: Incentives are provided as a percentage of incremental sales over base year sales, and as a percentage of investment in plant, machinery, and related infrastructure, disbursed annually over five years based on verified claims.
application_process: 1. Visit the PLISFPI portal via the Ministry of Food Processing Industries website. 2. Register as an applicant on the portal. 3. Submit application with required documents including business registration, product details, investment proof, and sales data. 4. Application is reviewed by the Ministry or its appointed Programme Management Agency. 5. Upon approval, enter into an agreement for incentive disbursement. 6. Submit periodic claims for incentives based on verified incremental sales and investment. 7. Incentives are released after validation.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year as per scheme guidelines.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Food Processing Industries

overview: Pradhan Mantri Gramin Awas Yojana (PMAY-G) is a flagship rural housing scheme launched by the Government of India to provide pucca houses with basic amenities to all houseless and households living in kutcha and dilapidated houses in rural areas by 2024. The scheme aims to achieve 'Housing for All' in rural areas by providing financial assistance for the construction of durable houses. It focuses on improving the quality of life and ensuring dignity through pucca housing for the rural poor.
target_beneficiaries: houseless households; households living in kutcha and dilapidated houses; rural poor
eligibility: Beneficiaries are identified based on housing deprivation parameters and socio-economic criteria as per Socio Economic and Caste Census (SECC) 2011. The scheme covers houseless households and households living in zero, one, or two-room kutcha houses (walls and roof made of grass, thatch, bamboo, wood, mud, unburnt brick, etc.). Priority is given to SCs/STs, freed bonded labourers, non-tribal forest dwellers, manual scavengers, legally released bonded labourers, and persons with disabilities.
benefits: Financial assistance for construction of a pucca house; provision of basic amenities such as electricity connection, LPG connection, toilet, and drinking water through convergence with other government schemes (Swachh Bharat Mission-Gramin, Saubhagya, Ujjwala, Jal Jeevan Mission); technical support for construction; and facilitation of institutional finance through banks.
financial_support: Unit assistance of INR 1.20 lakh in plain areas and INR 1.30 lakh in hilly states, difficult areas, and IAP (Integrated Action Plan) districts. The financial assistance is provided in three instalments via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the beneficiary's bank/Post Office account linked with Aadhaar, based on construction progress verified through geo-tagged photographs.
grant_amount_per_entity: INR 1.20 lakh in plain areas and INR 1.30 lakh in hilly states, difficult areas, and IAP districts
application_process: 1. Beneficiary identification is done through SECC 2011 data and Gram Sabha verification. 2. Eligible beneficiaries are intimated and their consent is obtained. 3. The beneficiary selects a house design from the rural housing technology menu. 4. The beneficiary opens a bank/Post Office account linked with Aadhaar for DBT. 5. Construction begins after the first instalment is released. 6. Second and third instalments are released upon verification of construction stages via geo-tagged photographs. 7. Upon completion, the house is verified and the beneficiary is facilitated for convergence benefits.
deadlines: The scheme was originally targeted for completion by March 2022, later extended to 2024. Beneficiary identification and sanctioning are done periodically based on fund availability and SECC data.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India

scheme_name: National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction
overview: The National Action Plan for Drug Demand Reduction (NAPDDR) is a centrally sponsored scheme under the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment aimed at reducing drug demand through preventive education, awareness generation, counseling, treatment, rehabilitation, and skill development for affected individuals. It supports NGOs and other eligible organizations in setting up and running District De-Addiction Centres (DDACs), Garima Greh (shelters for transgender persons), and other related interventions under its sub-schemes like SMILE and AVYAY. The scheme operates through the e-Anudaan portal for online submission of proposals, document verification, and grant-in-aid disbursement. It targets vulnerable populations including persons affected by substance abuse, transgender individuals, and senior citizens in need of care and protection.
target_beneficiaries: persons affected by substance abuse; transgender individuals; senior citizens; NGOs; charitable institutions
eligibility: Eligible organisations include NGOs, Trusts, and Charitable institutions registered for at least two years with a minimum of two years' experience in the social sector at the time of applying. They must register on the NGO-DARPAN portal of NITI Aayog to obtain a NGO Unique ID before applying on the e-Anudaan portal. For specific sub-schemes like IPSrC under AVYAY, applicants must propose projects in designated Gap districts as per the tentative list annexed in the guidelines. Applications must be submitted exclusively through the e-Anudaan portal (https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login) with complete documentation.
benefits: Financial support in the form of Grant-in-Aid (GIA) for setting up and running projects such as DDACs, Garima Greh, Senior Citizen Homes, and Continuous Care Homes. Non-financial benefits include technical support, guidance on scheme guidelines, access to training under PM-DAKSH for beneficiaries, geo-tagging of facilities for better service accessibility, and facilitation of monitoring and inspection processes via the e-Anudaan portal. The portal automates application verification, checklist filling, inspection report scrutiny, screening, recommendation, financial approval, and GIA sanction.
financial_support: Financial assistance is provided as Grant-in-Aid (GIA) to eligible NGOs for recurring and non-recurring expenses related to the establishment and operation of projects under NAPDDR, including DDACs, Garima Greh, Senior Citizen Homes, and Continuous Care Homes. The quantum of assistance, cost norms, and conditions are detailed in the respective scheme guidelines (e.g., AVYAY scheme guideline) uploaded on the Ministry's website and the e-Anudaan portal. Funds are disbursed in installments (e.g., 1st, 2nd, 3rd/final) based on project progress and submission of required documents and inspection reports.
application_process: 1. Register on the NGO-DARPAN portal (https://ngodarpan.gov.in) to obtain a NGO Unique ID. 2. Access the e-Anudaan portal at https://grants-msje.gov.in/ngo-login. 3. Log in using credentials and ensure NGO Unique ID and PAN are submitted during registration. 4. Navigate to the relevant scheme (e.g., NAPDDR, DDAC, Garima Greh, AVYAY/IPSrC) under 'Notice Inviting Applications'. 5. Fill in the application form with project details. 6. Upload prescribed documents including NGO Unique ID, PAN, project proposal, and other scheme-specific documents. 7. Submit the application before the deadline. 8. Await verification, inspection, and approval by district, state, and central authorities. 9. Upon approval, Grant-in-Aid will be sanctioned and disbursed in installments.
deadlines: Varies by scheme and notice; e.g., applications for new projects under IPSrC/AVYAY in Gap districts were accepted from 01st July 2024 till 15th August 2024 (as per one notice), while RRTC applications were accepted from 01st July 2024 till 31st July 2024. Deadlines are specified in individual notices; applicants must check the latest notice on the e-Anudaan portal.
implementing_agency: Department of Social Justice and Empowerment, Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, Government of India
contact_details: Email: bharati.gusain@nic.in (for IPSrC/AVYAY queries), support[dot]ngo-msje[at]nic[dot]in (technical support), feedbackddrs[at]gmail[dot]com (DDRS scheme feedback)

overview: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB PM-JAY) is a flagship public health assurance scheme of the Government of India, implemented by the National Health Authority (NHA), providing health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to approximately 12 crore beneficiary families. The scheme aims to reduce out-of-pocket expenditure for medical treatments and achieve Universal Health Coverage by offering cashless treatment at empanelled hospitals across the country. As of the latest data, over 23.39 crore beneficiaries have been verified and issued Ayushman Cards, with ₹61,501 crore worth of free treatment provided under the scheme till date.
target_beneficiaries: poor and vulnerable families; beneficiary families
eligibility: Beneficiaries are identified based on deprivation and occupational criteria from the Socio Economic Caste Census (SECC) database. The scheme covers poor and vulnerable families as per SECC data, including those in rural areas living in households with only one room, no adult member aged 16-59, female-headed households with no adult male member, households with disabled members and no able-bodied adult, SC/ST households, and landless households deriving major income from manual casual labour. In urban areas, beneficiaries include those working in specified occupational categories such as ragpickers, beggars, domestic workers, street vendors, construction workers, etc. The scheme is implemented in 36 States/UTs, with West Bengal being the latest to join.
benefits: Cashless treatment for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization up to Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year at any empanelled hospital across India. Coverage includes 1,949 procedures under 27 specialties, including medical oncology, emergency care, orthopaedics, and urology. Over 141 medical procedures are exclusively earmarked for women. Beneficiaries receive co-branded PVC Ayushman Cards. The scheme also provides gender-equitable access, with approximately 49% of Ayushman Card recipients being women and over 48% of authorized hospital admissions availed by women. Additional benefits include portability of benefits across states, coverage for pre-existing diseases from day one, and no cap on family size or age.
financial_support: The scheme provides a health cover of Rs. 5 lakhs per family per year on a family floater basis, meaning the total amount can be used by one or all members of the family. There is no cap on the number of family members or age. The financial support is entirely funded by the Government of India, with funds allocated to States/UTs for implementation. As of the latest data, ₹61,501 crore worth of free treatment has been authorized under the scheme, and over 5 crore hospital admissions have been authorized. The National Health Authority (NHA) oversees the financial management, including disbursement of funds to States/UTs and monitoring of utilization.
grant_amount_per_entity: ₹5 lakhs per family per year
application_process: 1. Check eligibility via the official NHA website or Ayushman App using mobile number or ration card details. 2. Visit a nearby Common Service Centre (CSC) or empanelled hospital with required documents. 3. Submit documents for verification. 4. Upon verification, the Ayushman Card is generated and issued. 5. Use the Ayushman Card for cashless treatment at any empanelled hospital by presenting it along with ID proof. 6. Hospitals submit claims to NHA through the transaction management system for reimbursement.
deadlines: Rolling basis — applications are accepted throughout the year.
implementing_agency: National Health Authority (NHA)
contact_details: Toll-Free Call Centre No: 14555 (NHA/PM-JAY) | Email: webmaster-pmjay@nha.gov.in

overview: Gyan Bharatam is a national mission led by the Ministry of Culture to digitally preserve, catalog, and share India’s rich manuscript heritage with the world. It aims to safeguard and revitalize India’s Manuscript Heritage through systematic survey and documentation, scientific conservation, digitization, and publication, along with the creation of a national digital repository (NDR). The initiative was envisioned by the Honorable Prime Minister, Shri Narendra Modi, and the Hon’ble Finance Minister announced it in the Union Budget 2025–26. The Standing Finance Committee (SFC) has sanctioned Rs 491.66 crore for the period 2025–2031.
target_beneficiaries: researchers; scholars; students; institutions; citizen contributors
eligibility: Open to individuals and institutions holding manuscripts, including citizen contributors, scholars, libraries, monasteries, temples, and academic institutions across India who wish to participate in the preservation, digitization, and sharing of manuscript heritage.
benefits: Access to digitized manuscripts for research, education, and public engagement; opportunity to register as a Gyan Bharatam Scholar; participation in workshops, conferences, and webinars; collaboration with researchers across India; access to manuscript repositories; contribution to national digital heritage infrastructure; recognition as part of a living network of seekers, scholars, teachers, and knowledge-keepers.
financial_support: The Standing Finance Committee (SFC) has sanctioned Rs 491.66 crore for the period 2025–2031 to support the operations of Gyan Bharatam, including survey, conservation, digitization, and creation of the National Digital Repository.
application_process: 1. Visit the Gyan Bharatam website at https://gyanbharatam.com/ 2. Navigate to the 'Register as Scholar' section via the homepage or community page 3. Fill out the scholar registration form with personal and institutional details 4. Submit the form to join the Gyan Bharatam Parivar network 5. Upon approval, gain access to manuscript repositories and collaborative opportunities.
deadlines: Rolling basis — no fixed deadline. Applications are accepted year-round.
implementing_agency: Ministry of Culture, Government of India
contact_details: Email: gyanbharatam2025@gmail.com | Phone: +91-11-23315625 | Shri Samar Nanda, Joint Secretary: +91 11 23386504 | Shri Inderjeet Singh, Director: +91 11 24014229 | Prof. Anirban Dash, Project Director: +91 11 23315625

overview: Mission Mausam is a flagship initiative of the India Meteorological Department (IMD) under the Ministry of Earth Sciences, aimed at enhancing weather forecasting, climate services, and disaster preparedness through advanced observational networks, numerical weather prediction models, and specialized services for agriculture, aviation, hydrology, and public safety. The scheme integrates satellite, radar, and surface-based observations to provide real-time weather data, forecasts, and warnings across India. It supports sectors such as power, water resources, and health by delivering sector-specific meteorological advisories and early warning systems.
target_beneficiaries: farmers; aviation sector; power sector; water resource managers; disaster management agencies; general public
eligibility: The scheme is implemented by the India Meteorological Department under the Ministry of Earth Sciences and serves government agencies, public institutions, and end-users across sectors such as agriculture, aviation, power, water resources, and disaster management. Direct beneficiaries include farmers receiving agromet advisories, aviation authorities using meteorological watch offices, power grid operators utilizing weather portals for grid management, and state and central agencies accessing hydrometeorological and environmental data. There is no formal application process for end-users as services are disseminated publicly through IMD’s portals, SMS advisories, and bulletins.
benefits: Mission Mausam provides real-time weather forecasts, nowcasts, and extended range forecasts; sector-specific advisories such as Gramin Krishi Mausam Seva (GKMS) for farmers, aviation meteorological services for flight safety, hydrometeorological support for flood forecasting, and environmental monitoring including air quality and ozone services. It delivers early warnings for cyclones, thunderstorms, heatwaves, and flash floods through SMS, web portals, and mobile applications. The scheme enables secure and economic operation of the Indian power grid via weather-informed scheduling, supports agricultural planning with crop-specific advisories, and enhances disaster preparedness through impact-based forecasting. Benefits also include access to climate data, research collaboration, and training programs for capacity building.
financial_support: Mission Mausam is a centrally funded government scheme under the Ministry of Earth Sciences. Financial support is provided through budgetary allocations for infrastructure development, including procurement of Doppler Weather Radars (DWRs), automatic weather stations (AWS), high-performance computing systems for numerical weather prediction, and satellite data reception systems. Funds are utilized for upgrading observational networks, developing forecast models, operationalizing early warning systems, and maintaining service delivery mechanisms. The scheme does not involve direct financial transfers to individuals or entities but invests in national infrastructure and service capabilities.
application_process: Mission Mausam does not have a direct application process for end-users as it is a public service initiative. Services are automatically available to beneficiaries through IMD’s official channels. Farmers receive agromet advisories via SMS on registered mobile numbers through the mKisan portal. Aviation users access meteorological data through Aerodrome Meteorological Offices and Meteorological Watch Offices. Power sector users utilize the Weather Power Portal developed in collaboration with POSOCO. General public and agencies can access forecasts, warnings, and data through the IMD website (mausam.imd.gov.in), mobile apps like UMANG, and district-wise warning GIS portals. No formal application or registration is required for accessing general weather services.
deadlines: Rolling basis — services are available year-round and updated in real time
implementing_agency: India Meteorological Department (IMD), Ministry of Earth Sciences, Government of India
